default: (a) two {@item spear|phb|spears} or (b) any {@filter martial weapon|items|source=phb|category=basic|type=martial weapon}
(a) a {@item greataxe|PHB} or (b) a {@item battleaxe|PHB} and a {@item shield|PHB}
(a) an {@item explorer's pack|phb} or (b) a {@item priest's pack|PHB}

fluff.name: Atavist
entries: An armored frontlining human takes blow after blow, and when she begins to weaken, she becomes all the more savage—hitting harder, striking faster, dying slower. A wounded dog is lethal, but a wounded atavist is a bloody nightmare. With caution halfway around the world, she wounds her own arm, but the enemy falls to the ground as he takes all the pain. Another foe comes near, but finding her only stronger than she was previous.

An orc throws more than his all into each blow, hurting himself in a magical effort to strengthen his blows, but knows it's not enough. He stills and lets the alien forces take over his mind, as his body erupts aberrant flesh strips, covering him entirely, growing out of holes and hairs—eyes darkening and true horror creeping. If his mark doesn't falter at the mere sight of the abomination, he will soon fall in battle.

The fireball rounds the corners of the maze, the wizard's foul fire chasing his would be killer down the hall, but a sacrificial slight crippling of his arm gives him the power he needs to dodge out of the fiery current.

No matter their upbringing, race, culture, or even time period, atavists are united by their ability to make use of their own vitality in their weaponplay. There may have been bloodlines at one point, but those are gone now.

entries.name: Old, Vengeful, and Scattered
entries: Before iron, before the blade, before organized cities, there were the atavists—using the only tools they had available: their bodies and their magic. In tandem, they use these dual aspects to protect, to conquer, to hunt monsters, or to achieve any other ends.

These people build on failure. They take their combat training of reflexive attacks when they're hurt and apply it to their lives: those that wrong atavists should know they will be struck back ten times harder. Some atavists even enjoy being attacked, hurt or betrayed, and use it as an excuse to hit back.

Atavists can come from anywhere in the world: tribes, kingdoms, royalty, or gutter poor; however, those peoples that trend towards using hemomancy and other necrotic, vitality-affected powers would seem to have a higher chance of a child with atavistic powers.

entries.name: The Origin of Hemomancy
entries: Magic that drains and takes from you is a studied and developed field that comes from atavists first. They started blood magic. But, what started the atavists? How did atavists begin in your setting—or is a piece of history lost long ago? Is it even important? Has it began multiple times, or can be it be restarted or instilled into potential users a variety of ways? Or, is every atavist in the same family tree, leading farther and farther back; it might lead to creatures beyond humanoids, perhaps from some union of opposite beings; or from a creature who has long left this universe.

The power to use one's own body in a general sense has been around for eons. Atavism is tied and twisted around that idea. To those born with the talent, it's no more difficult than training an instrument for others - the inclination was born into them. However, many atavists were not simply born with it - through the prowess of a strong blood warrior, a child can be formed and shaped physically, giving him or her a much higher chance of developing atavistic powers. It's up to you to decide how you obtained this talent.

entries.name: Powers Without Direction
entries: Many classes in Dungeons and Dragons have a clear indication and will for what a member would be doing—wizards study magic, paladins take up the cause of righteousness, and clerics serve their gods. But, many atavists have no such direction. If your character was ingrained with these powers through a blood rite, the question is, why?
The same question exists for those who were born with the abilities. Are you part of some tangential, many-webbed plan of a deity or another powerful being, or was it just chance?

If you don't know the answers—like many of those in the world do not know the purpose of their lives—you decide it. Maybe you don't care, and you focus on what you want and how you can get it in the future, instead of dwelling on the past.

entries.name: Infamous Heroes, Oft Villains
entries: The magic used (or abused) by atavists is normally questionable at best in most cultures, and when under close examination, can often be found to be a type that is commonly disowned, unsupported, and generally looked down on. This creates prejudice against atavists. They are more often cast as villains in stories, and tend not to be heroes; at least, in most of the world. It's harder for an atavist to be admired and easier to villainize.

If you have hidden your powers, or put them to an unusual cause, what effect has that had on how confident your character is? Would 'I was born like this'as a defense have held up wherever you came from?

entries.name: Creating an Atavist
entries: The most important question to consider when creating your atavist is the effect that their powers have had on them—and how those effects and powers were treated by those around you. Did you lose sets of friends due to fearful ignorance? Did your family embrace and accept you for who you are, or did they turn a blind eye to your powers and hope they—or maybe you—would disappear?

Atavists are known for their rigid health, physicality, and aptitude for circumstantially evil magics. Certain sages and other scholars would love to study you, or those with the same motives and less morals may pose a danger to you; maybe they already have. Do you wish you could hide or remove your power? Or do you want to defeat the stereotype you're applied and become a hero?

After your journey begins as an atavist, it might happen upon a new road. Are you a brutal warrior who wants to be attacked - a jaded, wearied soldier who will never fall? Are you a hopeful and strong warrior that has fallen to afflictions of the mind; the inluence of strange beings, that are a cursed blessing? Or are you a ruthless killer, lethal and weaponized, who will not stop to get what they want? Maybe you pledge yourself to or are pledged by a powerful fiend, who is respectful of your blood magic, and extends a fiery hand of power for you to grasp. You might even divine more power from meditation and self-reflection, with the idea that the more time you spend with yourself, the more able you are.

entries.name: Quick Builds
entries: You can make an atavist quickly by following these suggestions. First, Constitution should be your highest ability score, followed by Strength or Dexterity (depending on what kind of martial character you want). Second, choose the {@background Soldier} background.

classFeature.name: Rend Ichor
entries: You can tap into your raw health to deliver death. You have a rend die which powers certain class features, and is a {@dice d6}. When you rend, you roll this die and lose hitpoints equal to the result, which can't be reduced in any way. You can't rend more than once for a feature unless the effect says so.

When a feature says that you can rend to a roll, it means that you can rend yourself and add the result of the rend die to the roll. You can rend yourself to any damage roll for an attack with a melee weapon as a part of the {@spell Attack} action, for a spell attack, for a reaction, or for a bonus action. You can rend any amount of times per turn.
At 7th level, you can rend to Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws, and you can roll {@dice 2d6} for your rend dice. At 14th level, you can roll {@dice 3d6}, and {@dice 4d6} at 20th.

entries.name: Atavist Variants
entries: There are a couple variants that stem from the standard version: the painless variant and the painful variant, which make it easier or harder to play. With your DM's permission (or ruling), use one of the variants below.

entries.name: Painless
entries: You have a {@dice d8} for your hit dice (8 + CON mod hitpoints at 1st), instead of a {@dice d6}. Your rend dice begin as a {@dice d4} and progress to {@dice 2d4}, {@dice 3d4}, and {@dice 4d4}.

entries.name: Painful
entries: You have a {@dice d4} for your hit dice (4 + CON mod hitpoints at 1st), instead of a {@dice d6}. Your rend dice begin as a {@dice d4} and progress to {@dice 2d8}, {@dice 3d8}, and {@dice 4d8}.

classFeature.name: Unstopping Heart
entries: Once per turn, when rending yourself would reduce you to 0 hitpoints, you are instead reduced to 1. Additionally, you are proficient in death saving throws, and if the result of a death saving throw you make is 18 or higher, you regain 1 hitpoint.

classFeature.name: Spellcasting
entries: By 2nd level, your physical essence—either through your family, the blessing of some divine (or unholy) being, or even through sheer chance has produced a fortified and living ichor within you, wieldable for magical prowess. See {@book chapter 10|PHB|10} for the general rules of spellcasting and the end of this document for the {@filter atavist spell list|spells|class=Atavist (Atavist)}.

entries.name: Preparing and Casting Spells
entries: The Atavist table shows how many spell slots you have to cast your spells. To cast one of your atavist spells of 1st level or higher, you must expend a slot of the spell's level or higher. You regain all expended spell slots when you finish a long rest.

You prepare the list of atavist spells that are available for you to cast, choosing from the atavist spell list. When you do so, choose a number of atavist spells equal to your Constitution modifier + half your atavist level, rounded down (minimum of one spell). The spells must be of a level for which you have spell slots.

For example, if you are a 5th level atavist, you have four 1st-level and two 2nd-level spell slots. With a Constitution of 14, your list of prepared spells can include four spells of 1st or 2nd level, in any combination. If you prepare the 1st-level spell {@spell inflict wounds}, you can cast it using a 1st-level or a 2nd-level slot. Casting the spell doesn't remove it from your list of prepared spells.

You can change your list of prepared spells when you finish a long rest. Preparing a new list of atavist spells requires time spent attuning to your vitality: at least 1 minute per spell level for each spell on your list.

entries.name: Spellcasting Ability
entries: Constitution is your spellcasting ability for your atavist spells, since the power of your magic relies on your own blood. You use your Constitution whenever a spell refers to your spellcasting ability. In addition, you use your Constitution modifier when setting the saving throw DC for an atavist spell you cast and when making an attack roll with one.

entries.name: Ritual Casting
entries: You can cast an atavist spell as a ritual if that spell has the ritual tag and you have the spell prepared.

entries.name: Self Focus
entries: When you cast spells that require a material component, you ignore that component unless it has a value, such as the diamond dust worth 100 gp for the {@spell greater restoration} spell, in which case the components are required.

classFeature.name: Hardened Viscera
entries: At 2nd level, your body takes on physical traits and aspects of the toughest people and most resolute populations. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you aren't wearing heavy armor, you can reduce any damage you take by 2.
Your hit point maximum can't be reduced.

classFeature.name: Atavistic Aspect
entries: Choose an aspect at 3rd level, which describes and emulates your bodily magical power. They are detailed at the end of the class description. Your choice grants you features when you choose it at 3rd level and again at 6th, 10th, and 18th levels.

entries.name: Aspect Spells
entries: Each aspect has a list of associated spells. You gain access to these spells when you can cast a spell of its level, as is on the table. Once you gain access to an aspect spell, you always have it prepared. Aspect spells don't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day.
If you gain an aspect spell that doesn't appear on the atavist spell list, the spell is nonetheless an atavist spell for you.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 4th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Avenging Attack
entries: Starting at 5th level, when an attack hits you, you fail a saving throw, or you fail an ability check that requires an attack or an action, you can make a weapon attack against any creature within range as a reaction.
At 11th level, you can make two weapon attacks as part of this reaction.

classFeature.name: Atavistic Aspect Feature
entries: At 6th level, you gain a feature granted by your Atavistic Aspect.

classFeature.name: Humansbane (HP 10)
entries: Starting at 6th level, you exude physical might, and can take apart lesser humanoids with single blows. Whenever you hit a humanoid with an attack and rend to its damage, the creature is instantly slain if the damage reduces their hitpoints to 10 or lower.
At 10th level, the humanoid is instantly slain if it has 15 or fewer hitpoints remaining. At 14th level, it increases to 20, and 25 at 18th level.

classFeature.name: Greater Viscera
entries: At 7th level, your body has taken on bedrock and efficient properties. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you aren't wearing heavy armor, you can reduce any damage you take by 4, and 6 at 15th level.
When you take damage from a creature other than you, your next rend doesn't lose you hitpoints within the next minute.

classFeature.name: Rend Dice (2d6)
entries: At 7th level, your Rend Dice becomes {@dice 2d6}.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 8th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Atavistic Aspect Feature
entries: At 10th level, you gain a feature granted by your Atavistic Aspect.

classFeature.name: Humansbane (HP 15)
entries: At 10th level, your Humansbane feature imporves: Whenever you hit a humanoid with an attack and rend to its damage, the creature is instantly slain if the damage reduces their hitpoints to 15 or lower, instead of 10.

classFeature.name: Avenging Attack Improvement
entries: At 11th level, when you use your Avening Attack feature, you can make two weapon attacks as part of your reaction, instead of one.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 12th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Sanguine Sacrament (5th Level)
entries: Starting at 14th level, you begin to unravel hallowed secrets of the interwoven magic and body called a sacrament. Choose one {@filter 5th-level spell from the atavist spell list|spells|class=Atavist (Atavist)|level=5} as this sacrament.
You can cast this sacrament spell or an aspect spell of the same spell level once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

At higher levels, you gain more atavist spells of your choice that can be cast in this way: one 6th-level spell at 17th level and one 7th-level spell at 20th level. You regain all uses of your Sanguine Sacraments when you finish a long rest.

classFeature.name: Humansbane (HP 20)
entries: At 14th level, your Humansbane feature imporves: Whenever you hit a humanoid with an attack and rend to its damage, the creature is instantly slain if the damage reduces their hitpoints to 20 or lower, instead of 15.

classFeature.name: Rend Dice (3d6)
entries: At 14th level, your Rend Dice becomes {@dice 3d6}.

classFeature.name: Greater Viscera Improvement
entries: At 15th level, while you aren't wearing heavy armor, you can reduce any damage you take by 6, instead of 4.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 16th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Sanguine Sacrament (6th Level)
entries: Starting at 17th level, you begin to unravel hallowed secrets of the interwoven magic and body called a sacrament. Choose one {@filter 6th-level spell from the atavist spell list|spells|class=Atavist (Atavist)|level=6} as this sacrament.
You can cast this sacrament spell or an aspect spell of the same spell level once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

classFeature.name: Atavistic Aspect Feature
entries: At 18th level, you gain a feature granted by your Atavistic Aspect.

classFeature.name: Humansbane (HP 25)
entries: At 18th level, your Humansbane feature imporves: Whenever you hit a humanoid with an attack and rend to its damage, the creature is instantly slain if the damage reduces their hitpoints to 25 or lower, instead of 20.

classFeature.name: Ability Score Improvement
entries: When you reach 19th level, you can increase one ability score of your choice by 2, or you can increase two ability scores of your choice by 1. As normal, you can't increase an ability score above 20 using this feature.
If your DM allows the use of feats, you may instead take a {@5etools feat|feats.html}.

classFeature.name: Nigh Immortality
entries: At 20th level, you are all but impossible to kill. Your body doesn't age, and if you die and at least one part of your body exists—such as a fingernail or an eye—your body can regrow from that part over the course of 24 hours. At the end of that time, you return to life with 1 hitpoint. If you were killed due to the {@spell disintegrate} spell or a similar effect, your body has no healthy parts remaining to restore from. If your body has been burned completely through or otherwise ruined with powerful spells and abilities that affect an area (such as a dragon's breath weapon or {@spell cone of cold}), you can still regrow from internal body parts as long as the damage taken doesn't exceed half your hitpoint maximum. Finally, if you are dead or not yet fully regrown, any magic attempting to raise your body as an undead succeeds, but you instantly gain control.

classFeature.name: Sanguine Sacrament (7th Level)
entries: Starting at 20th level, you begin to unravel hallowed secrets of the interwoven magic and body called a sacrament. Choose one {@filter 7th-level spell from the atavist spell list|spells|class=Atavist (Atavist)|level=7} as this sacrament.
You can cast this sacrament spell or an aspect spell of the same spell level once without expending a spell slot. You must finish a long rest before you can do so again.

classFeature.name: Rend Dice (4d6)
entries: At 20th level, your Rend Dice becomes {@dice 4d6}.

feat.name: Embrace Death
entries: There are some amongst the annukara that channel their inherited powers and shape themselves to better fit their progenitor. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain resistance to necrotic damage.

You can cast the {@spell hex} spell using this feat, except it targets both Strength and Dexterity checks, instead of one ability score of your choice. You regain the ability to cast this spell when you finish a long rest, or if you die and are brought back to life. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this spell.

feat.name: Tenebrous Protector
entries: Sometimes the fiercest annukara manifest their heritage and power in a protective aura devoted to being a guardian to their allies, and a menace to all that would harm them. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you lose hit points from Shared Burden, you can gain temporary hit points equal to the hit points you lost. These hit points last for 1 minute.

When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while you have temporary hit points from this feat, you can lose a number of those hit points up to your proficiency bonus to deal extra necrotic damage equal to the number lost.

As an action, you can manifest a fierce aura of steadfast, dedicated protection for 1 minute. For the duration, you can add any temporary hit points you gain from this feat together, and when you use Shared Burden on a creature, you can use it on that creature without a reaction until the start of your next turn, but only once per instance of damage. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Fiendish Constitution
entries: Fiendish blood runs strong in you, unlocking a resilience akin to that possessed by some fiends. You gain the following benefits:

items: You have resistance to poison damage, and one damage type you didn't choose from your Fiendish Resistance trait.
You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition poisoned}.

feat.name: Horrid Corruption
entries: The abyssal blood in your veins enables you to afflict your corruption in horrid ways. When you use your Corruption of the Abyss trait and the target fails the initial saving throw, you can also give it one of the following afflictions while corrupted:

items: {@bold Contagion.} If the creature fails the repeated saving throw to end the corruption within 5 feet of one or more creatures you can see, you can choose any number of the other creatures. If a target is missing any of its hit points, it must make a Charisma saving throw against the same instance of corruption, becoming corrupted on a failure. This effect can spread from those creatures too, but the duration doesn't extend, and if a corrupted creature succeeds the saving throw at the end of its turn, the effect only ends for itself.
{@bold Leech.} The creature can't regain hit points. If another creature tries to restore hit points to it, you gain those hit points instead.
{@bold Rot.} The creature has disadvantage on saving throws against spells you cast. Once you use this affliction, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Three Tailed
entries: Through great focus and meditation, you have learned to manifest additional tails, which grant you latent magic abilities. Choose up to three 1st-level spells from any classes. The spells must be enchantment or illusion spells, and must have a casting time of 1 action. You can cast each of the chosen spells once with this ability. You regain the ability to cast these spells when you inish a long rest.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

feat.name: Lost Love
entries: The devotion and love of a dajix is often matched only by the power of its mourning. You gain the following benefits:

items: When a creature you're {@condition charmed} by dies, you can unleash a wail of anguish. Each creature within 15 feet of you must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or take psychic damage equal to your level + your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

feat.name: Enhanced Magnetism
entries: Perhaps it was through meditation, or perhaps it was through practice, but you have learned the deeper traits of your natural magnetism. You gain the following benefits:

items: Creatures can't benefit from advantage on saving throws made to resist being {@condition charmed} by you, if they can see you.

While {@condition charmed} by your Beguiling Charm trait, instead of as a friendly acquaintance, the creature regards you as a trusted friend to be heeded and protected. Although it isn't under your control, it takes your requests and actions in the most favorable way it can.

feat.name: One With Shadow
entries: The shadows others see in your soul might not be as present as they believe, but that doesn't mean they shouldn't check their shadows for your gleaming fangs every so often. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your darkvision range increases by 60 feet.
You gain proficiency with the {@skill Stealth} skill. If you already have proficiency, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with it.

As a bonus action in dim light or darkness, you can disappear and reappear in an area of dim light or darkness within 120 feet of you, including the shadows of other creatures. After emerging, you have advantage on the first attack you make this turn. If you emerge within 5 feet of a creature, you can use your Lifedrinker trait on them as an action, even if they aren't willing, {@condition grappled}, {@condition incapacitated}, or {@condition restrained}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Vampiric Agility
entries: The agility of your ancestors was the thing of legends, the type of story told around campfires, sung in hymnals, regaled in myth. You have harnessed it. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your speed increases by 10 feet while you aren't in bright light.
You can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check.
When you fall and aren't incapacitated, you can subtract up to 100 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage, and you don't fall prone when taking falling damage.

feat.name: Dragon's Senses
entries: You have inherited the keen eyes of dragonkind. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill. If you already have proficiency, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make with it.

You can take the Search action at the end of another creature's turn (no action required by you), but only to make a Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) check. You can't do so again until the end of your next turn.

feat.name: Immutable
entries: Only you are in control of your form, and your mind's shield is impenetrable. You gain the following benefits:

items: You are immune to any spell or effect that would alter your form, unless you wish to be affected by it.
You always know when you are targeted by a spell or effect that would alter or erase your memories, and you can use your Blessing of the Ambiguous One trait in response to such an event.
When you use your Blessing of the Ambiguous One trait, the duration is extended to 8 hours, and you are immune to any effect that would alter or erase your memories for the same duration.

feat.name: Soul of Defiance
entries: From the days of the one who freed your kin, a residing promise and spirit of freedom has existed throughout many pseudodragons. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain one additional use of your Ancient Power trait.

While under the effects of your Nostalgic Form, you have advantage on saving throws against spells and magical effects, and you can instead expend a use of your Ancient Power trait to reroll saving throws against such effects.

If you succeed on a saving throw to resist being {@condition grappled}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition restrained}, or {@condition stunned}, you can choose a number of creatures that you can see within 30 feet of you up to your proficiency bonus that failed the same saving throw. The effect ends for them.

feat.name: Elder's Ward
entries: The ward of the elder yuan-ti and your predecessors manifests every so often. Through continuous study, you have learned to repurpose and utilize it yourself. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you see a creature within 120 feet of you target you or an area you are in with a spell, you know what level the spell is.
When you would fail a saving throw against a spell, you can reroll the saving throw. You must use the new roll. You can do so twice, and regain both uses when you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Inferno Heart
entries: The inferno burning in your heart flows wilder than the greatest of wildfires, and through concentration it has come under your control. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can communicate telepathically with any creature that has innate fire resistance or immunity, while it is within 60 feet of you. The creature can understand you and can respond telepathically.

When a hostile creature forces you to make a saving throw to avoid fire damage, you can use your reaction to weave the flames aside, and reduce the damage you take by half. When you do, you can cause a creature within 10 feet of you to take fire damage equal to the amount you reduced. You can't cause a creature to take fire damage this way if it's subject to the same effect.

As an action, you can reach into the fire in your heart, manipulating it from the core of your being into the world around you for 1 minute. As an action on each of your turns, including when you activate this effect, you can send forth flames in a blazing trail. The area of the trail begins in a space within 5 feet of you and consists of up to six 5-foot cubes, which you can arrange as you wish. Each cube must have at least one face adjacent to the face of another cube. Each creature in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC = your Reach to the Blaze DC). A creature takes {@dice 3d6} fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Planar Mastery
entries: You can utilize your connection with the cosmos in battle, and to ensure you are always aware of your planar surroundings. You gain the following benefits:

items: You always know what plane you're on.
You have advantage on saving throws against effects that would forcefully teleport you, or send you to a different plane of existence.

As a bonus action, you can conjure a portal that teleports you up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see, wreathing you in force energy. After teleporting, your next attack before the end of your next turn deals an additional {@dice 1d6} force damage. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Wingdust
entries: Some mothfolk have mastered the art of flight, and can use it to its fullest extent, flying where others would glide. Some leave dust in their wake, but you harness magic into it, sending it towards your foes. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can use your flying speed to ascend, and no longer descend if you end your turn flying in the air.

When you Ride the Wind, you can throw sparkling dust from your wings in a 20-foot-cube of your choice. Creatures within the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Wisdom modifier) or be placed under the effects of the {@spell faerie fire} spell for 1 minute (no concentration required by you). A creature can use an action to wipe the dust off of itself, ending the effect. You can't use this ability again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Take Flight
entries: You've learned to soar in the skies, as your ancestors did. You gain the following benefits:

items: You no longer fall if you end your turn flying with nothing holding you aloft.

Once per turn, if you are flying and dive at least 30 feet straight toward a target and then hit it with a melee weapon attack, the attack deals an extra {@dice 1d6} damage to the target. This damage increases by an additional 1d6 at 5th level ({@dice 2d6}), 11th level ({@dice 3d6}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d6}).

feat.name: Telepathic Voice
entries: When you sing your song, your targets will hear it even should they cover their ears. You gain the following benefits:

items: The range of your Harpy's Melody increases to 300 feet, and you can transmit it telepathically, meaning a creature can hear it even if deafened or otherwise unable to hear you. You can also speak telepathically to any creature you can see in the same range. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language.

When you're speak telepathically to a creature using this feat, you can use your action to give that creature the ability to speak telepathically with you for 1 hour or until you end this effect as an action. To use this ability, the creature must be a valid target of your Harpy's Melody. You can give this ability to only one creature at a time; giving it to a creature takes it away from another creature who has it.

The tune of your song can reach more creatures. You gain a number of charges equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). When you begin singing a melody that can target more than one creature, you can expend any number of charges to target that many additional creatures with the melody. You regain all expended charges when you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Blessed Rainsong
entries: You can sing a song of magical rainfall, infusing a creature with latent magic. You gain a new melody option:

entries.name: Melody: Raindrop Imbuement
entries: Choose one creature within range, which can be yourself. While the melody is active, the creature gains the benefits of one spell from the following list: {@spell aid}, {@spell alter self}, {@spell arrow catcher|TheDarkAscension}, {@spell bless}, {@spell calm emotions}, {@spell expeditious retreat}, {@spell longstrider}, {@spell mage armor}, {@spell mirror image}, {@spell protection from evil and good}, {@spell protection from poison}, {@spell see invisibility}, {@spell shield of faith}, {@spell water breathing}, {@spell water walk}. If you're in an area of heavy rain, you can choose one additional spell. You can't infuse a target with the benefits of more than one spell that normally requires concentration through this melody.

You can use this melody option a number of times equal to 1 + half your proficiency bonus, and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. You can also expend additional uses when you start this melody or continue singing to choose a number of additional spells to infuse the target with equal to the number of uses expended.

feat.name: Revealing Light
entries: Your holy light is potent, assisting you in revealing all. You gain the following benefits:

items: While an object affected by your {@spell light} cantrip is within 5 feet of a creature, object, or area affected by an illusion spell, the DC to discern the illusion is reduced by your Charisma modifier.

As an action, you can create a magical burst of radiance from your hand that blinds your foes. Hostile creatures within 5 feet of you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier) or be {@condition blinded} until the start of your next turn. Creatures that fail this saving throw can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} for the same duration.

feat.name: Hulking Hurler
entries: The giants are renowned for hurling things great distances, throwing barrages of rocks at their enemies, and you have learned their ways. You gain the following benefits:

items: You are proficient with improvised weapons, and you can pick up an object as part of an attack made using it.
When you throw an improvised weapon, you can use Strength instead of Dexterity for the ability modifier. and its damage die is a {@dice d6} instead of a {@dice d4}.

As a bonus action while wielding an improvised weapon with two hands, you can charge up a powerful blow. The next time you hit a creature with the weapon in the same turn, the creature must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked {@condition prone}.

feat.name: Hard-Headed
entries: You've taken a liking to hitting things with your head, and become stronger for it. You gain the following benefits:

items: The damage die of your ram increases to a {@dice d8}.

Immediately after hitting an attack with your ram as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use a bonus action to shove the target. If you move at least 20 feet before making the attack, you can push the creature an additional 10 feet on a successful shove.

While you aren't {@condition incapacitated} or {@condition prone}, if you would be pushed 10 or more feet away, you are instead pushed half as far. You can also use your reaction to reduce the distance you're pushed to 5 feet.

feat.name: Spring-Step
entries: Some satyr learn to take the phrase 'a spring in one's step' to the next level. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your speed increases by 10 feet while in bright light, or 15 feet if the bright light is natural sunlight.

When you fall and aren't incapacitated, you can subtract a number of feet equal to ten times your proficiency bonus from the fall when calculating falling damage. You subtract the same number when calculating falling damage sustained by creatures you carry.

Once per turn, you can jump in any direction while in mid-air. Roll a {@dice d4}, with a +2 bonus if you're in direct sunlight. This jump covers a number of feet equal to five times the number rolled, costs movement as normal, and can be affected by Mirthful Leaps. You can also add this distance to the amount you subtract when calculating falling damage if you jump while falling.

feat.name: The Joy of Battle
entries: Revelry isn't just found in song and dance; for some, it's found on the battlefield. You gain the following benefits:

items: Once per turn, you can draw or stow an instrument without using an object interaction.

If you are proficient with an instrument, you are also proficient in using it as an improvised weapon, and it uses a {@dice d8} for damage. If the instrument is magical, so is the damage dealt with it.

When you score a critical hit with a melee attack while holding or carrying an instrument you are proficient with, magical tones reverberate through the air to your allies. All allies within 30 feet of you gain advantage on their next attack roll.

When you hit a creature, you can inspire a bout of encouragement. The next attack you or an ally makes before the start of your next turn gains a 1d4 bonus to the roll. If the attack hits, the next attack before the start of the attacker's next turn also gains that bonus. This bonus perpetuates until a creature that gains the bonus misses an attack. You can use this ability once, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: The Flame of Contradiction
entries: The blood of the Archdevil of Contradictions runs cold through your hellfire veins. You gain the following benefits:

items: You learn the {@spell mage hand} cantrip. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it, chosen when you take this feat.

As an action, you can use your {@spell mage hand} to grip a target within 5 feet of it. Make a melee spell attack using the spellcasting ability modifier you chose for the cantrip. On a hit, the target is gripped by the hand. While the target is gripped, it takes {@spell 1d4} fire damage at the start of each of its turns. The target can use its action to make a Strength check to break the hand's grip (DC = 8 + the spellcasting ability modifier you use for the cantrip + your proficiency bonus), ending the effect on a success.
When you deal damage with your {@spell mage hand} cantrip or a spell from your Infernal Legacy trait, you deal additional damage equal to your proficiency bonus to one target of the spell.
You can change any fire damage you deal to cold damage.

feat.name: Introverted Traditions
entries: As a reclusive race, some teachings became commonplace, not least of which was how to stand apart in society or get what you needed most with the least trouble. You gain the following benefits:

items: By engaging in conversation with a creature for at least 1 minute, you can determine if its alignment is good, neutral, or evil. If the Intelligence of the creature is 5 or lower, you gain no insight about its alignment.

After you converse with a creature for a minute, you can make an Intelligence ({@skill History}) check contested by the creatures Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) check. If you win the contest, you can add your Intelligence modifier to any Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) check directed at the creature for 1 hour.

feat.name: Empty Heart
entries: The emptiness in your heart is something that you have devoted yourself to resolving, and while such a thing might not yet be accomplished, you have learned to manifest that emptiness as a part of yourself. As an action, you can cause yourself to become unidentifiable, gaining the appearance of a vaguely humanoid silhouette. This effect lasts until you dismiss it as a bonus action.
While you're unidentifiable, creatures hear your voice as their voice, you leave no tracks or other traces of your passage, and you have advantage on Dexterity ({@skill Stealth}) checks.

feat.name: Annukara Magic
entries: You learn the protective magic of your people, used to preserve the life and will of their allies and companions. You learn the {@spell aftercare|TheDarkAscension} cantrip. You also learn the {@spell prayer of healing} and {@spell rescue|TheDarkAscension} spells, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast these two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for these three spells.

feat.name: Ventiam Necessities
entries: You learn the magic commonly used by your people, often considered a necessity in some circles. You learn the {@spell identify} spell, and can cast it as a ritual without components. You also learn {@spell magic mouth} and {@spell fly}, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. You regain the ability to cast those two spells in this way when you finish a long rest. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for all three spells.

feat.name: Portal Prophet
entries: Your connections to the greater cosmos are enhanced at large. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you're meditating, as with Familiarity with the Cosmos or Breath of the Wild, you can't be surprised, and you know the location of any creature within 30 feet of you that isn't behind total cover.

As an action, you can cause portals to blink in and out of existence in a 10-foot radius sphere centered on a point you can see within 30 feet of you for 1 minute. Any non-magical ranged attack made against a creature in the area misses, you can expend 5 feet of your movement while in the area to teleport to any unoccupied space in the area, and you can use your bonus action to teleport a creature you can see in the area to any other unoccupied space on the ground in the area. An unwilling creature that succeeds on a Charisma saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) is unaffected. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Celestial Mantle
entries: Since before you were born, celestials have had their eyes upon you, in hopes that you would come to don a mantle of your own. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you cast the {@spell light} cantrip, instead of targeting an object, you can cause the light to emit from your eyes. Your eyes shine bright light in a 30-foot cone, and dim light for an additional 30 feet for the duration, instead of the normal range of the spell.
You learn the {@spell detect evil and good} spell. You can cast it once without expending a spell slot, and can't cast it again in this way until you finish a long rest.
You regain your racial transformation on a short or long rest, instead of only a long rest.

feat.name: Antecedents' Teachings
entries: The teaching of your forebears is oft glimpsed into. Through continuous glimpses, you have expanded your skillset, and become more reliable at peeking into the experiences of your ancestors. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in two skills of your choice from {@skill Animal Handling}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Stealth}, and {@skill Survival}.
If you roll the number on your Ancestral Glimpse trait on an ability check that uses either of those skills, the trait triggers without expending a use of it.

feat.name: Blood of a Lion
entries: Most of your kin take after the ways of the jaguar, the cheetah, other faster cats. But you tapped into the blood of Kings. You gain the following benefits:

items: The damage die of your claws increases to a {@dice d8}.

If you move at least 20 feet in a straight line toward a creature and hit it with a claw attack on the same turn, the target must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be knocked {@condition prone}.

As an action, you can let out a sonorous roar of terrifying power in a 30-foot cone of your choice, a show of your strength. Hostile creatures in the area must make a Constitution saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Constitution modifier) or take {@dice 1d12} thunder damage. If a creature fails this saving throw, the next attack roll against it has advantage. You can't roar in this way again until you finish a short or long rest. The damage of this ability increases by {@dice 1d12} at 5th level ({@dice 2d12}), 11th level ({@dice 3d12}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d12}).

feat.name: Spiritual Guidance
entries: The connection with your ancestors is deeply ingrained in your soul, not only making you more in touch with your ancestors, but also extending their help to your allies. You roll three times at the end of a long rest for your Ancestral Glimpse racial trait, instead of once. You can use each result only once.
Additionally, you can use a glimpse when an ally you can see rolls the number on the glimpse on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw.

feat.name: Nerthulian Magic
entries: Your inherent leviathan nature calls, coming forth in the form of magical essence. You learn the {@spell shape water|XGE} cantrip.

In addition, you learn the {@spell crashing wave|TheDarkAscension} and {@spell swim through space|TheDarkAscension} spells, which you can cast at the lowest level for each. Once you cast either spell, you must finish a long rest before you can cast that spell again using this feat. Wisdom is your spellcasting ability for these spells.

feat.name: Warrior of the Deep
entries: You're blessed by the great deity of the sea, which sharpens your skill with traditional nerthulian weaponry. You gain the following benefits:

items: As a bonus action when you hit a creature within 20 feet of you with a ranged weapon attack using a {@item javelin|phb}, {@item spear|phb}, {@item trident|phb}, you can move up to half your speed. If you end this movement within 5 feet of the creature you hit, you can retrieve the weapon you attacked with and use it to make a melee weapon attack against the creature.

When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack using a {@item javelin|phb}, {@item spear|phb}, or {@item trident|phb}, you can impale it, which causes the weapon to stay lodged in its body until the weapon is removed as an action. While the creature is impaled this way, you have advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks to attempt to grapple it. Once you use this ability, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Watery Warlord
entries: You've been hardened on the battlefield, and can lead even during the cruelest conflict. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cause your voice to be heard up to 300 feet away.
When you use your Tactical Mind, you can instead cause your initiative to become equal to the target's initiative, or cause the target's initiative to become equal to your own.
While you're within 5 feet of a hostile creature, you can cause it to have disadvantage on opportunity attacks against creatures other than you.

feat.name: Confidence Is Elementary
entries: Wild and confident, forever free, and forever facing your fears head on! Your presence inspires courage, and what is courage if not elementary? You gain the following benefits:

items: Being {@condition frightened} of a creature doesn't prevent you from moving closer to it.

As a bonus action, you can inspire courage and confidence in your allies, wreathing them in elemental energy. Choose a number of allies you can see within 30 feet of you equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1). For 1 minute, or until you lose concentration (as though concentrating on a spell), these creatures have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}, and deal an additional {@dice 1d4} damage on attacks. The damage's type changes depending on your subrace: cold (Water), bludgeoning (Earth), thunder (Air), fire (Fire). You can't use this ability again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Crow's Insight
entries: Your progenitor blesses you with magical insight, giving you a deeper inherent synergy with magical items. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can bypass any racial prerequisites for magic items.

As a bonus action, you can increase the attack, damage, or AC bonus of one magic item in your possession of your choice by 1. Only one magic item can benefit from this feature at a time, and only you benefit from it.

As an action, you can end attunement to a magic item you are attuned to within 100 feet of you. If you are holding or wearing a magic item that requires attunement, you can attune to it as part of the same action. You can't end attunement again in this way until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Earthen Armor
entries: Hardening your skin like the earth you are aligned with makes armor hardly worth having, at times. You gain the following benefits:

items: You learn the {@spell stonefist|TheDarkAscension} cantrip. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for the spell.
Your skin hardens like earth. While you aren't wearing armor, you can calculate your AC as 13 + your Dexterity modifier. You can use a shield and still gain this benefit.
While you aren't wearing armor, bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage that you take from nonmagical weapons is reduced by 3.

feat.name: Eternal Threat
entries: Duneyrr was renowned for his legendary fortitude, and how his very presence on the battlefield turned the heads and attention of his foes. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you have all of your hit points, any creature that can see you have disadvantage on attack rolls against your allies, unless the creature can't be {@condition frightened}.

When you become {@condition unconscious}, you don't fall {@condition prone} or drop what you're holding, even if you die. You instead do so after becoming {@condition unconscious} if you are hit by an attack, take damage, or are moved.

When you would be reduced to 0 hit points, but not killed outright, you can call upon the life flowing through your veins for a surge of adrenaline to keep fighting. You regain a number of hit points equal to your level + your Constitution modifier. At the end of your next turn, you gain a level of exhaustion. You can't use this ability again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Blood of the Ordning
entries: The blood of ancient giants flows through your veins, giving you immense power and allowing you to surpass your mortal size to closer match your ancestors. You gain an additional use of your Stone's Endurance trait.

Additionally, you can use an action to grow in size, using the blood of the Ordning within you. For 1 minute, you are under the enlarge effects of the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell (no concentration required). Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

feat.name: Braveheart
entries: As a creature that inherently looks up to the great dragons, you have sought to better yourself, to one day be a good representation of them. You gain two additional uses of your Heart of the Dragon trait.

If an ally that can hear you within 30 feet of you would fail a saving throw, you can expend a use of Heart of the Dragon as a reaction to have them reroll the saving throw. If the ally succeeds on the new roll, they have advantage on the next attack roll or saving throw they make before the end of their next turn.

feat.name: Breath of Ancients
entries: The breath of those you take after is empowered, the glands growing even stronger through use and training. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your Breath Weapon's damage dice are doubled when you gain this feat. When your Breath Weapon gains an additional damage die, it instead gains two additional damage dice.
If your Breath Weapon is a cone, you can double its distance when you use it. If your Breath Weapon is a line, you can double its length when you use it.
If you take damage from a hostile dragon's breath weapon, you regain a use of your Breath Weapon. You can't regain a use of it in this way again until you complete a long rest.

feat.name: Draconic Incorporation
entries: Having practiced draconic forms, through rigorous focus and perpetual trials, you have managed to incorporate aspects of dragons into yourself. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you use your Change Appearance trait, you can manifest draconic properties to enhance your form, gaining one of the following features until you change it: claws, eyes, or wings.

{@b Claws.} Your claws become natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike. If you strike with two free hands, the {@dice d6} becomes a {@dice d8}.
{@b Eyes.} You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks based on sight.
{@b Wings.} You have a flying speed of 30 feet, which can't be used to ascend. When you end one of your turns flying in the air, you descend by 5 feet.

feat.name: Fallen Angel
entries: With your connection to your celestial heritage absent, all that has been left in its place is a well of terrifying necrotic power for you to tap into. You gain the following benefits:

items: Creatures don't benefit from advantage on saving throws made to resist being {@condition frightened} by you.

As an action, you can expend your Healing Hands trait to pulse necrotic energies into a creature you touch. The creature takes necrotic damage equal to your level + your Charisma modifier, and you regain hit points equal to half the damage you deal.

During Necrotic Shroud, you can repeat the effect causing creatures within 10 feet of you to be {@condition frightened} as a bonus action on each of your turns. If a creature succeeds its saving throw, it can't be affected again until 24 hours pass.

feat.name: Hakuna Matata
entries: Growing up, you were always free from the worries and stress of life, a habit and mindset that carried into adulthood. You gain the following benefits:

items: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}. If you already have advantage, or gain advantage from another source, you can reroll one of the dice once when making the saving throw.

Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make using {@item brewer's supplies|phb}, as well as the {@filter gaming set|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set} and instrument you chose with your Worry Free trait.

If a creature is {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, or suffering from madness within 60 feet of you, you can sing a song to soothe them over the course of 1 minute. At the end of the minute, the creature can repeat the saving throw against the effect with advantage. Regardless of success or failure, the target can't benefit from this feature again until it benefits from a long rest.

feat.name: Karma Chameleon
entries: Having learned how to hunt for your companions growing up, you learned to take upon different aspects of your lizardly heritage, specifically focused upon hunting and camouflage. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your climb speed increases by 10 feet.
You can climb difficult surfaces, including upside down on ceilings, without needing to make an ability check. While you aren't moving, you don't need your hands to remain on a surface.

If you remain pressed against a solid surface without moving or taking actions for 1 minute, you can cause your skin to naturally adapt to your surroundings. Until you move or take an action or reaction, you are {@condition invisible}.

feat.name: Relentless Crusader
entries: The depth of your anger for mortal evil is all-consuming, such so that you pull out even greater power from within you, but it need not consume your allies. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cast the {@spell bane} spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. You are aware of the location of creatures affected by the spell within 30 feet of you when cast using this feat, even if they are hidden or {@condition invisible}. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.
When you activate Radiant Consumption, you can choose creatures other than yourself to not be affected by the radiant damage it deals at the end of each of your turns.
You can unattune to cursed magic items without suffering the effects of the curse.

feat.name: Scales of the Vrael
entries: You have learned to harness the blood of your favored ancestors, to morph your own form into that of a snake's. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can speak with snakes, as with the {@spell speak with animals} spell.

As an action, you can assume the form of a {@creature giant poisonous snake}. While in this shape, you follow the rules for wild shape, except you retain your current hit points, hit dice, and hit point maximum, you retain your proficiency bonus, and you can still speak and cast spells that don't have material components. The DC to resist the poison damage becomes 12 + your proficiency bonus. This form lasts until you exit the form as an action.

feat.name: The Queen's Wings
entries: The blood of Glasya, Queen of the Erinyes, flows through your veins and blesses you with wings to fly. When you gain this feat, you can choose to have either the feathery wings of an {@creature erinyes}, or the leathery wings of an incubus or {@creature succubus}. You can sprout these wings from your back as an action or a bonus action, and they last until you cause them to fold into your back as an action or a bonus action. While you have these wings, you detect as a fiend, and you have a flying speed of 30 feet, which you can only use while you aren't wearing heavy armor.

If you are {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}, you can extend these wings as an action - sprouting them if they weren't already present - to break free of your confines, immediately ending all effects causing you to be {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained}. When you do so, creatures of your choice within 10 feet of you are pushed up to 10 feet away from you. You can't use your wings in this way again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Refractive Carapace
entries: Diamonds are inherently known for their hardness, and the diamondbacks are no different. In some, they can even refract light as a diamond does. Your Hardened Carapace increases to 16 + your Dexterity modifier (to a maximum of 2).

While benefiting from your Hardened Carapace, you can suffuse your carapace with refractive light as an action, making it difficult for others to target you from afar. For 1 minute, while you're in bright light ranged attacks have disadvantage against you, and you refract the bright light within 30 feet of you, and dim light for an additional 30 feet. If this light is sunlight, you refract it as sunlight. Once you use this action, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Vibrant Guardian
entries: You have tapped into your celestial ancestry, furthering the depth of your holy powers. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can cast the {@spell bless} spell, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for this spell.
The flying speed of your Radiant Soul trait is increased to 60 feet.
During Radiant Soul, you are also under the effects of the {@spell protection from evil and good} spell (no concentration required by you).

feat.name: Bane of Flyers
entries: Power in the name of glory is what the Dáinn practice, and as such their brawn can guide their accuracy. You gain the following benefits:

items: When making an attack with a ranged weapon, you can use your choice of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls. You must use the same modifier for both rolls.
Once on each of your turns, when you hit a ranged weapon attack against a flying creature, you can deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage to it.
When you use your Wrath of the Serpent trait with a ranged weapon attack, you can cause the target's speed to be halved until it uses an action to remove the projectile from its body.

feat.name: Gale Burst
entries: The ventiam spend much of their time commanding the winds and bursting from one place to another. You can do so with increased frequency. You gain the following benefits:

items: You learn the {@spell gust} cantrip. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.
You can use your Gale Force trait at will, without expending a use of it, but only to launch yourself up to 10 feet in any direction.

When you expend a use of Gale Force, you can command greater force to instead launch yourself up to 120 feet in any direction. If you do so, creatures within 5 feet of you must succeed on a Strength saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Intelligence modifier) or be pushed up to 10 feet away from you.

feat.name: Elven Ancestry
entries: Some are more in tune with their elven ancestry, such as yourself. You gain the following benefits:

items: You can trance as an elf does, instead of sleeping.
Choose a subrace of Elf. You gain one trait of your choice unique to the subrace.

feat.name: Light Armor Master
entries: You can use your armor to deftly dodge a deadly blaze, or a hail of arrows. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you aren't wearing medium or heavy armor, your speed increases by 5 feet.

While wearing light armor, when you make a Dexterity saving throw against an effect you can see, you can gain advantage on the saving throw. In order to gain this benefit, you can't be {@condition blinded}, {@condition deafened}, or {@condition incapacitated}. You can do so a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Death Bringer
entries: Whether approaching death or delivering it, you are a more formidable foe than most. You gain the following benefits:

items: While you have no more than half your hit points left, your melee weapon attacks deal additional damage equal to your Constitution modifier.

When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee weapon attack in combat, you regain a number of hit points equal to twice your proficiency bonus if you have no more than half of your hit points left.

Also when you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee weapon attack in combat, you can use your bonus action to move up your speed toward a different hostile creature you can see, ending the movement closer to the creature than you began, or to make one weapon attack.

feat.name: Wellspring of Vitality
entries: The latent power of the Spring of Youth from your ancestors' Plane flows through your veins once more. Your hit point maximum increases by an amount equal to your level when you gain this feat. Whenever you gain a level thereafter, your hit point maximum increases by an additional 1 hit point.
Additionally, you can cast the {@spell aid} spell, targeting up to five allies of your choice when cast using this feat, and you regain the ability to do so when you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Winds of Fury
entries: The winds are your ally, carrying you and your strikes to the destination you ordain. You gain the following benefits:

items: Your walking speed increases by 5 feet.
Once on each of your turns, when you hit a creature within 60 feet of you, you can push it up to 10 feet away from you, or pull it up to 10 feet towards you.

You can cast the {@spell warding wind|XGE} spell as an action, or as a reaction after you roll initiative, and regain the ability to do so when you finish a short or long rest. When cast this way, your ranged weapon attacks don't have disadvantage if the attack passes out of the wind. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for the spell.

feat.name: Expanded Knowledge
entries: You have honed your mind and your patience, expanding your capacity for knowledge. You gain the following benefits:

items: If you have levels in a class with a Spells Known column on its class table, you know an additional three spells from your class spell list. If you have levels in a class with spells that you prepare, you can prepare an additional three spells, and must spend additional time preparing them as normal for your class. If you have more than one class, and more than one of them fulfills the previously stated criteria, this applies to one class of your choice.

feat.name: Esoteric Adept
entries: When you gain this feat, choose one of the following damage types: force, necrotic, poison, psychic, radiant.

Spells you cast ignore resistance to damage of the chosen type. In addition, when preparing your spells or learning new ones, you can choose a spell not on your class spell list so long as it deals your chosen damage type. You can only learn or prepare a number of spells outside your class spell list equal to your proficiency bonus in this way.
You can select this feat multiple times. Each time you do so, you must choose a different damage type.

feat.name: Graviturgy Initiate
entries: You learn the {@spell catapult|xge} and {@spell levitate} spells, each of which you can cast once without expending a spell slot. Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells when you cast them using this feat (chosen when you gain this feat).

Additionally, as a bonus action, you can magically alter the weight of a Large or smaller nonmagical object you touch. You can reduce its weight to one-tenth of normal, or multiply its weight by ten. This change to the object's weight lasts for 1 hour, until you end it as a bonus action, or until you target another object in this way. If you alter a container's weight in this way, you can also alter the weight of any nonmagical object in the container while it is within the container.

feat.name: Light Weapon Master
entries: You've learned to specialize with lighter weaponry. You gain the following benefits:

items: Once per turn, when you deal damage with a light weapon, you can roll the weapon's damage die again and add it to the total.

If a light weapon has the thrown property, its normal and long ranges are doubled for you. Otherwise, you can treat it as though it has the thrown property, with a normal range of 20 feet and a long range of 60 feet.
While clothed or wearing armor, you can conceal up to two daggers or any weapons of a similar size on your person. While concealed, ability checks made to find the weapons have disadvantage.

feat.name: Natural Weapon Master
entries: When you gain this feat, choose a natural weapon you have, which can also be your fists. You gain a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls made with it, and it uses a {@dice d6} for damage. It is also considered a simple melee weapon that is magical and has the finesse property, if not already.
When you make a melee weapon attack as part of the Attack action on your turn, you can use your bonus action to make an attack with the natural weapon you chose with this feat.

feat.name: Indefatigable
entries: The test of endurance is one that is practically a pastime for you. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in Constitution saving throws. If you already have this proficiency, you can't have disadvantage on ability checks from {@condition exhaustion}.
You have advantage on saving throws made to resist {@condition exhaustion}.
The DC to avoid a level of {@condition exhaustion} after 24 hours without a long rest increases by 2 for every 24 hours after, instead of increasing by 5.

feat.name: Morning Person
entries: Waking up is the start of a new day. For many, the start of the day can be the worst, but for you the beginning of the day is when you shine brightest. When you finish a long rest, you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain temporary hit points equal to your level + your Constitution modifier.
You recover an additional level of {@condition exhaustion}.
You gain a {@dice d6} die that you can add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw you make within 1 hour.

feat.name: One Weapon Master
entries: You master wielding a weapon with a single hand. While you have at least one hand free, you gain the following benefits:

items: When you attack using a versatile weapon you're wielding with one hand, you deal damage as if you were wielding it with two hands.
When you attack a creature within 5 feet of you using a melee weapon you're wielding with one hand, you can use a bonus action to pry the creature's guard open. You gain advantage on the attack roll.
If you're disarmed of a weapon you're holding, you can use a reaction to immediately pick it up or to immediately draw another weapon.

feat.name: Tool Specialist
entries: Persistently sticking with a favored set of tools enables you insight into how to use them to their fullest extent. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency in one {@filter tool|items|type=tools;artisan's tools;instrument=sor} of your choice.
Choose one type of tool with which you have proficiency. You add double your proficiency bonus for any ability check you make with it.

feat.name: Shield Surfer
entries: A fun pastime turned into a combat tactic, you have mastered using a shield for mobility. You gain the following benefits:

items: You gain proficiency with {@item shield|phb|shields}.
You can don or doff shields as a bonus action.

If you move 10 feet or more in a straight line while holding a shield or a similarly shaped object, you can slide on it in a straight line without expending movement or provoking opportunity attacks. You can slide up to 10 feet for each 10 feet you moved. The distance you can slide is doubled if you slide downhill, and halved if you slide uphill.

feat.name: Thing of Legends
entries: The tales woven about you can be made of myths and legends, like standing strong in a dragon's breath and surviving, or being blasted by the ray of a beholder without turning to dust, but most importantly, these tales are sometimes true.
When you would fail a saving throw, you can choose to succeed instead. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

feat.name: Stalwart
entries: You stand tall, resilient, stalwart in the face of that which would sway, defeat, or cripple lesser people. You gain the following benefits:

items: When you gain this feat, choose one of the following conditions: {@condition charmed}, {@condition frightened}, {@condition paralyzed}, {@condition poisoned}. You have advantage on saving throws made to resist the chosen condition.

entries.name: Age
entries: Durumir mature at the age of 35, and can live to be 450 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: As the paragons of that which stands righteous, that which other races look up to, most would expect the durumir to tend towards lawful good. In fact, they are more often than not lawful neutral; obeying the laws of the land but protecting nature, by whatever means necessary.

entries.name: Size
entries: Durumir are often found to stand between 6 and 7 feet tall, weighing anywhere between 140 to 190 lbs, oddly lighter than most of their height despite maintaining the same or greater muscle mass. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: The Fruit of Knowledge
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Nature} skill.

entries.name: Mask of the Wild
entries: You can attempt to hide even when you are only lightly obscured by foliage, heavy rain, falling snow, mist, and other natural phenomena.

entries.name: Familiarity with the Cosmos
entries: By spending 1 hour uninterrupted in meditation, you can learn the general direction of the closest portal within 1 mile, along with which plane the portal is connected to, and can sense it until your next long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: As a denizen of the forest, your habitat was visited often by the Fey. You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: Four subraces populate the forests; Dáinn, Duneyrr, Duraþrór and Dvalinn.

entries.name: Age
entries: Mothfolk are born as larvae, and are only considered fully grown after metamorphosis. They are considered adults once their wings are fully grown, and live into their mid-forties on average. The oldest mothfolk live until their late fifties.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Mothfolk are slow to anger, yet quick to become impassioned about the things they find important, and they are good at making the most out of life. They lean toward chaotic alignments, and away from evil ones.

entries.name: Size
entries: Mothfolk range from a bit under six feet to almost seven feet tall, not counting their lengthy antennae. Their builds tend towards the light and lithe, and they usually weigh between 130 and 160 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Compound Vision
entries: Your compound eyes are excellent at finding what little light there is in darkness. You can see in dim light as if it were bright light out to 120 feet, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can discern colors in dim light and darkness, but only on the ultraviolet spectrum.

entries.name: Wings
entries: As a mothkin, you have large, cloak-like wings. You have a flying speed equal to your walking speed, which can't be used to ascend. When you end one of your turns flying in the air, you descend by 5 feet.

entries.name: Ride the Wind
entries: As an action, you can beat your wings and ascend up to 30 feet. You can't use this action again until you touch the ground. You can't use your wings if you are unable to move, and you can't Ride the Wind if you are wearing heavy armor.

entries.name: Armed and Dangerous
entries: Mothfolk have four arms, and each of them can hold an object as normal. You don't benefit from a shield if you're holding a weapon with the two-handed property or more than one weapon. You can interact with a second object or feature of the environment for free during your turn.

entries.name: Natural Camouflage
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Ivathi. Ivathi is a language native to mothfolk. It is learned and internalized as a larvae and spoken upon emergence. The language is complex and almost impossible to speak with a human mouth, as mothfolk speak with a specialized organ in their throat that other beings lack. Ivathi sounds like a series of squishes, clicks, and chitters, cut with the occasional thrumming or soft vowel. It uses its own alphabet, and translating it to other scripts only results in an approximation of the language's true sound.
If you know Ivathi, you can also communicate very simple ideas to moths, and understand similar ideas from moths that choose to share them with you.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: All mothfolk share a common ancestor, but there are three distinct groups of mothfolk that roam the world, each with their own customs and abilities. Most live in secluded communities, but some have chosen to engage in the world society. Choose one of these subraces.

entries.name: Age
entries: Harpies mature as humans do, but live roughly twenty years longer on average.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Due to the world at large generally shunning them, harpies tend toward chaotic neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Harpies are typically between 5 and a half and 6 and a half feet tall, while some wind harpies can reach as tall as 7 feet. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 30 feet, and you have a flying speed equal to your walking speed, but you fall if you end your turn in the air with nothing else holding you aloft.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Harpy's Talons
entries: You have powerful talons, which give you a climbing speed equal to your walking speed. In addition, your talons are natural weapons, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal slashing damage equal to 1d4 + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Recluse's Hobby
entries: You have proficiency with one {@filter artisan's tool|items|type=artisan's tools} of your choice.

entries.name: Harpy's Melody
entries: You know magical melodies, which you can start as an action unless a melody specifies otherwise, that can affect a number of creatures within 120 feet of you that can hear you up to your proficiency bonus. Each harpy knows how to sing a Luring Song, but each subrace of harpy knows one additional song, as well as a means of enhancing their Luring Song at 3rd level. After your melody starts, you must take an action or a  bonus action on your subsequent turns to continue singing, but you can stop singing at any time, and the song ends if you are incapacitated. If a saving throw is tied to a melody, the DC is equal to 8 + your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Melody: Luring Song
entries: You can begin singing a beguiling melody. Humanoid and giant targets of your choice within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} until the song ends.

While charmed, a target is {@condition incapacitated} and ignores the songs of other harpies. If the target is more than 5 feet away from you, it must move on its turn toward you by the most direct route. It doesn't avoid opportunity attacks, but before moving into damaging terrain, such as lava or a pit, and whenever it takes damage, a target can repeat the saving throw. A creature can also repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns. If a creature's saving throw is successful, the effect ends on it.
A target that successfully saves is immune to your Luring Song for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Auran.

entries.name: Subraces
