entries: Harpies are divided into three subraces, based on their favored weather: rain, thunder, and wind.

entries.name: Age
entries: Young dragonborn grow quickly. They walk hours after hatching, attain the size and development of a 10-year-old human child by the age of 3, and reach adulthood by 15. They live to be around 80.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Dragonborn tend to extremes, with chromatic dragonborn taking on their ancestor's cruelty and malice, while metallic dragonborn follow ideals of benevolence and justice.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dragonborn are taller and heavier than humans, standing well over 6 feet tall and averaging almost 250 pounds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Breath Weapon
entries: You can use your action to exhale destructive energy. Your draconic ancestry determines the size, shape, and damage type of the exhalation.

When you use your breath weapon, each creature in the area of the exhalation must make a saving throw, the type of which is determined by your draconic ancestry. The DC for this saving throw equals 8 + your Constitution modifier + your proficiency bonus. A creature takes {@dice 2d6} damage on a failed save, and half as much damage on a successful one. The damage increases to {@dice 3d6} at 6th level, {@dice 4d6} at 11th level, and {@dice 5d6} at 16th level.
After you use your breath weapon, you can't use it again until you complete a short or long rest.

entries.name: Natural Armor
entries: You have tough, scaly skin. When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 14 + your Dexterity modifier (max 2). You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic. Draconic is thought to be one of the oldest languages and is often used in the study of magic. The language sounds harsh to most other creatures and includes numerous hard consonants and sibilants.

entries.name: Color
entries: In the same manner as true dragons, dragonborn are divided into a number of different colors. These colors fall into two categories, the chromatics — black, blue, green, red, and white — and the metallics — brass, bronze, copper, gold, and silver.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tieflings mature at the same rate as humans but live a few years longer.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: A tiefling will typically steer toward neutrality, being left with little choice but to look out for themselves in a world that seems to care little for them.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tieflings vary in height wildly, depending on the humanoid bloodline they were birthed from. You are Medium or Small. You choose the size when you select this race.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Thanks to your fiendish heritage, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Fiendish Resistance
entries: You gain resistance to cold, fire, or lightning damage. You choose the resistance when you select this race.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: Tieflings can trace their bloodline back to one of the three fiendish planes, and are thus split into three: Abyssal, Gehennal, and Infernal.

entries.name: Age
entries: Kobolds reach adulthood at age 6 and can live up to 120 years, but rarely do so.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Kobolds are fundamentally selfish, making most of them evil, but their reliance on the strength of their group makes them trend toward law.

entries.name: Size
entries: Kobold are between 2 and 3 feet tall and weigh between 25 and 35 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern colour in darkness, only shades of grey.

entries.name: Distraction
entries: As an action on your turn, you can distract a nearby foe. Until the end of your next turn, your allies gain advantage on attack rolls against a creature of your choice within 30 feet of you.
Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Natural Trapper
entries: You are proficient in thieves tools, and whenever you make an ability check to create traps (found on page 84 of Xanathar's Guide to Everything), you can add double your proficiency bonus to the check, rather than your normal proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Heart of the Dragon
entries: When you succeed on a saving throw, you can have the next attack roll or saving throw you make before the end of your next turn have advantage.
Once you have used this trait, you cannot do so again until you complete a short or long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Satyrs mature and age at about the same rate as humans.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Satyrs delight in living a life free of the mantle of law. They gravitate toward being good, but some have devious streaks and enjoy causing dismay.

entries.name: Size
entries: Satyrs range from just under 5 feet to about 6 feet in height, with generally slender builds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Ram
entries: You can use your head and horns to make unarmed strikes. When you hit with them, the strike deals {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier bludgeoning damage, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.

entries.name: Mirthful Leaps
entries: Whenever you make a long jump or a high jump, you can cover an extra 5 feet, even when making a standing jump. This extra distance costs movement as usual.

Alternatively, when you make such a jump, you can add a number of feet to the jump equal to five times your proficiency bonus. This extra distance doesn't cost movement, but you must expend at least 5 feet of movement when jumping to do so. You can only add distance in this way a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Reveler
entries: As an embodiment of revelry, you have proficiency in your choice of the {@skill Performance} or {@skill Persuasion} skills, your choice of {@item brewer's supplies|phb} or {@item cook's utensils|phb}, and one musical instrument of your choice.
Additionally, you have advantage on checks made with your chosen proficiencies when preparing or partaking in a meal, feast, or other celebration.

entries.name: Comforting Presence
entries: When you finish a short rest, choose up to six creatures that finished the rest within 30 feet of you. Each creature gains a {@dice d4} die that it can add to one ability check, attack roll, or saving throw it makes within 1 hour. It can also roll the die as a bonus action to gain temporary hit points equal to the number rolled + your proficiency bonus. A creature can't benefit from this feature again until it completes a long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Age
entries: Nerthulians mature at 21, and can live to be 250 years old, though ones that live to be over 150 are rare, more often than not finding death on the battlefield.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The militaristic nature of their society will almost always lead to one of two things: Creatures tending towards law, or chaos. To be neutral is almost unheard of.

entries.name: Size
entries: Nerthulians often stand between 6 and 7 feet tall in adulthood. As they become older, they can reach 8 feet tall, at which point they cease growing. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Depth Dweller
entries: You can breathe both air and water, and you ignore any of the drawbacks caused by a deep, underwater environment.

entries.name: Deepsight
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray. If you are underwater, you can instead see in dim light and darkness within 120 feet of you as if it were bright light.

entries.name: Nerthulian Weapon Training
entries: You have proficiency with the {@item javelin|phb}, {@item spear|phb}, {@item trident|phb}, and {@item net|phb}.

entries.name: Tactical Mind
entries: When you roll initiative, you can swap your initiative and the initiative of a willing creature you can see.

entries.name: Wave Cutter
entries: You can use your action to shoot a stream of water from your mouth, sharp enough to pierce steel, which forms a line 30 feet long and 1 foot wide. All creatures in the area must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC equal to 8 + your Strength modifier + your proficiency bonus). On a failed save, a target takes piercing damage equal to your Strength modifier x 4, and the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn has advantage. You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Your aquatic heritage lends you the knowledge of the tongue spoken by the leviathans. You can speak, read, and write Common and Aquan.

entries.name: Age
entries: Tegokka often reach adulthood by 10 years of age, as they mature quickly. While most tegokka reach the end of their life by the time they're 80, some manage to live to the old age of 110.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Many tegokka are strong believers in the natural forces of the world, and believe perfect balance can be achieved if nothing is tampered with. As such, most tegokka are true neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Tegokka are short, standing only between 2 and 3 feet tall, and weigh around 25 pounds. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Adhesive Skin
entries: You have a climbing speed of 30 feet, and you don't suffer disadvantage on ability checks you make while climbing a slippery or wet surface.

entries.name: Forest Resilience
entries: You're used to the natural dangers of a forest environment. You have advantage on saving throws against diseases.

entries.name: Handy Tail
entries: When you take the Use an Object action, you can use your tail to manipulate an object, open an unlocked door or container, stow or retrieve an item from an open container, or pour the contents out of a vial. Your tail can't carry more than 10 pounds.

entries.name: Shed Skin
entries: As a bonus action, you can shed your outer most layer of skin. You immediately escape from any grapple, moving to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you. In addition, if you are {@condition restrained} or {@condition poisoned}, those conditions immediately end.
Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Worry Free
entries: You gain proficiency in {@item brewer's supplies|phb}, as well as one {@filter gaming set|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set} and one instrument of your choice.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can read and write Common and one other language of your choice. Most tegokka often learn Elvish, Gnomish, or Halfling, as those languages reflect the races the tegokka deal with the most.

entries.name: Age
entries: Ventiam mature at 85 years old, and can live to be 900.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The ventiam are an incredibly neutral people, often not feeling one way or the other so long as they further their own research. Some tend towards the creation of that which will help the forces of good, and some tend towards imbuing their creations with the dark arts.

entries.name: Size
entries: The ventiam were not created with any care for their height, and so are not incredibly tall. They are usually between 5 and a half feet to 6 and a half feet tall, and can weigh between 140 to 190 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Ease of Descent
entries: You can cast the {@spell feather fall} spell at will, without expending a spell slot, targeting only yourself.

entries.name: Gale Force
entries: As a bonus action, you can launch yourself up to 60 feet in any direction without provoking opportunity attacks. You can't use this feature again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Knowledge of the Ancients
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana} skill. Additionally, your proficiency bonus is doubled for any Intelligence ({@skill Arcana}) checks and Intelligence ({@skill History}) checks made to determine the origin or power of a magical item.

entries.name: Ventiam Technique
entries: You gain proficiency with one type of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} of your choice.

entries.name: Denizens of the Sky
entries: You're acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet. You're also naturally adapted to cold climates, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Your grand knowledge and long times of research gives you a deep insight into the dialects of other languages. You can speak, read, and write Common and up to 2 other languages of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Annukara mature at 7 years old, and can live to be 65 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The annukara often do not follow laws, and tend towards a naturally chaotic state amongst their nomadic tribes, with only a single unifying factor; protecting the young.

entries.name: Size
entries: The annukara are relatively above average in height. They are usually around 6 feet to 6 and a half feet tall, and can weigh between 180 to 250 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Heartsense
entries: While the skin of your palms are in contact with a creature, you can detect the heartbeat of that creature and any other creatures in physical contact with it. You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Insight}) checks against a living creature you can sense in this way. An unwilling creature must be {@condition grappled} or {@condition restrained} for you to use this trait on it.

entries.name: Desert Wanderers
entries: You're acclimated to harsh deserts. You're also naturally adapted to hot climates and extreme heat, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Quadrupedal Instinct
entries: If you have both hands free, your movement speed is not halved while {@condition prone}, and it only costs you 10 feet of movement to stand from {@condition prone}.

entries.name: Shared Burden
entries: When a creature within 5 feet of you takes damage, you can use a reaction to reduce the damage it takes by your proficiency bonus. You then lose the same number of hit points.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You hold a heritage of darkness within you, one which helps you speak to the depths. You can speak, read, and write Common and Abyssal.

entries.name: Age
entries: Pseudodragons mature at 14 years old, and reach their peak at around 120 years old. At 200 years old, they can hold onto life for as long as they deem necessary before passing on, but they invariably retire as their bodies can longer sustain the lifestyle they otherwise may have.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: With the spirit of freedom ingrained in their history, pseudodragons tend towards chaotic alignments.

entries.name: Size
entries: Pseudodragons are generally longer than they are tall due to the fact that they are often quadrupeds. They are around 3 to 4 feet in length, and weigh around 40 to 60 lbs. Your size is Small.

entries.name: Speed
entries: Your base walking speed is 25 feet, and you have a flying speed of 30 feet. If you end your turn in the air with nothing to hold you aloft, you fall.

entries.name: Draconic Nature
entries: Your creature type is dragon, rather than humanoid.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Ancient Power
entries: As a reaction when you would make a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, you can gain advantage on the roll. You can use this trait twice, and regain both uses when you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Draconic Awareness
entries: As a bonus action, you can gain blindsight with a range of 30 feet until the start of your next turn. You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, regaining all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Nostalgic Form
entries: By spending 1 minute in concentration, you can make yourself Tiny as though under the reduce effects of the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell. While Tiny, you cannot wield weapons nor hold anything heavier than 10 lbs, but your wings can now support your weight. You do not fall at the end of your turn if you are in the air with nothing holding you aloft. You must maintain a deep concentration on this form to maintain it. Engaging in combat, or taking damage, will immediately end it. You can also end it at will (no action required).

entries.name: Languages
entries: The draconic energy flowing through your mind enables you great knowledge. You can speak, read, and write Common and Draconic.

entries.name: Age
entries: Changelings mature slightly faster than humans but share a similar lifespan—typically a century or less. While a changeling can shapeshift to conceal their age, the effects of aging still catch up to them. They usually mature at 15, and live to around 90 or 100.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Many changelings tend to embrace good, while others embrace evil. Some err towards the path of abiding law, while others lean towards the freedom that chaos can bring. Often time a changeling can end up between a lot of these, but due to their nature and the experiences such a nature brings, it is exceedingly rare that one is truly neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Changelings are about the same size as humans, ranging from 5 to 6 feet tall in their natural form. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Change Appearance
entries: As an action, you can change your appearance and your voice. You determine the specifics of the changes, including your coloration, hair length, and sex. You can also adjust your height and weight, but not so much that your size changes. You can make yourself appear as a member of another race, though none of your game statistics change. You can't duplicate the appearance of a creature you've never seen, and you must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs that you have. Your clothing and equipment aren't changed by this trait.
You stay in the new form until you use an action to revert to your true form or until you die. If you die, you revert to your true form over the course of 1 minute.

entries.name: Natural Deceiver
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill.

entries.name: Blessing of the Ambiguous One
entries: You always know when you are targeted by a divination spell, and as a reaction to being targeted by one, you can call upon the blessing of your ancestry. You are placed under the effects of the {@spell nondetection} spell, but with a duration of 1 hour instead of 8 hours. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you finish a long rest. Activating this ability isn't perceptible by any means.

entries.name: Gift for Teaching
entries: You have a knack for imparting what you have learned onto others due to being able to mimic their bodies. Over the course of 1 hour, you can teach a creature proficiency with a skill you are proficient in. The creature must have an Intelligence of 6 or higher and be able to communicate with you. This proficiency lasts until the creature finishes a long rest, or until another creature benefits from the trait in this way. A creature can't benefit from this trait if it already has the benefits from another source, or if it has this trait.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and two other languages of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Lagavir mature at the same rate as humans, and can live to be around 200 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The lagavir rarely align themselves with anything other than the interests of their heritage and home, but their cousins of the forest - the durumir - often ground their beliefs in lawful neutral.

entries.name: Size
entries: Lagavir generally average over 7 feet tall in height, weighing in between 180 and 250 lbs. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Lagomorphic Legs
entries: You can long jump up to your walking speed and high jump up to half of your walking speed, with or without a running start, unless you would be able to jump longer or higher.

entries.name: Ancestral Glimpse
entries: When you finish a long rest, roll a {@dice d20} and record the number rolled, as a glimpse into an ancestor's experiences that you correct when chanced upon. If it is a 20, reroll it. The first time you roll that number on an ability check, attack roll, or saving throw, it becomes a 20. The glimpse is lost once used, or when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: Life in the forests has lent you the language of the Fey, to commune with them when they arrive as your ancestors once did. You can speak, read, and write Common and Sylvan.

entries.name: Subraces
entries: The lagavir fall into two categories, depending upon how they focus upon their heritage and view the path to peace; Ana-Vir, and Vir-Ana.

entries.name: Age
entries: Dajix mature at 15 years of age, and can live up to 100 years old, but their lack of boundaries to which they will extend their devotion and overall recklessness makes it rare for one to live beyond 60.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: The dajix are unpredictable, irregular, and often go against anything that doesn't fit the narrative that best suits the object of their devotion. They are usually chaotic, and are very rarely lawful.

entries.name: Size
entries: Dajix are usually between 4 feet tall and 6 feet tall, rarely differing in weight from a healthy human of the same height. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Dajixian Culture
entries: You gain proficiency in {@skill Deception} and one type of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} of your choice.

entries.name: Obsession
entries: As a bonus action, you can choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. You're {@condition charmed} by that creature for 1 minute, or until it dies. While {@condition charmed} by that creature, you're immune to being {@condition charmed} by any other creature. You can't use this trait again until you finish a short or long rest. If you are immune to being {@condition charmed}, you ignore the immunity.

entries.name: Painful Love
entries: Being {@condition charmed} by a creature doesn't prevent you from attacking it or targeting it with harmful effects or abilities, and when you become {@condition charmed} by a creature, you have advantage on the next attack roll you make against it within the next minute.

entries.name: Pursue Love
entries: While a creature you're {@condition charmed} by is within line of sight, your movement speed increases by 15 feet, and you can't willingly move away from it.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Dhampir mature at the same rate as humans, but live twice as long.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: A dhampir's alignment is entirely dependent upon their upbringing: Those that are shunned can become evil, but those that are accepted often turn good. Neutrality is rare amongst dhampir.

entries.name: Size
entries: First-generation dhampir usually follow the average height and weight of their mother's original race, but invariably, the code of the First Vampire shapes the generations that come after to be as humans are. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Blood Born
entries: You have resistance to necrotic damage.

entries.name: Sunlight Sensitivity
entries: You have disadvantage on attack rolls and on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on sight when you, the target of your attack, or whatever you are trying to perceive is in direct sunlight.

entries.name: Lifedrinker
entries: As an action, you can drain blood from a willing creature, or one that is {@condition grappled} by you, {@condition incapacitated}, or {@condition restrained}. Make an unarmed strike against the target. If the target is willing, you automatically hit. On a hit, you deal an additional {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage, and you gain temporary hit points equal to the necrotic damage dealt.
This necrotic damage increases by an additional {@dice 1d6} at 5th level ({@dice 2d6}), 11th level ({@dice 3d6}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d6}).
After dealing necrotic damage with this trait, your Sunlight Sensitivity trait is suppressed until you finish a long rest or take radiant damage.

entries.name: Beguiling Charm
entries: As an action, you can attempt to charm one humanoid you can see within 30 feet of you. If the target can see you, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier), and does so with advantage if you or your companions are fighting it, or be {@condition charmed} by you. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw. While the creature is {@condition charmed}, it regards you as a friendly acquaintance, and it is always a willing target for your Lifedrinker attack.

Each time you or your companions do anything harmful to the target, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success. Otherwise, the charm lasts for 1 hour, or until you end it as a bonus action. When the effect ends, the target knows you {@condition charmed} it.
Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Age
entries: Although elves reach physical maturity at about the same age as humans, the elven understanding of adulthood goes beyond physical growth to encompass worldly experience. An elf typically claims adulthood and an adult name around the age of 100 and can live to be 750 years old.

entries.name: Alignment
entries: Elves love freedom, variety, and self-expression, so they lean strongly toward the gentler aspects of chaos. They value and protect others' freedom as well as their own, and they are more often good than not.

entries.name: Size
entries: Elves range from under 5 to over 6 feet tall and have slender builds. Your size is Medium.

entries.name: Darkvision
entries: Accustomed to twilit forests and the night sky, you have superior vision in dark and dim conditions. You can see in dim light within 60 feet of you as if it were bright light, and in darkness as if it were dim light. You can't discern color in darkness, only shades of gray.

entries.name: Keen Senses
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill.

entries.name: Fey Ancestry
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed}, and magic can't put you to sleep.

entries.name: Trance
entries: Elves don't need to sleep. Instead, they meditate deeply, remaining semiconscious, for 4 hours a day. (The Common word for such meditation is "trance.") While meditating, you can dream after a fashion; such dreams are actually mental exercises that have become reflexive through years of practice. After resting in this way, you gain the same benefit that a human does from 8 hours of sleep.
{@note If you meditate during a long rest, you finish the rest after only 4 hours. You otherwise obey all the rules for a long rest; only the duration is changed.}

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Elvish. Elvish is fluid, with subtle intonations and intricate grammar. Elven literature is rich and varied, and their songs and poems are famous among other races. Many bards learn their language so they can add Elvish ballads to their repertoires.

optionalfeature.name: Augmented Shadow
entries: The damage of the bestowed shadow's claw attack increases from a {@dice d4} to a {@dice d10}, and it gains proficiency in all saving throws.

optionalfeature.name: Silent Watcher
entries: The bestowed shadow gains proficiency in the {@skill Perception} skill, as well as two other skills of your choice.

optionalfeature.name: Cunning Shade
entries: The bestowed shadow can take the Dash, Disengage, or Help action as a bonus action.

optionalfeature.name: Eldritch Imbuement
entries: When you cast the {@spell eldritch blast} cantrip, you can forgo any number of beams to allow your bestowed shadow to make that many additional attacks with the {@action Attack} action on its next turn. So long as you forgo at least one beam, its reach extends to 10 feet, and its attacks are magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances and immunities until the start of your next turn..

optionalfeature.name: Malleable Dark
entries: The bestowed shadow can make itself Large as an action, immediately ending any other size-altering effects upon it. This effect lasts until the shadow ends it as an action. While Large in this way, the bestowed shadow has advantage on Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks and it cannot be affected by the {@spell enlarge/reduce} spell.

optionalfeature.name: Vigorous Defender
entries: The bestowed shadow adds six times your warlock level to its hit points, instead of four times your warlock level.

optionalfeature.name: Midnight Resilience
entries: The bestowed shadow gains resistance to necrotic and radiant damage. While in darkness, it gains resistance to force damage.

optionalfeature.name: The Swift Umbra
entries: The bestowed shadow's speed increases by 20 feet, its AC becomes 15 + PB, and opportunity attacks have disadvantage against it.

optionalfeature.name: Twilight Fiend
entries: The bestowed shadow's type changes to fiend, and it gains resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage from nonmagical attacks. Additionally, it has advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

monster.name: Bestowed Shadow

hp.special: 3 + your Charisma modifier + four times your warlock level

languages: speaks the languages you can speak

trait.name: Dark Shroud
entries: Bright light within 5 feet of the shadow becomes dim light.

trait.name: Return to Darkness
entries: As a bonus action, the shadow can become a part of your shadow once more. You can use a bonus action on your turn to activate the shadow, and it appears in an unoccupied space of your choice within 5 feet of you. The shadow cannot manifest in nonmagical darkness, but if you are in magical darkness, it can manifest anywhere in the radius that you can see. Additionally, magical darkness doesn't impede the shadow's darkvision.

trait.name: Shadow of Yourself
entries: The bestowed shadow uses your mental ability scores, and has proficiency in Charisma saving throws if you do.

action.name: Claw
entries: {@atk mw} {@hitYourSpellAttack} to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@h}{@damage 1d4 + 3 + PB} slashing damage.

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: An ash-covered diary or journal, a set of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} (one of your choice), a straw hat, a holy symbol of a Good deity, a set of ash-covered {@item traveler's clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 5gp

entries.name: Requirement: Non-Evil Alignment
entries: To take this background, one must have dwelled within the ashes for long enough to have already been affected by them to some degree. As a result of this, you cannot take this background if you start as an Evil-aligned character.

entries.name: Feature: Spirit of Justice
entries: Wherever you go, people find themselves drawn to you for comfort. They see you as kind, and mark you as a Good and just soul upon first meeting you, regardless of how your actions have swayed your alignment on the axes. You might find yourself thrust into a position where your input can mean the difference between life or death of a prisoner, or seeking your advice on how they should move forward with a moral quandary.
You can use this to your advantage, as people are less likely to oppose your presence in discussions of importance, and often value your input after even the briefest of introductions.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Ash Dwellers hold some aspect of the Platinum Dragon's soul within themselves, and are usually soft-hearted, but can also be strong willed and determined. They usually prefer to see the good in someone's heart than to dismiss them as irredeemable.

rows: If I see someone in trouble, I can't help but want to solve their woes.
It takes a lot for any heat to be too much. In fact, much of the world is too cold for my liking.
A long walk for me usually spans over twenty-four hours. Anything shorter is brisk.
Having anything but hills of ash around me is unnerving. I'm not used to seeing buildings, trees, or anything dotting the horizon.
I cover my hands in fire ash to feel closer to home.
Even if I'm picky about the people I travel with, I always delight in having new personalities around me.
I can get a little overzealous when I'm invested in my objective. Just a little. I don't know why everyone thinks it's a problem.
I don't value wealth any more than I value the people I can help with it.
Being Good is nice and all, but a part of me does miss having darker desires.
I feel more ready to die for my cause than I do to live for it.
Justice. I am honor-bound to see justice through to its end, as the law dictates, and will suffer no transgressions against it. (Lawful)
Mercy. To suffer is to be mortal, but to stand by while others suffer and do nothing is unforgivable. (Good)
Reform. Not all laws are just. If my head tells me the law while my heart tells me that law is wrong, I must stand by them both and bring reform. (Chaotic)
Wrath. Eons of anger for the unjust death of the Platinum Dragon will be set upon the Mother's children, this I swear. (Evil)
Peace. Nothing is black and white. Good can become evil, law can become chaos. To stand apart will allow me to bring harmony between them. (Neutral)
Generosity. Wealth serves a greater purpose than merely to be hoarded. It should be given to the masses, so that all may benefit and live. (Good)
My family, clan, or tribe is the most important thing in my life, even when they are far from me.
The ashes that linger upon my clothes are of those I couldn't help see the errors of their ways, and their faces stay with me as long as their ashes do.
I am taking bettering myself as a personal challenge, as there are many inner vices to overcome.
The dragon that slew my family saw the error of their ways soon after, and I'm trying to find the will to forgive them.
My wealth goes to the orphanage I grew up in when possible.
I've fallen in love with a dragon.
I often give away too much money, and end up unable to feed myself or rent a room to sleep for the night.
When it comes to creatures bigger than me, I've learned better than to try and fight them.
I find it hard to punish someone or make them follow through on their debts.
I'm way too trusting, and unwilling to believe someone's playing me, even when all evidence points to it.
Whilst I wear a loving and kind exterior, I'm still as rotten inside as I was when I first entered the Ashes.

items.name: Skill Proficiencies
items.entry: {@skill Arcana}, plus one from among {@skill History}, {@skill Nature}, and {@skill Religion}

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A bottle of black ink, a quill, 10 sheets of paper, a letter you can't get rid of explaining your exile and the reason behind it, a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 10 gp

entries.name: Prohibition: Wizardry
entries: In a situation as extreme as exile, the possibility that you discovered the greatest secrets of the Spires couldn't be ignored, and as a result you have been prohibited from practicing Wizardry, where it seems those secrets are supposedly most dangerous in practice.

entries.name: Feature: Arcane Specialist
entries: From your time working in the Spire, you've become sensitive to even the faintest arcane signatures around you. As a result of this, by focusing you can usually tell what school of magic an item holds, a person has recently been affected by, or a ritual circle emulates without magic. Your DM might state that something was performed in a way so archaic that you can't make heads or tails of it, or that you might require specialized equipment that only the Spire has in order to discover the truth behind the target of your curiosity.

entries.name: Department
entries: To determine which department you were a part of, roll a {@dice d12} or choose from the options in the table below.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: Exiles often hold grudges against the Spire for the nature of their dismissal, but sometimes they couldn't care less. Their characteristics are often reflective of someone who is aloof, but they can also realise that just as they might have been, judging others unfairly without giving them a chance can just lead to bitterness and lost opportunities.

rows: As a victim of cynicism myself, I refuse to subject others to it.
The presence of authority is discomforting to me. I'd much rather be the one in charge.
I still harbor respect for the Spire and those who work there, and can't stand people who look down on it.
I find myself drawn to those who are outcast. Nobody deserves to be alone.
Everything must be a part of something greater, otherwise why would it happen?
If there's one thing I learned from my mentor, it's that rambling about something eventually gets your point across.
I try my hardest to be straight-forward and communicative.
People often think I don't listen, due to how despondent I can be.
Preservation. All knowledge must be preserved for future generations. (Neutral)
Secrecy. Some knowledge must be kept secret for the good of all. (Lawful)
Honesty. All knowledge must be shared, so that all can make their own educated decisions. (Chaotic)
Altruism. Knowledge is only useful if it can benefit the many. The ultimate goal of learning is using that information to make the world a better place. (Good)
Exploitation. Those less knowledgeable than myself can be used for my endeavors. (Evil)
Pursuit. The acquirement of knowledge is the only thing that matters in life. Comfort, happiness, and even my own well-being are secondary. (Neutral)
I was exiled for something out of my control, and I must find the one responsible.
Upon being exiled, I have become obsessed with proving my worth, and showing the world just how wrong they were.
I heard whisperings of a dark rumor I am too terrified to repeat, and now shudder at the dark, unknown recesses of the world.
I've been searching my whole life for the answer to a certain question.
Though we were both exiled, my mentor remained in the Spire. I'll do anything to find out why.
My reason for exile is something that causes me immense guilt, and whether intentionally or subconsciously, I seek to atone in everything I do.
I find it hard to get along with people when I meet them for the first time, due to my bitterness.
It's a bad habit of mine to not inform people of vital information, since I'm used to keeping quiet.
I obey the law, even if it would means someone stays dead that could have been saved.
Seeing someone do something wrong that I can do better infuriates me, and I can't keep quiet.
I overlook obvious solutions in favor of complicated ones.
I'm drawn to forbidden knowledge like a moth to a flame.

items.name: Equipment
items.entry: A {@item riding saddle|phb} that is worn but well-maintained, a set of {@item leatherworker's tools|phb}, {@item saddlebags|phb}, a reminder of your first mount (such as a leather patch, a scale), a set of {@item common clothes|phb}, and a belt {@item pouch|phb} containing 20 sp

entries.name: Feature: Eye for Talent
entries: You've seen a wide variety of mounts from all walks of life. You've bonded with them, rode with them, cared for them, and you can immediately tell where a mount might be deficient or efficient. If you spend at least 10 minutes looking over a creature to gauge its capabilities as a mount, you can determine anything extraordinary about it; a drake with harder scales but a weak heart, a pony with an odd gait but unyielding bravery, and qualities of that nature. You can use this information to gauge the true value of the creature, by both its current prowess and its potential.

entries.name: Suggested Characteristics
entries: A Rider's view on nature and the inherent value of wildlife has been shaped by their experiences. Perhaps they believe that bonds are to be treasured, or that due to the constant coming and going of companions, they're don't want to make lasting friendships. Sometimes they think animals are so much more capable than we give them credit for, while others feel that they're good for little more than meat and transport.

rows: At a gathering, I'm more likely to sit with the animals than to talk with anyone of importance.
I find walking anywhere on foot to be tedious.
There are few things better to me in life than feeling the wind rushing past me.
When I'm mounted, my bravery is unmatched. When I'm dismounted, I'm...less brave, to put it lightly.
I'm rather quick to trust and form bonds, to make up for those I have lost along the way.
Little can sour my optimistic attitude, I always try to see the best in a situation.
I've spent so long talking with creatures that aren't sentient that I have a bad habit of forgetting how to talk to people.
I hate to see anything suffer.
Redemption. All life deserves a second chance. (Good)
Education. Those less knowledgeable should be taught and educated as we have been, where possible. (Lawful)
Liberty. Nothing should be chained or confined; all should be free to live and die as they please. (Lawful)
Ambition. To have a great partner is to have a great goal, and mine will be greater than all. (Any)
Supremacy. Those less intelligent than us are meant to be subservient to us. (Evil)
Wild. The wilderness is more important than all the constructs of civilization. (Chaotic)
I once found a spectacularly talented creature in the wild, and I've searched for it ever since.
My first companion was poached, and I'll never love another like it.
I've been through a portal to another realm, and I want to find it again to search for talent there.
I'll die for those who'll die for me.
The love of an animal is the purest love there is.
If it came down to life or death, I would sacrifice myself for the one who wasn't able to save themselves, as someone once did for me.
I am deeply impulsive, and often follow my whims even when it's inappropriate.
The death of an animal can leave me inconsolable.
Without a companion by my side, I'm easily terrified.
I will not stand anyone that calls a companion a 'pet'.
I can't let go of a creature I can't keep.
I despise poachers. They deserve nothing short of death, and I will hunt them down until achieved.

spell.name: Courageous Rebuttal

time.condition: which you take when a creature makes a melee attack against you

entries: As part of the reaction used to cast this spell, you use a weapon to make a melee weapon attack against the creature that attacked you, after the outcome of their attack is decided. If you hit, the target suffers the weapon's normal damage, and you gain half the damage dealt in temporary hitpoints that last for up to 1 minute.

spell.name: Heart Sunder
entries: You cause a melee weapon you are holding to thirst for blood, opening a creature's weaknesses. The first attack you make with that weapon on this turn adds {@dice 1d6} to the attack roll. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects and the next weapon damage rolled against the target is automatically maximized.

spell.name: Lesser Bloodsight
entries: Your eyes take on a crimson hue. For the duration, you can see the life force of nearby creatures within the spell's range. When a creature is at full health, they appear perfectly normal, but as they become more wounded a red aura begins to surround them.

You can notice when a creature's current hitpoints are below their hitpoint maximum and half their hitpoint maximum. When a creature falls below their maximum, they glow red, and can be seen behind materials of 3 inches thick or less. Once a creature is below half of their maximum, you can see them even through walls.

spell.name: Withering Necrosis
entries: You extend your hand and attempt to vastly decrease the health of a creature you grasp. Make a melee spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 5d6} necrotic damage at the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d6} damage for each spell slot above 1st.

spell.name: Needle and Thread
entries: Select a target with blood within range, who must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, it takes {@damage 3d8} piercing damage and its blood explodes in three sharpened needles from its body to as many creatures or objects of your choice as there are needles, each target of which must be within 30 feet of the original. A secondary target must make the same save, taking {@damage 1d8} piercing damage per needle on a failure, or none on a success. If the first target succeeds on the saving throw, the first target only takes half as much piercing damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the first target takes {@damage 1d8} additional damage and releases one more needle for each spell slot above 2nd.

spell.name: Polyphemus' Might
entries: You imbue one creature of your choice within range a shadow of giant's might. The target gains a number of {@dice d4}s equal to your level in temporary hitpoints, and while those hitpoints last, they have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}, and roll damage dice twice for melee attacks, choosing the higher result.

spell.name: Redwood Expansion
entries: For the duration, you cause a creature of your choice to undergo a spontaneous expansion of sinews, bones, muscle, organs; their entire body. If the target is unwilling, it can make a Constitution throw to be unaffected on a success. Everything the creature is wearing and carrying changes size with it. Any item dropped by an affected creature returns to normal size at once.

The target's size quadruples in all dimensions and its weight is multiplied by thirty-two. This growth increases its size by two categories—from Medium to Huge, for example. If there isn't enough room for the target to double its size, the creature attains the maximum possible size in the space available. Until the spell ends, the target also has advantage on Strength checks and Strength saving throws, and can reroll one of the dice once. The target's weapons also grow to match its new size. While these weapons are enlarged, the target's attacks with them add your spellcasting modifier as extra damage.

spell.name: Aura of Decay
entries: A diseased aura radiates from you within range. Until the spell ends, the aura moves with you, centered on you. Each hostile creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn within it has disadvantage on death saving throws and must make a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 5d6} necrotic damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the spell's radius increases by 15 feet for each spell slot above 4th.

spell.name: Quicken Atavism
entries: You perform a rite of blood on a young person. Choose a humanoid that is a number of years old or less equal to your spellcasting modifier. You imbue them with an atavistic spark, granting them a chance at developing atavism, which takes a full month to mature. Roll percentile dice. On a result of your level or lower, the child's Constitution score raises to 15 if it were lower and they become a 1st-level atavist after the maturation time.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the humanoid's maximum age increases by 5 years and the casting time halves for each spell slot above 4th.

spell.name: Exoscleroma
entries: You touch a creature who must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are cursed, and their bones slowly begin to dissolve and regrow under their skin. This spell has no effect against creatures who are immune to poison damage or the {@condition poisoned} condition. After they finish their first long rest, they become {@condition poisoned}. After the second, {@condition deafened}; after the third, {@condition restrained}; fourth, {@condition incapacitated}; fifth, {@condition paralyzed}; sixth, {@condition petrified}.

To be cured a creature must be healed by a healing spell or any restoration spell of the same level of day progression they are on or higher. Once three days have passed for a {@condition petrified} creature under the effect of the spell, only 9th level magic can cure them.

spell.name: Exsanguinate
entries: Choose a creature within range that has blood. They must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, half of their blood falls through their skin to the ground in a puddle of difficult terrain in a 10-foot-radius around them, they take {@damage 10d10} necrotic damage, and they become {@condition incapacitated} until the end of their next turn.

spell.name: Haemorrhaging Rupture
entries: You momentarily rip apart blood vessels of a creature you choose within range, who must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, for every 5 feet of movement they make for the duration to a maximum of 60, they lose {@dice 2d12} hitpoints due to blood loss or half as much on a success as their blood pours through their skin and out of their body, as if they were a cup overflowing with water. They take this damage even if they were moved against their will. At the end of each of its turns, the target can make another Constitution saving throw. For any success, the spell ends.

spell.name: Eternal Blood
entries: You touch a creature and grant it a momentary dominion over death. If a creature dies while under the effect of this spell, they are revived after 1 minute from their remains, or, if none remain, their place of death.

spell.name: Greater Withering
entries: A creature you touch must make a Constitution saving throw or be afflicted with a living greater withering curse. On a failed save, immediately and at the end of each long rest that they take, they lose 1 point from their Strength and Constitution scores. If either fall below 4, they can't move, and if either fall below 1, they die.
This curse can only be removed by a spell of 7th level or higher, such as a 7th level {@spell remove curse} or {@spell regenerate}.

spell.name: Marrowmelt
entries: You snap your fingers and attempt to cause the spine of a creature with one within range to break in half and then melt into marrow. The target chosen must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failure, they are permanently {@condition paralyzed}.
This paralysis can only be cured by a {@spell regenerate} spell of 9th level.

spell.name: Killing Joke

components.m: a blood soaked feather that is waved in the air

entries: Target a creature you can see within range. If that creature can hear you it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition incapacitated} and fall {@condition prone}, unable to stop laughing.

At the start of an affected creature's turns, it can repeat the saving throw. On success, it is no longer {@condition incapacitated} until the start of its next turn. If a creature succeeds this saving throw three times, the spell ends. If it fails this saving throw three times, the creature becomes unable to breathe and begins to suffocate (see rules for Suffocation) and its speed is reduced to 0 until the spell ends. Once a creature begins to suffocate, it no longer makes saving throws at the start of its turn.

spell.name: Finale
entries: The time has come for your show to enter its final act. You cast this spell, accepting your end, and go out with a bang.

You can cast any number of spells you know of 8th level or lower up to a total of 18 levels of spells, so long as they have a casting time of 1 action or 1 bonus action. You cannot cast the same spell twice, and you must provide the materials as the spell requires them, but you do not require the spell slots available to cast them.

After the effects of this spell transpire, you explode into confetti and die. This death cannot be prevented by any means, and you cannot be brought to life by any means short of the {@spell true resurrection} or {@spell wish} spell.

spell.name: Aftercare

components.m: a healer's kit, of which one use is consumed

entries: This spell is often used by healers working in areas of conflict and strife. By expending one use of a healer's kit, you can perform one of the following effects on a target within range:

items: You cleanse all visible wounds on the target.
You cause a needle and thread to suture a wound closed with care and precision for 1 minute.
You cause any of the target's broken or fractured bones to slowly set in place for 1 minute, causing them as little pain and discomfort as possible.

You cause a roll of bandages to unfurl and begin wrapping around the target's wounds for 1 minute where you desire, or until you end the effect. When you end the effect, the bandages secure themselves as you wish, and then sever from the roll.

entries: You can also cast the spell with a casting time of 10 minutes, targeting a creature that is stable and at 0 hit points. The target must be within range of the spell for the duration of the casting time. When you finish casting the spell, the target regains 1 hit point.

spell.name: Stonefist
entries: You channel primal earth magic into your fist, hardening it into stone to deliver a powerful blow. Make a melee spell attack against one target within 5 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes {@dice 1d8} bludgeoning damage and is pushed up to 10 feet away from you. After you make the attack, your fist returns to normal.
The spell's damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach 5th level ({@dice 2d8}), 11th level ({@dice 3d8}), and 17th level ({@dice 4d8}).

spell.name: Guided Offensive
entries: You place a guiding essence in up to three creatures of your choice within range. For the duration, a target's attack rolls can't have disadvantage, and their attacks ignore half cover and three-quarters cover.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 1st.

spell.name: Crashing Wave
entries: The ocean's waves bend to your will, surrounding your weaponry with the force of the waves and crashing down.

The next time you hit a creature with a weapon attack before this spell ends, the weapon is covered in a wave of water as you strike, dealing an extra {@dice 2d6} bludgeoning damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a Strength saving throw or be pushed 30 feet away from you.

spell.name: Swim Through Space
entries: Neither ground or air, sand or earth, or anything else can impede you. Until the end of your turn, you gain a fly speed and a burrow speed of 60 feet.

spell.name: Arrow Catcher
entries: You create a shield visible to you and your allies that hovers idly over the target's head until the spell ends. While this shield is active, nonmagical ranged attacks against the target have disadvantage.

spell.name: Peaceful Walk
entries: You extend an enchanting aura of calm in a 30-foot radius sphere centered on yourself, forcing everyone to walk with measured steps. A creature other than yourself that starts its turn in the sphere must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have its speed halved until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, you can choose one creature to not be affected for each slot level above 1st.

spell.name: Weapon of Choice
entries: You imbue a weapon you're holding with a specific signature, empowering it. Until the spell ends, you are considered proficient with the weapon, and you gain a +1 bonus to weapon damage rolls made with it. The spell ends early if you let go of the weapon.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the bonus increases to +2. When you use a spell slot of 5th level or higher, the bonus increases to +3.

spell.name: Dusk Pulse
entries: An inky blackness ripples upon the ground. All hostile creatures within a 30-foot radius centred on you must succeed a Constitution saving throw or take {@dice 2d8} necrotic damage. A creature that succeeds takes half as much damage. A creature that failed the saving throw takes damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier at the start of their next turn, and sees as though in darkness. A creature that is {@condition blinded} in this way treats all terrain within the area as difficult terrain.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, both the initial and secondary instances of damage increase by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Old Town Road
entries: Every creature other than you within range must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be teleported to the space where they began their previous turn. A creature can choose to fail this saving throw.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the range of the spell increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Scholar's Passion
entries: This spell is used by scholars and scribes to assist them in their work and to care for their books. You create one of the following effects:

items: This spell creates a perfect duplicate of any written or drawn document that you touch onto blank pages you supply.
You can copy up to 20 pages of text with one casting of this spell. Magical writing, including spellbook pages, can't be copied with this spell.

This spell copies and translates any text that you touch onto blank pages you supply. You can copy up to 20 pages of text with one casting of this spell. This spell can copy the text into any language the caster is capable of understanding.

With this spell you can touch a book or scroll and absorb the knowledge contained within as if you had just read it. This is equivalent to a solid reading but not deep study — the caster does not gain perfect recall of the information, just whatever they would have gotten from reading it completely once. If you can't read the language of the source, the spell has no effect. This spell cannot be used to prepare spells or to cast magical scrolls, nor does it have any effect when reading a magical book (such as a tome of understanding).

This spell will completely repair and clean a mundane book or scroll given that greater than half of the original is still available. It will even restore missing or faded pages. The spell has no effect on a magic book, spell scrolls or spell books.
This spell will animate a book giving it a mouth and voice to read its pages aloud. The book will speak until finished or told to be quiet.

spell.name: Cold Snap
entries: A chilly frost saps the heat from your foes around a point you choose within range. Each creature within a 5-foot-radius sphere of that point must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@dice 6d6} cold damage and has their movement speed halved until the end of their next turn. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage, and their movement speed isn't halved.
If a creature took fire damage since the end of your last turn, they take an additional {@dice 3d6} cold damage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Deny Form

time.condition: which you can take when you see a creature begin to change its shape through a spell or effect

entries: You let forth a refutation at a shapechanging target you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw. On a failure, the creature takes {@dice 6d6} force damage, its form is reverted, and it cannot change shape again until the start of its next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Faerie's Embrace
entries: Choose a creature you can see within range. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or become embraced by blue, green, or violet light (your choice) that takes a shape of your choosing. For the duration, the affected creature sheds dim light in a 10-foot radius. Any attack roll against the target has advantage if the attacker can see it, and the affected creature can't benefit from being invisible.

When you cast the spell, choose any number of creatures within range. A glimmering trail of light of the same color connects you to the target by the shortest and most direct route (but not necessarily the safest) and is visible only to you and the chosen creatures. A creature that can see invisible creatures and objects can also see this trail. If the target shifts to a different plane of existence, the trail cuts off where they shifted, but will reconnect if they return within the duration.

If you are holding an object of sentimental value belonging to a creature you have met, you can target the creature even if it is hidden as long as it is within 500 feet, and the target has disadvantage on the saving throw.

spell.name: Lightning Spear
entries: A bolt of lightning forms in your hand, as you throw it towards your quarry. Make a ranged spell attack against the target. On a hit, the target takes {@dice 5d10} lightning damage and is knocked {@condition prone} or pushed 20 feet back (your choice).

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Rescue

time.condition: which you take when you see an ally within 120 feet of you drop to 0 hit points

entries: You teleport a willing or {@condition unconscious} creature you can see within range to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you, potentially saving them from dire circumstances. If the creature is dying, they are also stabilized.

spell.name: Shackles of Pain
entries: You send forth ethereal spiked chains to shackle another, making them destined to share your plight. Choose one creature you can see within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. If it fails, the target is linked to you for the duration. While linked in this way, whenever your hit points are reduced, the target's hit points are reduced by the same amount. The target can repeat the saving throw each time it loses hit points in this way, severing the link on a success.
These shackles remain while the target is within 1 mile of you, even if it is in the Ethereal Plane.

spell.name: Army of the Dead

components.m: a black onyx stone worth at least 2,000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: And so came the day that the dead would walk the earth, under your command, if only briefly. You mutter an unholy incantation that crushes the black onyx stone in your hand and scatters its dust in a 300-foot radius around you.

You can cast this spell only at night. You can create 200 hit points worth of undead at points you choose within range. These undead can be {@creature ghoul||ghouls}, {@creature ghast||ghasts}, {@creature mummy||mummies}, {@creature skeleton||skeletons}, {@creature wight||wights}, or {@creature zombie||zombies}. Each undead has average hit points, and it uses your proficiency bonus in place of its own. If you can see a corpse within range, you can create an undead from the corpse, reducing the number of hit points needed by 10 for that undead. You can't have more undead than your level.

As a bonus action on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 300 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any or all of them at the same time, issuing the same command to each one). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move during its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular chamber or corridor. If you issue no commands, the creature only defends itself against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow it until its task is complete.
The creatures are under your control until the spell ends, after which they become inanimate once more.

spell.name: Virulent Scourge
entries: You weave together a serpentine whip that uncoils from your hand, slowly weaving in place in wait for your command. This serpentine whip lasts for the duration, and if you let go of the whip, it disappears, but you can evoke the whip again as a bonus action.

You can use your action to make a melee spell attack with the serpentine whip against a creature you can see within 10 feet of you. On a hit, the target takes poison damage equal to {@dice 2d12} + your spellcasting ability modifier on a hit, and you can cause the target to make a Constitution saving throw or be poisoned until the end of their next turn.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a 4th- or 5th-level spell slot, the damage increases to {@dice 3d12}. When you cast it using a 6th- or 7th- level spell slot, the damage increases to {@dice 4d12}. When you cast it using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the damage increases to {@dice 5d12}.

spell.name: Bulwark

time.condition: which you take when you or an ally within 5 feet of you are hit by an attack or targeted by the {@spell magic missile} spell

entries: A large shield of magical force appears and protects you and your allies. Until the start of your next turn, you and up to two allies of your choice within 5 feet of you have a +6 bonus to AC - including against the triggering attack - a +2 bonus to Dexterity saving throws, and you take no damage from the magic missile spell. If an ally benefiting from this spell moves 5 feet away from you, you can choose a new ally (no action required by you). This does not stack with the {@spell shield} spell.

spell.name: Healing Mist
entries: A 15-foot radius sphere of soft golden mist appears centered on a point of your choice within range. A creature within the sphere regains {@dice 1d4} hit points at the start of each of its turns. The sphere lasts for the duration or until a wind of moderate or greater speed (at least 10 miles per hour) disperses it.

spell.name: Dream Eater
entries: When you cast this spell, choose one creature within range. If that creature is conscious, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or fall {@condition unconscious}, and its hit point maximum is reduced by {@dice 1d10}. You regain hit points equal to the result. If the creature is {@condition unconscious}, there is no saving throw. While this spell is active, as a bonus action on each of your turns you can reduce the target's hit point maximum by {@dice 1d10} and heal yourself for the result. This spell automatically ends if the creature takes damage from a source other than this spell.

spell.name: Hammer Crush

time.condition: which you take when a creature provokes an opportunity attack

entries: You retaliate to the movement of someone attempting to leave your reach with a crushing blow. As part of the reaction used to cast this spell, you must make a melee attack with a bludgeoning weapon against the creature that provoked the opportunity attack within the spell's range, otherwise the spell fails. On a hit, the target suffers the attack's normal effects, takes an additional {@dice 6d8} bludgeoning damage, is knocked {@condition prone}, and has their movement speed reduced to 0 until the start of your next turn.

spell.name: Rancid Expulsion

components.m: a vial of acid worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You empty the contents of the vial into your mouth, the magic multiplying its volume explosively until it is expelled in a 60-foot cone of your choice. All creatures within the area must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 15d4} acid damage. A creature takes half as much damage on a success. For the duration, nonmagical objects and structures within the area are melted by the acid, and creatures that end their turn in the acid, or enter the area for the first time on a turn, take {@dice 4d4} acid damage. If damage from this spell leaves a target with 0 hit points, the target's body is dissolved.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each slot level above 6th.

spell.name: Melthia's Spontaneous Arrival
entries: You teleport yourself and all willing creatures of your choice within range to the upper atmosphere of the plane you currently reside on.

spell.name: Terra Guardian
entries: The ground quakes, erupting spikes in a protective 30-foot radius, 15-foot high cylinder around you. All hostile creatures must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 6d4} piercing damage and be pushed beyond the radius. The erupted earth forms 15-foot tall obstacles at the end of the radius in the form of stalagmites, curved teeth akin to an earthen maw, or sharp boulders. Each 5-foot square of obstacles have an AC of 15, and 30 hit points.
While this spell is active, you can repeat the action on yourturn to change the radius and force hostile creatures to repeat the saving throw. Doing so restores the hit points of each obstacle.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 7th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 2d4} for each slot level above 6th.

spell.name: Execution Block
entries: A colossal guillotine falls upon a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 12d10} slashing damage. If the creature is {@condition prone}, they make this saving throw with disadvantage. If this leaves the target with 0 hit points, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.

spell.name: Thunder Reign
entries: A vibration hums around hostile creatures you can see within 30 feet of you. Hostile creatures must succeed on a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the vibration grows in pitch for that creature for the duration, and if a target willingly moves (except to stand from {@condition prone}), it immediately takes {@dice 5d8} thunder damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. A creature can only take damage from this spell once per turn. A creature can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the initial damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 7th.

spell.name: Devouring Maw
entries: You call forth a calamitous serpent's likeness to a point you can see within range, causing a humongous maw to snap shut over it in a 100-foot radius sphere.

Creatures within the radius must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 8d10} force damage. A creature takes half as much damage on a success, and an object or structure takes the maximum damage. A creature is consumed if the damage from failing the saving throw leaves it with 0 hit points, as is a structure or object.

A consumed creature and everything it is wearing or carrying, except magic items, are consumed entirely, leaving behind no trace of its existence. The creature can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a wish spell.

spell.name: Inferno Aura
entries: Wild flames of a vast inferno swirl erratically around you in a 30-foot radius. For the duration, all hostile creatures within range lose resistance to fire damage, and any time damage dice would be rolled to deal fire damage to a hostile creature, it takes maximum damage, instead of rolling.

spell.name: End of the World

components.m: everything you're carrying aside from your clothes

entries: Light, life, love, all are reduced to nothing. A void appears in a point you can see, and each creature in a 120-foot radius sphere centered on that point is teleported to a point of your choice within 15 feet of the center of the sphere. All creatures of CR 5 or lower that are teleported are instantly slain, and do not occupy a space.

Afterward, each creature in a 30-foot radius sphere centered on that point take {@dice 20d10} force damage, and are {@condition blinded} and have their movement speed reduced to 0 until the end of their next turn.

spell.name: Grim Harvest
entries: The scythe of the reaper appears behind you, prepared to sacrifice your life to harm your foe.

You take {@dice 15d8} necrotic damage, and one creature of your choice that you can see within range takes damage equal to double the damage you took. You can choose to let the damage you take overcome any damage resistances or immunities you might have.

spell.name: Toward Earth
entries: You touch a creature. The first time the target takes falling damage, it gains immunity to the damage, and the spell ends.

spell.name: Tracker's Mark

components.m: a compass worth at least 5 gp

entries: You touch an object that isn't being worn or carried that you can see within range and place a mystical mark on it, invisible to creatures other than you. Until the spell ends, the compass points in the direction of the object, or spins wildly if the object isn't on the same plane of existence as you. The spell ends early if the object is destroyed.

spell.name: Lightning Helix
entries: You launch a twisted stroke of lightning at a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 2d8} lightning damage and make the saving throw again. If the target fails the Dexterity saving throw three times, the effect ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Tombstone
entries: You conjure a tombstone above a creature you can see within range, which crashes down on them. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@dice 4d6} bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. On a successful save, the target takes half damage, but suffers no other effect.
If you target an undead creature, it makes the saving throw with disadvantage.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each slot level above 2nd.

spell.name: Bird's Eye
entries: You create an {@condition invisible}, magical eye within range that hovers in the air above you and moves with you for the duration.
You mentally receive visual information from the eye, which has normal vision and can look in every direction.

As an action, you can change the eye's altitude by up to 120 feet in either direction. A solid barrier blocks the eye's movement, and if you're underground or indoors, the eye moves to within 5 feet of you and closes until you aren't.

spell.name: Caustic Swamp
entries: A swamp covers the ground in a 20-foot radius sphere centered on a point within range and turns it into difficult terrain. A creature that enters the area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there takes {@dice 4d4} acid damage. If this damage leaves a target with 0 hit points, the target's body is dissolved. Any organic object in the swamp that isn't being worn or carried, such as a corpse or food, is also dissolved.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the radius of the swamp increases by 10 feet for each slot level above 3rd.

spell.name: Edge of Winter
entries: You create a massive blade made of frost and swing it in a 15-foot cone up to two times. Each time you swing the blade, you make a melee spell attack against each creature of your choice in the area, dealing {@dice 3d8} cold damage on a hit.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can swing the blade an additional time for every two slot levels above 4th.

spell.name: Lightning Vortex
entries: You launch a wicked stroke of lightning at a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@dice 5d8} lightning damage and make the saving throw again with disadvantage. If the target fails the Dexterity saving throw three times, the effect ends.

entriesHigherLevel.name: At Higher Levels
entries: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each slot level above 5th.

spell.name: Staff of Death
entries: You summon a staff with the appearance of a human spine. You can use the staff as a spellcasting focus.
