While holding the staff, you can use your action to hurl three skull shaped motes of darkness at targets within 120 feet of you. You can hurl them at one target or several. Make a ranged spell attack for each mote. On a hit, a target takes {@dice 2d10} necrotic damage. When you cast a spell on your turn while holding the staff, you can use your bonus action to hurl a mote of darkness.

spell.name: Toward Heaven

components.m: snow from the peak of a mountain

entries: A circular, horizontal plane of force springs into existence on the ground around you, centered on you. The plane is 15 feet in diameter and 1 inch thick. As an action while you're on the plane, you can cause the plane to move up to 120 feet in a straight line, causing anything on it to move with it. The plane can hold up to 5,000 pounds. If more weight is placed on the plane, the spell ends, and everything on it falls to the ground.

spell.name: Reverse Undeath

components.m: diamond dust and black onyx dust worth a total of 1,000 gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You touch the corpse of an undead creature, or an undead creature that isn't incorporeal, that wasn't originally undead. The process by which it became undead is reversed, restoring it to its non-undead form. If the target isn't a corpse, if it was transformed into an undead while it was alive, the creature is alive in its non-undead form after the spell is complete. Otherwise, they become a corpse of their non-undead form.

If the creature is unwilling, it must succeed on a Charisma saving throw to resist the spell's effect. If the undead is under the direct control of another creature, you automatically undo the control as part of the spell.

Your DM might rule that certain undead don't have a will of their own to be willing for the spell, such as a {@creature zombie}, a {@creature ghast}, or a {@creature mummy}. In such a case, the creature automatically fails the saving throw.

spell.name: Holy Rain

components.m: holy water worth at least 25gp, which the spell consumes

entries: You cause holy water to rain around you in a 60-foot radius. The rain moves with you, centered on you. When you cast this spell, you can choose any number of creatures you can see. When a chosen creature ends its turn within the rain, it regains hit points equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. When a fiend or undead ends its turn within the rain, it takes radiant damage equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.

item.name: Potion of Necrotic Shroud
entries: When you drink this potion, you can detect the presence of undead creatures within 60 feet of you for 10 minutes, but not their exact location. The potion's transparent liquid has a floating, bone knuckle within it, marked by striation marks from a twisted ring. The knuckle always appears to belong to the same race as the viewer.

item.name: Potion of Lesser Dwoemersight
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell detect magic} spell for 1 hour (no concentration required). This potion's liquid is a clear sapphire blue, with twinkling stars visible within it, and a floating autumnal leaf.

item.name: Potion of the Minotaur
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the ability to perfectly recall any path you have traveled for 24 hours, starting from when you drink the potion. You also become immune to the {@spell maze} spell. This potion's liquid looks like the blood of a minotaur, with a few tufts of its hair floating idly within.

item.name: Potion of Radiance
entries: When you drink this potion, you shine as though targeted by the {@spell daylight} spell for 1 hour. This light is sunlight. You can suppress the light as a bonus action until you revert it as a bonus action. While the light isn't suppressed, you detect as a celestial. This potion's liquid is a constantly shining light, and while contained, shines like the {@spell light} spell permanently.

item.name: Polyglot's Potion
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell tongues} spell for 1 hour. This potion's transparent liquid has a silver snake's tongue floating within it. Agitating it makes the tongue wriggle and writhe like a snake.

item.name: Potion of Lesser Masking
entries: When you drink this potion, envision an appearance. You gain the effects of the {@spell alter self} spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required), except you can't change your appearance from the appearance you envisioned. This potion's liquid flows like molten silver, with theatre masks of happiness and joy floating along the surface.

item.name: Potion of Spider Legs
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effects of the {@spell spider climb} spell for 1d4 hours (no concentration required). A spider is curled up within the transparent liquid of this potion, and agitating the liquid makes the spider's form disperse into the water around, slowly becoming whole as the liquid calms.

item.name: Potion of the Winter Dwoemer
entries: This potion only takes effect while in a winter environment, or during the winter months. When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell detect magic} spell for 1 hour (no concentration required), and you can cast the {@spell identify} spell as an action on each of your turns to target an object you detect in the radius. This potion's icy liquid is a clear sapphire blue, with twinkling stars visible within it, and a floating autumnal leaf that is frosted at the edges.

item.name: Potion of Oracle's Protection
entries: When you drink this potion, a faint mist appears around you for 8 hours. While concentrating on a divination spell that doesn't deal damage, you can use a bonus action to gain the effect of the gaseous form spell until the end of your next turn (no concentration required). This potion looks like a crystal ball is floating within its misty, semi-opaque liquid.

item.name: Firebone Stimulant
entries: When you inject this concoction into your neck, your head detaches from your body, is lit aflame in ghastly fire, and your body goes limp. For 1 minute, or until reduced to 0 hit points, your game statistics become that of a {@creature flameskull}, except you can cast spells you know using the {@creature flameskull||flameskull's} spell slots in addition to its own, you retain the languages you know, and you use your proficiency bonus in place of the {@creature flameskull||flameskull's} if it would be higher. You still must provide expensive material components if the spell would consume them, and you can only do so while you can see the components and they aren't being worn or carried by another creature. At the end of the duration, your head returns to your body and attaches like it was never severed if it is within 10 feet of the body. If it isn't, you die at the end of the duration.

item.name: Potion of Masterwork
entries: When you drink this potion, choose one set of {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} that you are proficient with. For 24 hours, you can add double your proficiency bonus to any checks you make with those tools. This potion's liquid is clear gold, and bubbles filled with ideas for new items constantly float to the surface.

item.name: Moonblade Oil
entries: This oil sparkles as though bathed in moonlight, regardless of the time of day or one's surroundings. The oil can coat one slashing weapon, and applying the oil takes 1 minute. For 1 hour, the coated item gains the following benefits:

items: It has a +1 bonus to attack and damage rolls, which is doubled while in direct moonlight.
It is considered to be made of moonstone, for the purposes of inflicting vulnerabilities or overcoming resistances and immunities to creatures it would be relevant to.

When you hit a creature with the weapon, roll the weapon's damage dice again. If the original attack would hit creatures within 5 feet of the target, they take the damage rolled as radiant damage, a moonlight arc bursting from the target.

item.name: Pit Fiend's Elixir
entries: When you drink this potion, your skin a brimming red color, horns curling upwards from your head. For 1 hour, you detect as a fiend, and gain the following benefits:

items: Your Strength score increases by 4, to a maximum of 26.
Your current hit points and hit point maximum increase by 20.
You gain a fly speed of 60 feet. If you already have a fly speed, it increases by 30 feet.
You can cast the {@spell fireball} spell as a 6th-level spell (spell save DC 19) twice.
You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

entries: The liquid is heated, smells of brimstone, sulfur, and bubbles like lava.

item.name: Potion of Cloak Absorption
entries: When you drink this potion, a light shimmer covers your form for 24 hours. The first time you would be attacked by a creature that is {@condition invisible}, you teleport to a random unoccupied space within 5 feet of you that isn't behind total cover, causing the attack to miss, and become {@condition invisible} until the end of your next turn, or until you attack or cast a spell. The attacker is then no longer {@condition invisible}. This liquid is entirely transparent, and it doesn't seem like there's anything within the vial at all until it's agitated.

item.name: Potion of Reversal
entries: When you drink this potion, you become the opposite sex for 1 hour. For the duration, you gain a +1 bonus to AC against creatures that are attracted to your new sex. The liquid of the potion swirls blue in the hands of someone feminine, pink in the hands of someone masculine, and both in the hands of someone that is neither.

item.name: Krampus Detector
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell detect evil and good} spell for 10 minutes (no concentration required), except it doesn't detect celestials or fey, nor does it detect if a place or object has been consecrated. As a bonus action, you can surround a creature you detect in this way in a frosty aura. If the creature is {@condition invisible}, it can't benefit from being {@condition invisible} until the start of your next turn. The liquid in the vial looks like it's frozen, even when agitated, despite flowing like normal liquid.

item.name: Thornbaron Stimulant
entries: When you drink or inject this concoction, you regain {@dice 4d10} hit points immediately, and your Strength score is increased by 2, to a maximum of 22, for 8 hours. When this stimulant wears off, your Strength score becomes 10 for 7 days, and your hit point maximum is halved for the same duration. This liquid has rose petals that float on the surface, but just beneath it coils a crown of thorns.

item.name: Potion of Electric Fingers
entries: When you drink this potion, electricity jumps from fingertip to fingertip, and focuses into the form of an instrument you choose for 10 minutes. This instrument coalesces when you bring your hands into the position you would be to play it, and disappears when you let go of it. You are proficient with the instrument, and can add double your proficiency bonus to any ability check you make to play it. Additionally, when you use the {@action Attack} action, you can make ranged spell attacks against creatures within 60 feet of you by playing the instrument. The instrument deals {@dice 1d10} lightning damage on a hit and uses your Charisma modifier for attack and damage rolls. The liquid flows with electricity, and hums as though a concert echoes through it.

item.name: Potion of Surveillance
entries: When you drink this potion, you gain the effect of the {@spell clairvoyance} spell, except the duration is 8 hours, and the location of the invisible sensor can only be within a prison cell or cage. An eyeball bobs in this yellowish liquid, peering out from between bars, but vanishes when the potion is opened.

item.name: Potion of the Webslinger
entries: When you drink this potion, your veins feel as though they are filled with threads. You become capable of slinging webs for 10 minutes. For the duration, when you make a grapple attempt, you can use your Constitution modifier instead of your Strength modifier and you ignore size limitations. Additionally, you can target any creature or object within 60 feet of you, provided the object isn't being worn or carried. If you target a Small or smaller object that isn't being worn or carried, you can immediately pull the object to you. If you target a surface or an object heavier than you, you can immediately pull yourself to the nearest unoccupied space to the object or surface, and remain adhered to this surface. If you successfully grapple a creature, you immediately pull yourself to the nearest unoccupied space within 5 feet of the creature. While the potion isn't agitated, it slowly begins to gain the appearance of a ball of threads inside of the bottle.

item.name: Potion of Divine Wrath
entries: When you drink this potion, you feel your body suffused with divine energy. You gain 3 charges of divine power, which last for 10 minutes. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can expend 1 or more charges to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} radiant damage to the target, plus another {@dice 1d8} per charge expended. The vial appears to have a slowly floating divine blade within the golden liquid that rotates in place.

item.name: Drug of Rent Nature
entries: When you drink this concoction, you gain the Great Tree benefits of the {@spell guardian of nature|XGE} spell for {@dice 1d4 + 1} rounds. When the duration ends, your speed is reduced to 10 feet for 1 minute, and cannot be increased in any way. This drug comes in an opaque bottle, and smells of rotten wood.

subclass.name: Way of the Mad Dog
subclassFeatures: Way of the Mad Dog|Monk||Mad Dog|TheDarkAscension|3

subclass.name: Path of the Branch Reader
subclassFeatures: Path of the Branch Reader|Barbarian||Branch Reader|TheDarkAscension|3
Knowledge of the Great Tree|Barbarian||Branch Reader|TheDarkAscension|14

subclassFeature.name: Bloodied Aspect
entries: Your color of atavist is one who thrives on a wounded physique, gaining adrenaline and power from being closer to death. These atavists tend towards extreme masochism, and are sometimes only truly happy when they can feel cuts, bruises, and blood pumping from their body alarmingly.

subclassFeature.name: Wounded Nature
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this aspect, you gain two benefits as a bloodied aspect warrior, which strengthen as you become weaker, and your innate reflexes take over. Choose one of the following features, which remain active as long as you are below your maximum hitpoints:

entries.name: Alacrity
entries: You hover in and out of a foe's reach, stepping between it as easily as a flick of a blade. You can take the Disengage action as a bonus action.

entries.name: Beating Reflexes
entries: Your agility and bulk are unparalled. You can rend to Strength and Dexterity ability checks.

entries.name: Control
entries: You finely manipulate your blood. When you rend, you can reroll the rend dice once per turn and choose either result.

In addition, choose one of the following features. When you fall below half your hitpoint maximum (rounded up), your chosen feature activates for you, remaining active as long as you are under that threshold:

entries.name: Serrated Strike
entries: Your blade's edge coats and sharpens in spilled blood. When you roll the maximum number on a damage die or rend die, you can add an additional {@dice 1d8} to the result. If you roll an 8 on the additional {@dice 1d8}, you can add an additional {@dice 1d8} to the result, and so on.

entries.name: Thickening Humors
entries: Your spells are overloaded as your bones crack and disjoint under the heavy hand of atavism. You can add your Constitution modifier to the damage you deal with any atavist spell.

entries.name: Unceasing Flow
entries: Your blood runs like water. When you rend yourself and add the result to a weapon damage roll, the result is doubled—you both take and apply double the rend result.

subclassFeature.name: Maiming Aura
entries: At 6th level, you and friendly creatures within 10 feet of you add your Constitution modifier to their weapon damage rolls when you are below half your hitpoint maximum.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Deathbound Vitality
entries: At 10th level, you have advantage on one weapon attack roll per turn if you are below half your hitpoint maximum (rounded up).

subclassFeature.name: Rage, Rage Against the Dying
entries: Starting at 18th level, when you are reduced to 0 hitpoints or killed, you can drop to 1 hitpoint instead. If you do, you enter a fury against death for 1 minute. While in a fury against death, your attack rolls, saving throws, and ability checks treat a {@dice d20} roll of 9 or lower as a 10, you can't be hit by attacks except for critical hits, and when you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw. When your fury against death ends, you gain four levels of exhaustion.

subclassFeature.name: Murderous Aspect
entries: Murderous atavists are honed on the hunt: they use their power to deplete and destroy others. They attack strategically weak spots in a creature, taking them down in stride. Their motivations for this are more varied than the monsters and people they kill: money, fame, altruism, addiction, blackmail, divine, profane, insanity, vengeance, thrill seeking, political gain, or any other inscrutable motivations; perhaps some don't have or need a reason.

subclassFeature.name: Fighting Style
entries: At 3rd level, you choose one of the fighting styles below. You can't choose the same fighting style more than once, even if you later get to choose again.

entries.name: Variant: Fighting Maneuvers
entries: With this variant you can replace the fighting style feature with this one. Beginning at 3rd level, you've disciplined raw power into a fighting form. Choose two of the following fighting maneuvers, for which you can't choose the same again.

entries.name: Parry
entries: If you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn and attack with a single melee weapon and no shield, you can use your bonus action to ready a parrying maneuver. You have a bonus to your AC equal to your Strength or Dexterity modifier (your choice) against the first melee attack made against you before the start of your next turn.

entries.name: Power Attack
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn and make a heavy melee weapon attack against a creature with advantage, you can choose to forgo advantage and make a powerful strike. If you hit, you deal additional damage equal to your proficiency bonus.

entries.name: Pushing Attack
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while wielding a shield, you can attempt to drive the target back. If the target is Large or smaller, you can push the target up to 5 feet away from you.

entries.name: Twin Strike
entries: When you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, and have a different light melee weapon in each hand, you can use your bonus action to make a twin strike with both weapons. Make a single attack roll against a creature within reach of both weapons. If either weapon has the finesse property, you can choose to use your Dexterity modifier in place of your Strength modifier for this attack. You do not add your ability modifier to the damage of either weapon.

subclassFeature.name: Lethal Strike
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can rip a creature apart with a killer's instinct, knowing exactly where to strike. Once per turn, you can deal an extra {@dice 1d6} damage to one creature you hit with an attack if you have advantage on the attack roll.

You don't need advantage on the attack roll if there is no other creature hostile to you within 5 feet of the target or if a creature other than you that is hostile to the target is within 5 feet of it. The amount of damage you gain increases as you reach higher levels: it becomes {@dice 2d6} at level 6, {@dice 3d6} at 10th, and {@dice 4d6} at 18th.

subclassFeature.name: Skirmisher
entries: Starting at 6th level, you become highly effective at tracking and eluding enemies. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} and {@skill Survival} skills, and for one of those skills of your choice, you double your proficiency bonus for any ability checks made with that skill. Finally, you can take the {@action Dash}, {@action Hide}, and {@action Search} actions as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Cruorbent Accuracy
entries: At 10th level, you can rend yourself to an attack roll. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Constitution modifier, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.
Additionally, when you rend to an attack roll, you can use your Lethal Strike feature even if you don't meet the prerequisites.

subclassFeature.name: Life for Life, Blood for Blood
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can match a life form's health in a brazen contest of power. When you score a critical hit on a creature, you can trade blood for blood. The creature with less hitpoints remaining falls to 0 hitpoints and the creature with more loses an equal amount.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Demonic Aspect
entries: In certain cultures, manipulation of one's blood for magic can be seen as the influence of hellish forces, outright feared as being caused by a demon. They might be wrong or, occasionally, may be very right. Whether some shard of a demon's soul awakens hemomancy within you, you've made a pact of power, an old fiendish blood god occupies a forgotten line of your family tree, or you've been cursed by a godless soul, you have a demonic aspect about you.

subclassFeature.name: Fiendish Constitution
entries: At 3rd level, your body takes on characteristics of demons. You register as a demon for any features that detect or interact with demons. Additionally, you can't be {@condition poisoned} and have resistance to poison damage. Finally, you can speak, read, and write Abyssal.
If you wish, your body can begin to reflect these changes within you. The smell of brimstone could lightly surround you, your hair could thicken and redden, and your eyes might blacken.

subclassFeature.name: Demon's Pyre
entries: At 3rd level, you can enter or exit a demon's pyre as a bonus action, granting you greater demonic might. If you do, your eyes change to your choice of color and iris shape. While in the pyre, you can't be {@condition frightened}, and each creature other than you within 5 feet of you takes {@dice 1d4} fire damage when you enter the pyre and at the start of each of your turns. This increases to {@dice 2d4} at 6th level, {@dice 3d4} at 10th, and {@dice 4d4} at 18th.

The demon's pyre lasts for up to 1 minute. You can use this feature a number of times equal to half your Constitution modifier (rounded up) and regain all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Hellfire of the Abyss
entries: At 6th level, when you rend while in a demon's pyre, you can change the weapon damage dealt to fire damage. Additionally, any fire damage you deal ignores resistances, and you have resistance to fire damage.

subclassFeature.name: Archdemon's Pyre
entries: At 10th level, when one or more creatures take fire damage from your demon's pyre, you can expend one spell slot to deal additional fire damage to them. The extra damage is {@dice 2d4} for a 1st-level slot, plus {@dice 1d4} for each spell level higher than 1st, to a maximum of {@dice 5d4}. A celestial or fiend creature takes an additional {@dice 2d4} damage.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of a Demon Prince
entries: At 18th level, when you enter a demon's pyre, you can also enter an avatar of a demon prince. If you do so, for the duration of the pyre, you gain the following benefits:

items: You become a Large fiend with wings and a flying speed of 60 feet.
You have advantage on saving throws against spells and other magical effects.

You can create a whip of hellfire as a bonus action or when you enter a demon's pyre. The whip deals fire damage, has a {@dice d12} damage die, and disappears if it leaves your grasp or when your pyre ends.

entries: Once you enter an avatar, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Dread Aspect
entries: They who embody dread are not often seen outside of the holes within which they are born, stewing in darkness and shadow, reaping that which they sow. Some say it signals a coming end when one that practices Atavism awakens to the Dread Aspect, while others believe it is a harbinger of victory, a symbol of death for their enemies. You might have awoken to your power because it was your path to dark and insidious glory, or you might have awoken to it because necessity called for it; a great calamity that you must stall or cause, a terrible foe that you must conjure or defeat. Regardless of your reasons, your powers bring only death, necrosis, and annihilation.

subclassFeature.name: Tenebrous Shroud
entries: Starting at 3rd level, a dark shroud envelops you in a manner you deem fit, like a death knight's armor or a wraith's robe. While you are not wearing any armor or wielding a shield, your Armor Class equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Constitution modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Necrotic Pulse
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can use your blood to summon shadows that burst forth. When you rend to a damage roll, you can deal necrotic damage equal to the amount rolled on the rend damage dice to a creature you can see within 5 feet of the original target.

subclassFeature.name: Heart of Darkness
entries: Starting at 6th level, the blood pumping from your heart ebbs with flowing shadows. Once on each of your turns, when you inflict necrotic damage with an atavist spell or an effect you are concentrating on, you deal additional necrotic damage equal to your Constitution modifier.

Additionally, if you rend to a damage roll against an undead creature, you can attempt to gain control over it if its challenge rating is equal to half your level or lower. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failure, it obeys one command you give it (no action required) until the end of your next turn. It won't obey a command directly harmful to it. If the target is reduced to 0 hit points by your attack, it automatically fails the saving throw and can act as if alive for the duration, but dies if it would lose any hit points.

subclassFeature.name: Floating Death
entries: At 10th level, you can approach as a reaper would, floating with trailing shadows behind you. You gain a flying speed equal to your walking speed, but you can't fly higher than 30 feet above ground using it. While flying, you can cause your feet to disappear or become a cloud of shadows.

Additionally, during your turn you can cause one weapon you are wielding to float free from your hands in your space (no action required by you), moving with you as a grasping, ethereal claw carries it. You can draw this weapon without an object interaction, and a creature can't take the weapon unless you allow it.

subclassFeature.name: Beauty of Annihilation
entries: At 18th level, you can become an incarnation of death, bringing dread to your enemies by making them face annihilation. As an action, you can transform into a {@creature nightwalker|MTF} for 1 minute, or until you lose concentration (as though concentrating on a spell). Your game statistics are replaced by that of the {@creature nightwalker|MTF}, though you don't assume its hit points and Hit Dice. Instead, you gain temporary hit points equal to the {@creature nightwalker|MTF|nightwalker's} average hit points for the duration. You revert to your normal form when you have no more temporary hit points, and these temporary hit points can't be replaced by temporary hit points from another source.

You also retain your alignment, personality, and Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores, and you retain all of your skill and saving throw proficiencies, along with all of your Dread Aspect features. Finally, you can rend to the damage of the {@creature nightwalker|MTF|nightwalker's} Enervating Focus attack.
When you transform, you choose whether your equipment falls to the ground or merges into your new form. You can't use any of your equipment while in this form unless it states as such.
You can't use this feature again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heavenly Aspect
entries: The ancient powers of Atavism are not immune to the influences of the Outer Planes, and occasionally, whether by chance or design, the two combine in a unique way. Heavenly atavists are an example of this, persons touched by the goodly nature of one of the Upper Planes. Infused with celestial power, these self-sure individuals possess the unique ability to tap into their health to aid others, guiding the strikes of their allies and leading them to glorious victory.

subclassFeature.name: Heavenly Presence
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this aspect, you can use your blood to lend your words a celestial cadence and resist planar influence. You can rend to Charisma checks and saving throws.

subclassFeature.name: Angelic Guidance
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can draw upon your lifeforce to enhance your allies, infusing them with holy strength and providence. Once per turn when an ally within 30 feet that you can see makes a damage roll, an ability check, or a Charisma saving throw, you can rend yourself to that roll. You can choose to use this feature after the ally makes their roll, but before the DM determines if the roll was a success or a failure, or before the ally deals their damage.

subclassFeature.name: Shared Burden
entries: At 6th level, your allies can shoulder the stress of rending, lessening the toll it takes on your body, and you can ward them from harm in turn—at a cost. Whenever you use your Angelic Guidance feature, the ally you rend for can choose to take the rend damage instead of you. Additionally, whenever an ally within 30 feet that you can see takes damage, you can use your reaction and rend to reduce the damage by the amount rolled.

subclassFeature.name: Celestial Ascendance
entries: At 10th level, you take on some aspects of an Angel, the divine denizens of the Upper Planes. Once per round, you can deal an extra {@dice 1d8} radiant damage when you hit a creature with a melee or thrown weapon, and you can cast {@spell detect evil and good} at will, without expending a spell slot.

subclassFeature.name: Sound the Horns of Meriton
entries: At 18th level, you can sacrifice tremendous vitality to sound a divine call to arms, summoning a celestial champion to your side. As an action, you can rend yourself by rolling a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your level, causing a Justice Archon to appear in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of you. The Justice Archon has the same statistics as a {@creature Deva|MM} (MM 16), but lacks the Healing Touch action and wields a longsword. The Justice Archon is friendly to you and your companions and will fight for you, acting on your initiative and following your verbal commands. The Justice Archon remains for 10 minutes, or until you act in a manner that offends or displeases it, at which time it returns to the Upper Planes.
Once you've used this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Cruorwrought Aspect
entries: Atavists use their bodies in ways far beyond the normal capacity of any other creatures. Certain atavists follow their blood, gaining physical command of it—shaping and wielding it inside and outside of their bodies, as well as in the bodies of others.

subclassFeature.name: Weapon of Blood
entries: Beginning at 3rd level when you choose this archetype, you can create a weapon of blood to wield. When you take the {@action Attack} action, you can rend to create a weapon of blood in either one of your hands. You can create a melee weapon you are proficient with or coat a weapon you are currently holding in blood. On a hit, the target loses hitpoints equal to the weapon's damage dice + your rend dice + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.
The blood of the weapon falls into a pool after two hits with the weapon or if it leaves your grasp.

subclassFeature.name: Create or Destroy Blood
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can either create or destroy blood as a bonus action. To create, you make up to 10 gallons of blood within 30 feet of you in a space you can see. Alternatively, the blood falls as rain in a 30-foot sphere within range. To destroy, you destroy up to 10 gallons of blood within 30 feet of you in a space you can see. Once you destroy blood, you can only create blood once before needing to destroy it again. Additionally, you can drain one Small or larger corpse of blood within range. If you drain a corpse of blood, you can create a weapon of blood without losing any hitpoints yourself the first time you do so within the next minute.

subclassFeature.name: Hearteater
entries: Starting at 6th level, when you kill a creature with a weapon of blood, you can roll one of its Hit Dice + its Constitution modifier, gaining hitpoints equal to the result.
You can use this feature twice, regaining all expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Rebuttal of Body
entries: At 10th level, you can refute many attacks on your body. When you are subjected to an effect that allows you to make a Constitution saving throw to take only half damage, you instead take no damage if you succeed on the saving throw, and only half damage if you fail.

subclassFeature.name: Call Godslayer
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you are capable of creating a blood weapon that rivals the most powerful weapons in the multiverse. By creating a weapon of blood, you can create an artifact of blood instead. You rend for {@dice 20d6}, instead of the normal amount. It looks how you wish. After three hits with the artifact of blood, it falls apart.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Anomalous Aspect
entries: You are an anomaly: an eldritch abomination. Blood magic was the hint at a deeper, older, and alien piece that has become part of you. Likely, your robust physical body was a coveted host for eldritch forces, pairing a consciousness with your mighty vessel. Because of its influence, you pick up strange qualities, and can momentarily release the anomalies within you. You can also pick up weird quirks. You can roll or select on the table below.

entries.caption: Anomalous Quirk
rows: When you wake up, daydream, or lose focus, you hear indecipherable tongues from many voices all around.
The stars occasionally seem as if they're not in the right place. Perhaps not yet.
You talk about the current gods as if they are children.
You have a taste for humanoid brains.
Occasionally, surreal, eldritch words pour from your mouth while you are mid conversation.
You refer to yourself as 'we', and 'us', instead of 'I' and 'me'.

subclassFeature.name: Release the Anomaly
entries: Beginning when you choose this aspect at 3rd level, you can release the eldritch influence within you. As an action, you can reveal the anomaly. Tentacles erupt from your body, your eyes reveal the infinite void, and the might of the old gods fills you. The anomaly stays revealed for 1 minute. While the anomaly is revealed you gain the following benefits:

items: You gain two tentacles that sprout from your body, and four at 10th level. As a bonus action, you can attempt to grapple a number of creatures equal to your tentacles within 15 feet of you. You can grapple a creature of one size bigger than you otherwise could by using two, and a creature of two sizes bigger than you could with four.

As an action, you can squeeze creatures {@condition grappled} by your tentacles. A {@condition grappled} creature must make a Strength saving throw (against your spell save DC) or take {@dice 3d6} bludgeoning damage. You can rend to the damage of the squeezing tentacles, affecting all creatures {@condition grappled} by them.
You gain temporary hitpoints immediately and again at the start of each of your turns equal to your Intelligence modifier.

entries: You have a number of uses of this feature equal to your Intelligence modifier, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Awakened Mind
entries: Also at 3rd level, You can communicate telepathically with any creature you can see within 30 feet of you. You don't need to share a language with the creature for it to understand your telepathic utterances, but the creature must be able to understand at least one language. The creature doesn't gain telepathy itself, and can't respond in kind.

subclassFeature.name: Grasp of the Stars
entries: At 6th level, the damage of your tentacles counts as magical for the purposes of overcoming resistances and immunities. Additionally, you can throw any number of creatures grappled by your tentacles as an action a number of feet equal to your speed in a horizontal direction. A creature must make a Dexterity saving throw or land {@condition prone} and take {@dice 3d6} bludgeoning damage, which you can rend to the damage for all thrown creatures. If you would throw it into another creature, it must also make a Dexterity saving throw or taking the same damage.

subclassFeature.name: Greater Anomaly
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can grapple creatures with your tentacles up to 30 feet away from you, and you can move creatures {@condition grappled} by your tentacles up to 30 feet per turn, not including dragging them with you if you move.

subclassFeature.name: To The Old Ones
entries: Beginning at 18th level, you can devour the soul of a creature that dies while {@condition grappled} by at least one of your tentacles. If you do so, for the duration of the current anomaly, you gain one additional tentacle, you can change your face into the creature's or back to your previous as an action, and you know everything that creature knew in life.

subclassFeature.name: College of Villainy
entries: Bards of the College of Villainy are monstrous souls, taking joy in whatever insults and misery they can force unto their foes. Wearing a smile in the face of danger and a wicked grin in the face of certain death, they can often be found within dark alleys, outlined as a scarlet shade wielding a blade dripping with crimson ichor. Other times these villains are the victims, laying beaten upon the ground with harmonies of dreadful joy echoing from their hoarse throats. These twisted performers turn infamy into an art form; after all, no matter how many times a villain might die, they are never forgotten.

entries.name: Villainous Guile
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you've learned to become a true nuisance to your enemies. You gain proficiency with martial weapons. Additionally, while you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

entries.name: Insult the Injury
entries: Also at 3rd level, you've learned to laugh in the face of pain, wounding your enemies' psyche. When you take damage from a hostile creature you can see within 10 feet of you that can hear you, you can expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration as a reaction. The target takes psychic damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1), and can't take reactions until the start of its next turn.
At 5th, 10th, and 15th level in this class, this damage increases by an additional {@dice 1d6}.

subclassFeature.name: Twist the Knife
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can cut through to a creature's mind with your weapon. You learn the {@spell vicious mockery} cantrip. If you already know this cantrip, you learn a different {@filter bard cantrip|spells|class=bard|level=0|source=phb} of your choice. This cantrip doesn't count against your number of cantrips known.

When you hit a creature with a weapon attack as part of the Attack action, you can use your bonus action to cast {@spell vicious mockery} against the creature, adding your Charisma modifier to the damage roll. If the target succeeds its saving throw, it takes half as much damage from {@spell vicious mockery}.

subclassFeature.name: Petty Inspiration
entries: Starting at 14th level, your laugh is so potent and loud that such pettiness inspires your allies. The range of your Insult the Injury feature increases to 60 feet. Additionally, when you deal damage to a creature using Insult the Injury, you can immediately grant a Bardic Inspiration die to an ally you can see without expending a use of the feature. The ally can only use the die on rolls against or caused by the creature.

subclassFeature.name: College of Desire
entries: Bards of the College of Desire are peerless in a particular art, and that art is infatuation. With a pluck of a string, they can cause the heart to stir and bubble with complex emotion, whether through music or with their enchanted arrows. While a benevolent member of the College of Desire would seek to lighten the load of a heavy heart if only for a moment, many of them take advantage of their ability to cause chaos. Nothing is easier to control than the heart, or so they say.

entries.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with the {@item longbow|phb}, the {@item lyre|phb}, and the {@skill Persuasion} skill.
Additionally, you can use a {@item shortbow|phb} or a {@item longbow|phb} as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.

entries.name: Cherubic Arrow
entries: At 3rd level, you bolster a heart from a distance. While you're wielding a {@item shortbow|phb} or {@item longbow|phb}, you can use a bonus action and expend one use of your Bardic Inspiration to choose a creature you can see other than yourself within range of your weapon. You shoot a harmless magical arrow at the creature, and it gains temporary hit points equal to the number you roll on the Bardic Inspiration die + your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). The next spell you cast before the end of your turn can ignore range if it isn't a harmful spell, doesn't have a range of self, and targets only that creature.

subclassFeature.name: Cherubic Charm
entries: At 6th level, your enemies often forget they're your enemies. Once per turn, when you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using a {@item shortbow|phb} or {@item longbow|phb}, you can attempt to charm it. It must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw against your spell save DC or be {@condition charmed} by you until the end of your next turn. If the creature you hit isn't hostile to you, you can instead cause the attack to deal no damage and for the arrow to vanish on contact. If you do so, the creature must instead make a Wisdom saving throw with disadvantage against your spell save DC. On a failed save, the creature forgets that it was shot, is {@condition charmed} by you for 1 hour or until you or your companions do anything harmful to it, and can't be {@condition charmed} in this way again for 24 hours. While a creature is {@condition charmed} by this feature, it is also {@condition charmed} by any creature that has temporary hit points from your Cherubic Arrow.

subclassFeature.name: Amorous Atmosphere
entries: At 14th level, each and every heart flutters at your presence. As an action, you can exude an aura of adoration within 30 feet of you. For 1 minute, when a creature enters the aura for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be {@condition charmed} by you until the start of its next turn. While a creature is {@condition charmed} by this feature, it is also {@condition charmed} by any creature that has temporary hit points from your Cherubic Arrow.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Armiger
entries: Those who earn the right to call themselves an Armiger are few and far between. Within their hands is a magic passed down from person to person, rumored to have been created by a lost line of royalty. Compared to an Eldritch Knight and their spellcasting, an Armiger uses their magic for the sole purpose of wielding their vast and varied array of weaponry.

entries.name: Arcane Armory
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your weaponry lies at your beck and call. As an action, you can add a melee weapon that you're holding to your Arcane Armory. You can have a number of weapons in your Arcane Armory up to twice your Intelligence modifier (minimum of 2). Your Arcane Armory can't contain two of the same type of weapon, such as two rapiers. You can remove a weapon from your Arcane Armory at any time. While in your Arcane Armory, a weapon gains the following benefits.

items: As a bonus action, you can cause any number of weapons in your Arcane Armory to vanish.

As a bonus action, you can cause any number of weapons you caused to vanish to appear and float around you. You can draw a floating weapon without an object interaction, and if you drop it within 5 feet of you, it floats once more. A creature can't take a floating weapon unless you allow it.

You can make a ranged weapon attack as if the weapon had the Thrown (range 20/60) property. If the weapon already has the Thrown property, attacking at long range with it doesn't impose disadvantage on your attack roll.

entries.name: Warp Strike
entries: At 3rd level, you call your weaponry to yourself and vice versa. When you hit a creature with a ranged weapon attack using a weapon in your Arcane Armory, you can teleport to an unoccupied space within 5 feet of that creature. If you do so, you can retrieve the weapon and immediately make a melee weapon attack using the weapon against the creature you hit. You can use this feature only once on each of your turns.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Intelligence modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Heraldic Weaponry
entries: Starting at 7th level, weaponry you craft can't be mistaken. You gain proficiency in smith's tools. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses them.
You can craft a non-magical melee weapon within one day, regardless of the weapon's market value, and you can leave a signature on the weapon which can't be perfectly forged.
Additionally, the weapons in your Arcane Armory count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.

subclassFeature.name: Flexible Fighting Style
entries: Starting at 10th level, you aren't held back by a single style. Once on each of your turns, you can replace the option you chose for your Fighting Style feature with another option.

subclassFeature.name: Army of One
entries: Starting at 15th level, when you hit a creature with three weapon attacks using three different types of weapon on your turn, you deal extra force damage equal to your Intelligence modifier x 3 (minimum 3). For example, you deal extra damage if you hit with a rapier, shortsword, and dagger.

subclassFeature.name: Royal Armory
entries: Starting at 18th level, you access the power of every Armiger before you, back to the time when it was wielded by royalty. As a bonus action, you can call forth the power of kings. For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: Your movement speed is doubled, and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.
When you hit with a weapon attack using a weapon in your Arcane Armory, you deal maximum damage with the weapon's damage die, instead of rolling.
You can use your Warp Strike without expending a use.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Swiftblade
entries: An unusual breed of combatant, the Swiftblade excels not at mounted combat like the Cavalier, nor even in fighting spirit like the Samurai, but instead in a core value: alacrity. Taught by inhabitants from the Elemental Plane of Air, they often serve in positions where speed is valued: duelists, spies, assassins, and such. Without a shield, their protection is most often attacking with cutting forces from afar, and felling their foes before they know the Swiftblade is present.

entries.name: Swiftest Warrior
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you've undertaken the technique of swift wielding. When you wield a slashing weapon in one hand and nothing else in the other, you are swift. You can draw or stow a slashing weapon as part of making an attack with it, and when you use the {@action Attack} action, you can make one attack with the weapon as a bonus action. You don't add your ability modifier to the damage of the bonus attack.
Additionally, your attacks send cutting forces toward their targets. On your turn, the weapon has a reach of 30 feet.

entries.name: Instruction of Wind
entries: Also at 3rd level, you have learned to speak with the wind, and move as one alongside it. You can speak, read, and write Auran, and you are immune to the effects of strong wind. While in an area of strong wind, moving across difficult terrain costs you no extra movement.

subclassFeature.name: Surging Current
entries: At 7th level, those who witness your bursting movement liken it to the force of a hurricane. When you use your Action Surge feature, you gain the benefits of the {@spell haste} spell until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Without Equal
entries: At 10th level, you can reach greater heights than your peers. You can take the {@action Disengage} or {@action Dash} action as a bonus action on your turn. If you do, you gain the ability to move along vertical surfaces and across liquids on your turn without falling during the move.
Additionally, if you would share your initiative count with another creature when you roll initiative, you can increase your initiative count by 1, and you always win initiative ties.

subclassFeature.name: Embodiment of Alacrity
entries: At 15th level, your movement is little more than a blur for your foes. Your speed is increased by 15 feet, and instead of moving to a location, once per turn you can expend an equivalent amount of movement to teleport to your destination. If you teleport into the air, you fall at the end of your turn if you have nothing to hold you aloft.
Additionally, when you use your bonus action to make an attack with a weapon that makes you swift, you can make a second attack, and you add your ability modifier to the damage of both bonus attacks.

subclassFeature.name: Supersonic Slice
entries: At 18th level, the enemy falls before it even hears you strike them. Once per turn when you hit a creature with a weapon that makes you swift, you can make the attack a critical hit.

Additionally, you can harness your speed into a single, powerful strike. While wielding a weapon that makes you swift, you can choose one nonmagical object or structure within 120 feet of you and send a cutting force toward it as an action. If the target isn't being worn, you cut the target in two, even if your weapon wouldn't be long enough to do so.

subclassFeature.name: Curse Bearer
entries: The foolish who decide to become a Curse Bearer tend to live a short life, their body, mind, and soul wracked by torment in exchange for power: Their decision cannot be called correct. Whether their aim is to protect, to avenge, or to die an early death, none aside from the Curse Bearer themselves know.

entries.name: Cursed Covenant
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you call countless curses upon yourself for the sake of strength. As a bonus action, you can invoke a number of curses. The curses last for 1 minute, or until you end the curses as an action. You can't invoke curses again until the curses end. For each curse invoked, you take {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage, which ignores resistance and immunity.
The effect of the curses depend on the curses you invoke from the options below.

entries.name: Luxuria
entries: You are immune to being {@condition charmed}.

entries.name: Gula
entries: You gain a +1 bonus to your Armor Class.

entries.name: Avaritia
entries: You can remove disadvantage on your weapon attack rolls, but you can't gain advantage on the roll.

entries.name: Acedia
entries: Your speed increases by 10 feet.

entries.name: Ira
entries: You are immune to being {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Invidia
entries: You can remove advantage on weapon attack rolls against you, but disadvantage can't be imposed on the roll.
You can invoke two of these curses at once. You can invoke two additional curses at 7th and 10th level.

subclassFeature.name: Last Fragment
entries: Starting at 7th level, you keep a fragment of yourself safe from harm. When you are reduced to 0 hit points but not killed outright, you can end any invoked curses and drop to 1 hit point instead. You can't use this feature if you don't have any curses invoked, and once you use this feature, you can't invoke curses again for 1 minute.
Additionally, you have advantage on saving throws against curses and can break attunement to cursed items normally.

subclassFeature.name: Meaningless Pride
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can invoke the cruelest curse. If you invoke six curses at once, you can invoke the curse below as part of the same bonus action.

entries.name: Superbia
entries: At the end of each of your turns, you can inflict 7 necrotic damage to a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, which ignores resistance and immunity.

subclassFeature.name: End of the Night
entries: Starting at 15th level, you've lived longer than you should've. If you die except by old age, you return to life with 1 hit point and with any mortal wounds closed in {@dice 1d4} hours, as long as you aren't missing any body parts integral for your survival.
Additionally, you are immune to curses unless you willingly choose to be cursed, including the effects of cursed items.

subclassFeature.name: All the World's Evil
entries: At 18th level, you're closer to a curse than a person. When you're damaged by a creature you can see within 30 feet of you while you have seven curses invoked, you can inflict the same amount of damage as necrotic damage to the creature as a reaction, which ignores resistance and immunity. This damage can be no higher than your fighter level.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Judgement
entries: The Oath of Judgement is made by a paladin with the belief that any crime can be forgiven, but a few of them can only be forgiven by death. Whether they're called an executioner, an inquisitor, or insultingly a butcher, they work within the law and allow the indicted to atone for whatever deed they've done. To imprison a thief or execute a murderer, only the appropriate punishment for the crime committed can be called judgement. To act otherwise is merely vengeance.

entries.name: Tenets of Judgement
entries: The tenets of the Oath of Judgement are nearly identical regardless of where they're sworn or who they're sworn by. As they abide by the law and are empowered by it, they tend to be lawful in alignment, though whether they're good or evil is dependent on the paladin. Their tenets can't be mistaken.

entries.name: Heed the Judge
entries: I will heed the verdict passed down on the guilty by the law, and I will not stray from that verdict.

entries.name: Heed the Jury
entries: The law is made by society and not by an individual, I will not decide the law by my will alone.

entries.name: Be the Executioner
entries: I will punish the guilty as they deserve and allow them to be forgiven for their crime.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Be Just
entries: As an action, choose a creature you can see within 60 feet of you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition prone} and have a speed of 0 until the end of its next turn. A creature that has directly killed a humanoid within the last year has disadvantage on the saving throw.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Be Dead
entries: As an action, make a melee weapon attack. If the target is {@condition incapacitated}, {@condition prone}, or {@condition restrained}, the attack deals maximum damage on a hit.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Judgement
entries: Starting at 7th level, the guilty cannot raise their head in your presence. While you're not {@condition incapacitated}, an aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover. If a hostile creature is {@condition prone} and in the aura, it costs the creature an amount of movement equal to its movement speed to stand.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Hope for Tomorrow
entries: Starting at 15th level, the guilty can have hope for tomorrow, they can be forgiven once they've received their punishment. When you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with an attack or spell, you can choose to knock the creature out instead.

Additionally, you deny hope to those who don't deserve it, only death can redeem them now. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can attempt to execute it. After the attack, the target is reduced to 0 hit points if it has hit points equal to your paladin level or fewer. If it dies, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.

subclassFeature.name: Holy Order
entries: Starting at 20th level, you can become an avatar of absolute judgement. Using your action, you undergo a transformation.
For 1 minute, you gain the following benefits:

items: You can use Be Just as a bonus action, and using Be Just or Be Dead doesn't expend a use of Channel Divinity.
Your movement speed is doubled, and your movement doesn't provoke opportunity attacks.
If you attempt to execute a creature, it dies if the attack reduces its hit points to equal to or less than twice your paladin level.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Oath of Clemency
entries: The Oath of Clemency is one taken by a paladin who often have seen too much conflict, strife, and dissent in their life, and seek a resolution for it all. They are often referred to as peacekeepers, but are at other times revered and welcomed as healers. A paladin that has taken this oath seek to resolve conflict wherever they can find it, to mend the wounds inflicted upon body and soul, to repair the bonds broken and severed between former allies, and to create new bonds where previously existed only animosity.

Many of these paladins find themselves alongside angels - those seen as perfect embodiments of all that is good - to bring peace, while many might find themselves facing angels, for often the perfect servants of good find themselves unable to stand the taint of evil in their existence. Such is the way of clemency; it cannot exist for one if it does not exist for all.

entries.name: Tenets of Clemency
entries: The tenets of the Oath of Clemency differ from one to another based on their perception of peace and the means that must be used to bring it, but the tenets will usually include or emphasize the following:

entries.name: Peace for All
entries: When tensions are high and unnecessary conflict arises, you must bring peace between them, be it through words or action.

entries.name: Mercy for All
entries: When conflict arises, necessary or not, you must show mercy to your foes and understand that like you, they too fight for clemency in these times.

entries.name: Health for All
entries: When the wounds of conflict cut deepest, you must give of yourself and dip into your deepest reserves to cleanse any who need it. Friend or foe, an open wound is one that must be mended.

entries.name: Oath Spells
entries: You gain oath spells at the paladin levels listed.

entries.caption: Oath of Clemency Spells
rows: {@spell protection from evil and good}, {@spell sanctuary}

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Pacify
entries: As an action, choose one creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw, which it can choose to fail. If the target is at 0 hit points, it automatically fails this saving throw, and if it is dying, it is stabilized. On a failure, the target can't take harmful actions for 24 hours. Until the effect ends, any creature who targets the creature under this effect with an attack or a harmful effect must first make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the creature must choose a new target or lose the attack or effect.

The effect ends on a creature if it is harmed by an attack or effect, or if it is targeted with an attack or harmful effect by you or your allies. If a creature targeted by Pacify succeeds its saving throw or the effect ends for it, it can't be affected by Pacify again for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: Channel Divinity: Replenishment
entries: As a bonus action, you can focus your sacred energies to replenish your pool of healing power. You regain a number of points in your Lay on Hands pool equal to your paladin level + your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Aura of Clemency
entries: Starting at 7th level, those within your vicinity are subject to your peaceful will. While you're not {@condition incapacitated}, an aura extends 10 feet from you in every direction, but not through total cover. When you use your action to use Lay on Hands, you can target any number of creatures as part of that action, and can target any creature within the aura. You decide how many points to spend on each individual creature you chose.
At 18th level, the range of this aura increases to 30 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Spirit of Clemency
entries: Starting at 15th level, your desire to resolve conflict and save as many lives as possible empowers your restorative powers. When you cast a paladin spell using a spell slot of 1st level or higher that restores hit points to a creature, it has the following additional effects:

items: You can choose to place the creature under the effects of the {@spell protection from evil and good} spell, or the {@spell sanctuary} spell, until the end of your next turn. If the spell restores hit points to more than one creature, the effect you choose applies to all targets.
If the creature is at 0 hit points, the creature regains additional hit points equal to your paladin level.

subclassFeature.name: Avatar of Peace
entries: At 20th level, as an action, you become a beacon for peace. For the next minute, blessed rain pours down in a 30-foot radius around you. You gain the following benefits:

items: Creatures of your choice that end their turn in the blessed rain regain a number of hit points equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1).

While you are conscious, if a creature within the rain would die from a spell or effect that kills them when reduced to 0 hit points, such as the {@spell disintegrate} spell or a {@creature nightwalker|MTF|nightwalker's} Life Eater trait, the killing effect is nullified. Creatures reduced to 0 hit points in the rain are automatically stabilized, and you can cast the rescue spell as a reaction on them without expending a spell slot.
You can use Pacify as a bonus action, and using Pacify doesn't expend a use of Channel Divinity.

entries: Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The Night Serpent
entries: Dendar, Nidhogg, World Eater, Mother of the Night Parade. Regardless of their title, the primordial Night Serpent has existed since the dawn of time to feed on the dreams and nightmares of mortals and deities alike, taking different forms in myth, legend, and reality. An ever-hungry being, one is taught to fear what might happen when that hunger is sated, for the world itself could be devoured in the blink of an eye.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Night Serpent lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Spiral Serpent
entries: Starting at 1st level, you have a shadow serpent coiled around each of your arms. These shadow serpents count as simple melee weapons with which you are proficient, and don't need to be wielded to use them. They deal bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing damage on a hit, use a {@dice d6} for damage - which increases to a {@dice d8} at 10th level in this class - and you can use each one to grapple. The serpents use Charisma instead of Strength for attack and damage rolls made with them, as well as any Strength (Athletics) checks involving a grapple, including breaking free of one yourself. If you do wield them, these weapons are magical, and have a reach of 15 feet.

Additionally, if you are wielding a shadow serpent, you can use your bonus action to make an attack with it. If you hit, the target takes extra necrotic damage equal to your warlock level and is knocked {@condition prone}. Once you use this bonus action, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Creeping Darkness
entries: Starting at 6th level, the Night Serpent bestows upon you sight through darkness. You gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision from another source, its range increases by 30 feet.

As an action, you can uncoil a shadow serpent from your arm in a space within 5 feet of you. You can't use your Spiral Serpent feature with the hand on that arm while the serpent is uncoiled, and you can only uncoil one serpent per arm. You can see as if you were in the space of the serpent, and you can move the serpent up to 30 feet as a bonus action. When seeing through the space of the serpent, the range of your darkvision is increased by an additional 60 feet. While uncoiled, you can attack with the serpent as though you were in its space, including making an opportunity attack when a creature within 5 feet of your serpent moves at least 5 feet away from it, or use an action to return it to your arm.

subclassFeature.name: Abyssal Stare
entries: Starting at 10th level, you can reach beyond the darkness. You have truesight with a range of 15 feet, and creatures provoke an opportunity attack from you when they enter the reach of your shadow serpent, which can only be made with it. You can also make this opportunity attack with the serpent while it is uncoiled.

subclassFeature.name: Serpent's Maw
entries: Starting at 14th level, the Night Serpent will consume the world. You create a void on a point you can see. Each creature within 60 feet of that point must make a Constitution saving throw. A creature takes {@dice 8d8} force damage and is pulled 30 feet toward the point on a failed save, or half as much damage and is pulled half as far on a successful one. If this damage reduces a creature to 0 hit points, they die and are swallowed by the Night Serpent, rendering them annihilated, and the creature can be restored to life only by means of a true resurrection or a wish spell.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The Night
entries: You've formed a pact with an embodiment of the night, feared despite their gentleness. While every creature under the veil of darkness wishes for respite, night is the time that they are weakest and most afraid. The knowledge you've been granted is for the purpose of calming that fear, allowing the weary to find a moment of rest within the dark.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Night lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Symphony of the Night
entries: Starting at 1st level, you prepare the party for the dark night with your song. You gain proficiency in the {@skill Performance} skill.

Additionally, you can perform a lullaby during a short rest. A number of creatures other than you up to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1) that hear your lullaby can sleep until the end of the rest. A creature that finishes a short rest this way regains 1 spent Hit Die, which it can spend immediately.
Once a creature regains 2 Hit Dice from this feature, it can't benefit from this feature again until it finishes a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Down a Dark Path
entries: Starting at 6th level, night is clear as day. You gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet. If you already have darkvision, its range increases by 30 feet.

As an action, you can reduce the range of your darkvision by 30 feet and cause a creature you touch to gain darkvision out to a range of 60 feet or increase the range of its darkvision by 30 feet. The effect lasts for 1 hour or until you end it (no action required). You can't reduce the range of your darkvision below 0 feet using this feature.

subclassFeature.name: Night's Embrace
entries: Starting at 10th level, you're held closely by the night. When you are damaged by a creature, you can use your reaction to cause magical darkness as with the darkness spell to fill the area within 30 feet of you until the end of your next turn. You and any creature affected by your Down a Dark Path feature can see normally in this darkness. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Starry Night
entries: Starting at 14th level, the countless lights that are scattered across the night sky are on your side. As an action, choose a number of creatures you can see up to twice your Charisma modifier (minimum 2). You and each chosen creature must be under the night sky without any obstruction. If the creature is friendly, it regains {@dice 8d8} hit points from soothing moonlight. If it isn't, the creature must make a Constitution saving throw against your spell save DC. On a failed save, it takes {@dice 8d8} radiant damage and is {@condition blinded} until the end of its next turn from burning starlight. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and isn't {@condition blinded}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Predator
entries: Among barbarians, the Path of the Predator isn't uncommon. Rage is primal ferocity, but they delve beneath even that and become indistinguishable from a beast. With a primal instinct to kill or be killed, many who tread this path tend to die early. Those who are able to survive long enough with their instinct alone reach the apex, they can consider everything else prey.

entries.name: Bloody Beast
entries: Starting when you choose this path at 3rd level, the amount of prey doesn't make a difference, you'll hunt them all down. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack while you're raging, you can attempt to damage another creature with the same attack. Choose another creature within 5 feet of the original target and within your reach. If the original attack roll would hit the second creature, it takes damage equal to the number you roll on the weapon's damage dice. The damage is of the same type dealt by the original attack.

Additionally, you devour your prey before they escape you. When you roll initiative, you can use a reaction to move up to your speed toward a hostile creature that you can see or hear. You must end this move closer to the enemy than you started.

entries.name: Bloody Feast
entries: At 3rd level, you're never without a way to hunt. Your bite is a natural weapon, which you can use to make unarmed strikes. If you hit with them, you deal piercing damage equal to {@dice 1d6} + your Strength modifier, instead of the bludgeoning damage normal for an unarmed strike.
Additionally, your senses have become accustomed to the scent of blood. You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks that rely on smell or taste.

subclassFeature.name: Bestial Stance
entries: Starting at 6th level, you lower yourself to the earth and move with four limbs, the way a beast would move. When you have two free hands, your walking speed increases by 10 feet, and opportunity attacks against you are made with disadvantage.

subclassFeature.name: Rip the Throat
entries: Starting at 10th level, the lives of any that falter before you are forfeit. When you score a critical hit against a creature, you can use your bonus action to make an unarmed strike using your bite against it. On a hit, the attack is a critical hit.

subclassFeature.name: Rule of Nature
entries: At 14th level, your dominance can't be questioned, all are prey in your presence. As an action, you can unleash an immense roar. Each hostile creature within 60 feet of you that can hear you must suceed on a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Strength modifier) or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of your next turn. A creature has a speed of 0 and is {@condition incapacitated} while {@condition frightened} in this way. A creature of CR (or level) 3 or lower automatically fails the saving throw. You can't roar again until you finish a long rest.
Additionally, you've slaughtered countless prey. When you damage a creature of CR (or level) 3 or lower with a melee weapon attack, you can reduce the creature's hit points to 0.

subclassFeature.name: Destroyer
entries: The Destroyers are a special type of combatant often found lurking within the depths, or fighting that which could only be deemed as prey, for the sake of protection, truth, power, or sometimes the thrill of battle itself. Their way of fighting is ruthless, and they seek to end their foes in the quickest way possible, be it by choking, grappling, or ripping them in twain.

entries.name: Flesh Ripper
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your hits cut deeper, and the momentum of battle flows through you like a wave. Throughout combat you gain momentum, to a maximum of 10, which can be used to perform Onslaughts. Momentum is gained and lost in the following ways:

items: You gain 3 momentum when you roll initiative, and at the end of your turn if at least one of your attacks hit.
You lose 2 momentum if you don't attack on your turn.
You lose all momentum when combat ends.

entries.name: Onslaught
entries: At 3rd level, when you take the {@action Attack} action on your turn, momentum can be used for the following Onslaughts when you make an attack or hit, as specified in the Onslaught. Damage dealt with an Onslaught is always of the weapon's type; bludgeoning, piercing, or slashing. You can use multiple Onslaughts on one attack if you can afford them, but you cannot use the same one multiple times:

entries.name: Harpoon Strike
entries: When you would make a ranged weapon attack with a thrown weapon, you can spend 2 momentum to double the normal range and long range at which the thrown weapon can be used. If you hurl at a target that is within the weapon's normal range, you deal an additional {@dice 3d4} damage on a hit, and the target is pushed 5 feet away from you. As part of the attack to throw the weapon, you can draw another melee weapon.

entries.name: Lunging Jaws
entries: If your movement speed isn't 0, you can spend 2 momentum to make a melee weapon attack against a creature up to 10 feet beyond your reach. If you do so, you move until the creature is within reach of your attack as part of the attack towards it, and add {@dice 1d6} to the damage roll.

entries.name: Mutilate
entries: When you hit with a melee weapon attack against a hostile creature, you can spend 4 momentum to inflict an additional {@dice 3d6} damage and reduce the target's speed by 15 feet until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Destroyer's Grasp
entries: At 7th level, your grip is that of a leviathan's jaws. You gain a bonus to Strength ({@skill Athletics}) checks made to maintain a grapple equal to your Constitution modifier, and your melee attacks count as magical for the purpose of overcoming resistance and immunity to nonmagical attacks and damage.
Additionally, you gain a swim speed equal to your walking speed. If you already have a swim speed, it instead increases by 10 feet.

subclassFeature.name: Unrelenting Assault
entries: At 10th level, you fight like a relentless current. When you hit with a melee weapon attack, you can use your bonus action to attempt to grapple or shove the target. While grappling a creature, the damage of your unarmed strikes and any melee weapon you use against the {@condition grappled} creature increases to {@dice 1d8}, unless it is already higher.
Additionally, when you use your Action Surge feature, you gain 3 momentum.

subclassFeature.name: Flesh Destroyer
entries: Starting at 15th level, the waves of battle flow through you with ease. When you roll initiative, you start with an additional 1 momentum for each hostile creature you can see within 120 feet of you beyond the first, and your maximum becomes equal to your fighter level. Additionally, you gain access to the following Onslaughts:

entries.name: Destroy
entries: If you hit a creature with two melee weapon attacks in the same turn, you can spend any amount of momentum to deal {@dice 1d6} damage per momentum spent beyond the first and add your Strength or Dexterity modifier to the damage.

entries.name: Endless Pursuit
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 6 momentum to deal an additional {@dice 5d6} damage and mark it for inevitable doom. Your movement speed can't be reduced until the end of your next turn unless you're {@condition incapacitated}. At the start of your next turn, you can move up to 120 feet toward the target. You must end this movement within 5 feet of the target if possible.

entries.name: Hammerhead Strike
entries: When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, you can spend 4 momentum to deal an additional {@dice 3d6} damage and push the target up to 30 feet directly away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Rip and Tear
entries: Starting at 18th level, you become a harbinger of death and destruction, tearing the opportunity for survival from your enemies' eyes. When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, if the attack would reduce its hit points to an amount equal to or less than your fighter level + your Strength or Dexterity modifier, the creature is reduced to 0 hit points. If the creature is grappled by you, the threshold is instead two times your fighter level + your Strength or Dexterity modifier.
Additionally, when you kill a creature with a melee weapon attack, you gain 2 momentum.

subclassFeature.name: School of Invention
entries: Your study isn't focused, driven instead by inspiration and whatever you're interested in at the moment. An inventor is biased toward experimentation and field work rather than theory and rote repetition. They wear arcane armor of their own design to serve as protection in their desperate search for another breakthrough, another advancement that will alter the way that everyone thinks about the arcane. They work with a controlled chaos that is well known to them but unpredictable to everyone else. The line between brilliance and insanity is narrow, and you cross that line as you wish.

entries.name: Invention Savant
