entries: Starting at 2nd level, you gain proficiency in two {@filter artisan's tools|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=artisan's tools} of your choice, and you add the {@spell find familiar} spell to your spellbook if it is not there already. When you cast {@spell find familiar}, the familiar can instead be a construct.

entries.name: Arcane Armor
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you've developed a precaution for your work. You gain proficiency with light armor, and can create a set of magical armor, called arcane armor, with a ritual. The ritual can be done during a long rest, and uses 10 gp worth of iron or steel and a set of leather or studded leather armor.
Only you can wear the arcane armor you created, and while you're wearing it, you have immunity against damage from your own spells, unless you choose to take the damage.

The armor provides an AC of 13 + your Dexterity modifier, weighs 10 pounds, and can be donned or doffed as an action. If you create another set of arcane armor, any previous set of arcane armor reverts to non-magical armor.

entries.name: Shift Spells
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can introduce a random variable into your own magic to catch the enemy by surprise. When you cast a spell that deals damage, you can roll on the Shift Spells table and substitute the damage type for the result. If the result is the same as the original damage type, you can add your Intelligence modifier to one damage roll of that spell. You can use this feature twice. You regain expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Idea Improvement
entries: Starting at 6th level, you've developed improvements to your arcane armor, soon enough it could be called a masterpiece. Your arcane armor gains one of the following options.

entries.name: Helm
entries: You can use a bonus action instead of an action to see through the senses of a familiar you summoned with {@spell find familiar}, and you aren't {@condition blinded} or {@condition deafened} during that time.

entries.name: Gloves
entries: When you target a creature within 5 feet of you with a ranged spell attack, you can consider it a melee spell attack instead. You can use this feature only once per turn.

entries.name: Boots
entries: You gain a flying speed of 15 feet, you fall if you end your turn in the air and nothing else is holding you aloft.
Your armor gains another option at 10th and 14th level.

subclassFeature.name: Improved Ingenuity
entries: At 10th level, you're able to adapt to the situation rather than work with what you have. You can change a number of spells on your list of prepared spells equal to your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1) when you finish a short rest.

subclassFeature.name: Peerless Pioneer
entries: At 14th level, you've reached the forefront of the arcane, you will lead the way to the future. When you cast a wizard spell of 1st through 5th level, you can cause that spell to be cast as if cast with a spell slot one level higher. If you do, you cannot cast spells other than cantrips until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, when a creature you can see within 30 feet of you casts a spell, you can cause that spell to be cast as if cast with a spell slot two levels higher as a reaction. You can't use this reaction again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Seer Bloodline
entries: You've inherited both the blood and sight of a being able to see into the future. The being could be an oracle or even a deity, and it's certain your birth has been foretold by them. You can see what destiny has in store for you, but be warned, your destiny is not set in stone. To see the past, present, and future, then decide your own destiny, that is the seer's right.

subclassFeature.name: See the Future
entries: Starting at 1st level, your bloodline allows you to learn spells that allow you to see into the future. When your Spellcasting feature lets you learn a sorcerer spell of 1st level or higher, you can choose a divination spell that isn't on the sorcerer spell list. You must otherwise obey all the restrictions for selecting the spell, and it becomes a sorcerer spell for you.
At 6th level, when you cast a divination spell of 1st level or higher using a spell slot, you regain sorcery points equal to the level of the spell, up to a maximum of 5.

subclassFeature.name: Second Sight
entries: At 1st level, you can see several seconds ahead. While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 10 + your Dexterity modifier + your Charisma modifier.

Additionally, you predict the future, and any who subvert it are punished by the world itself. As a bonus action, choose a hostile creature you can see within 10 feet of you, and say whether or not the creature will target you with an attack or harmful spell during its next turn. The creature understands your prediction, even if it can't hear you or doesn't know your language. At the end of the creature's next turn, if it didn't act as you predicted, it takes force damage equal to equal to half your sorcerer level (rounded up) + your Charisma modifier.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). When you finish a long rest, you regain all expended uses.

subclassFeature.name: Minute Sight
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can see several minutes ahead, you act before you're acted against. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Charisma modifier.
Additionally, you can spend 1 sorcery point to use your Second Sight without expending a use of it.

subclassFeature.name: Hour Sight
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can see several hours ahead, you act before destiny itself. You can cast divination spells with a casting time of 1 to 10 minutes as an action.
Additionally, you can't be surprised while you're conscious.

subclassFeature.name: Change the Future
entries: Starting at 18th level, you can grasp the future you wish for, even if you suffer for it. Whenever you finish a long rest, note the location. If you die, your corpse and any memory of you since that long rest vanish as if you were never there. Any illogical outcomes of your actions since that long rest are rationalized. For example, if you damaged a creature before you died, the creature rationalizes that another creature damaged it instead. You then appear at the location where you finished your last long rest, alive and in the state you were when you finished the rest.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again for 7 days.

subclassFeature.name: Way of the Mad Dog
entries: Those of the Way of the Mad Dog run rampant on the field of battle without restraint or remorse, thirsty for the blood of their enemies. Their mere presence is an omen that those around them will be reduced to gore, they become more feared with each person they murder.

They say when you encounter a Mad Dog, you don't know you're already dead until you've made them bleed. As they lose themselves to their thirst with quaking eyes and a grin that could slice like a knife, your life will flash before your eyes, and you'll regret not writing your will.

entries.name: Mad Dog's Dive
entries: Starting when you choose this tradition at 3rd level, you can tear into the throat of those that think they're safe. Immediately before you take the Attack action on your turn, you can spend 1 ki point to jump 10 feet toward a creature you can see and make a melee weapon attack against the creature as a bonus action. If you hit the creature, it must make a Strength saving throw or be knocked {@condition prone}.
Additionally, the distance you can leap with a long jump isn't halved when you make a standing long jump.

entries.name: Bloodsoaked Beast
entries: At 3rd level, those who spill your blood will regret it. When a creature hits you with an attack while you have all of your hit points, it must make a Wisdom saving throw against your ki save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of its next turn. If a creature's saving throw is successful or the effect ends for it, it is immune to this feature for the next 24 hours.
At 11th level, each hostile creature within 60 feet of you must also make a Wisdom saving throw against your ki save DC or be {@condition frightened} of you until the end of their next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Mad Dog's Dominance
entries: Starting at 6th level, you keep your enemy under your heel. If a creature within your reach stands from {@condition prone}, you can spend 1 ki point as a reaction to make a melee weapon attack against it. If you hit the creature, they're knocked {@condition prone} and their speed is reduced to 0 until the start of their next turn.
Additionally, while you are above half of your hit points, you have advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.

subclassFeature.name: Hellfire Heart
entries: Starting at 11th level, the madness within you causes your body to burn with a fire that can only be quenched by blood. When you take the Attack action on your turn and score a critical hit with a melee weapon attack with the action, you can make one additional weapon attack as part of that action.

subclassFeature.name: Reign
entries: Starting at 17th level, your thirst for blood has become insatiable, those in your path either submit or succumb to you. Using your action, you enter a trance of murderous intent. You gain the following benefits for 10 minutes.

items: You ignore the effects of {@condition exhaustion}, and you cannot gain {@condition exhaustion}.
If you reduce a creature to 0 hit points with a melee weapon attack, you can make another melee weapon attack (no action required by you).
When you hit a creature with a melee weapon attack, it has disadvantage on attack rolls against you until the end of its next turn.

entries: When your trance ends, you suffer one level of {@condition exhaustion}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Reaper
entries: Those who become a Reaper are aware of a certain truth, which is that any creature born will die. Though many people would say this is common sense, those who truly understand their mortality are rare. Whether they had a near death experience or met a god of death, a Reaper stands at the boundary of life and death. They decide when a creature's time has come.

entries.name: Death Glare
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you understand the fragility of life. You know if the hit points of any creature you can see within 30 feet of you are equal to or below your fighter level + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

Additionally, you can extinguish life with a glance. As an action, choose a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. If it has hit points equal to your fighter level + your Wisdom modifier (minimum 1) or fewer, it is reduced to 0 hit points. Whenever you use this action, your eyes glow for an instant.

subclassFeature.name: Decapitate
entries: Starting at 7th level, you grant an end without agony. When you take the Attack action and a melee weapon attack that you make as part of it hits a creature, you can forgo any number of your attacks from it. For each attack that you forgo, you deal extra damage equal to the weapon's damage + the ability modifier used for the attack. If this leaves the target with 0 hit points, you can cut off or destroy one of the target's heads if possible.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (a minimum of once). You regain any expended uses when you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Sudden Death
entries: Starting at 10th level, those you see perish without warning. The range of your Death Glare increases to 60 feet, and you can extinguish life using your Death Glare as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Certain Death
entries: Starting at 15th level, you are as inescapable as death itself. You can use a bonus action to mark a creature you can see within 120 feet of you, and teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of it. On each of your turns while you can see the marked creature, you can teleport in this way again using your bonus action. The mark lasts for 1 minute, and it ends early if your turn ends and you haven't attacked the creature since your last turn. When the marked creature dies, you can apply the mark to a different creature you can see within 120 feet of you, provided you aren't {@condition incapacitated}.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.

subclassFeature.name: The End
entries: Starting at 18th level, you cross the boundary. Having 0 hit points doesn't knock you {@condition unconscious}. You still must make death saving throws, and you suffer the normal effects of taking damage while at 0 hit points. If you die and your body isn't destroyed, such as by the {@spell disintegrate} spell, you can act as if you're alive for a number of your turns equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of 1 turn). You can't act as if you're alive again until you're returned to life and you finish a long rest.
Additionally, whenever you roll damage for a weapon attack while you're dead or have 0 hit points, you can deal maximum damage, instead of rolling.

subclassFeature.name: Servant
entries: There is an endless demand for the skilled, and you're one of them. Regardless of your past, you've decided your countless talents are best used for the sake of someone else. A rogue of this archetype has taken a path of servitude, supporting the person they chose as their master with elegance and grace. Whether you're serving your master tea or murdering their enemies, to do what they're told is the delight of a Servant.

entries.name: Sharply Dressed
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your appearance conceals your lethality. While you are wearing no armor and not wielding a shield, your AC equals 12 + your Dexterity modifier, or 13 + your Dexterity modifier instead if you are wearing fine clothes.

Additionally, you can conceal up to two {@item dagger|phb|daggers} or any objects of a similar size in your clothes. While concealed, ability checks made to find the objects have disadvantage, and both can be drawn with a single object interaction.

entries.name: By Your Side
entries: At 3rd level, you stand at your master's side. As a bonus action, choose a friendly creature within 5 feet of you. Until the start of your next turn, whenever the creature moves or is moved, you move to stay within 5 feet of it. If an attacker you can see hits the creature with an attack, you can use your reaction to cause the attack to hit you instead. The effect ends if you choose to end it (no action required), if the creature moves to a location you can't reach, if you move more than twice your movement speed, or if your speed is reduced to 0.
When you gain the Uncanny Dodge feature, you can use it as part of the same reaction.

subclassFeature.name: Gentle Care
entries: Starting at 9th level, you tend to your master's health. As an action, you can spend one use of a {@item healer's kit|phb} to allow a creature within 5 feet of you to spend one Hit Die. The creature rolls the die, adds its Constitution modifier, and regains a number of hit points equal to the total (minimum of 1). The creature can't regain hit points from this feature again until it finishes a short or long rest.
Starting at 13th level, tending to your master bolsters your spirit. You gain temporary hit points equal to the amount the creature regained.

subclassFeature.name: Prepare Item
entries: Starting at 13th level, you answer your master's whims. If you have a pack or a similar container, you can withdraw any non-magical object worth up to 50 gp that would fit inside, even if you don't have the object. If the object is a {@item dagger|phb} or an object of a similar size, you can instead withdraw the object from your clothes. You must spend an amount of gp equal to the cost of the object to withdraw it. Once you use this feature, you must finish a short or long rest to use it again.

subclassFeature.name: By Your Wish
entries: Starting at 17th level, you grant your master's wishes. After you use your By Your Side feature, the first time the chosen creature makes an attack, you can immediately make a weapon attack against the same target. If this attack benefits from your Sneak Attack, the extra damage from it is halved.

subclassFeature.name: Grim
entries: Many rogues focus their training on the art of death, some enhance their abilities through magic and other such ways. You focus on sheer terror. Through exuding malice, delaying the inevitable death to draw out more fear and despair from your targets, and even teetering on the brink of death only to instill further hopelessness, you invoke a deathly discipline that brings even demons and devils to falter from your gaze.

entries.name: Frightful Ensemble
entries: When you choose this archetype at 3rd level, you gain proficiency with the {@item disguise kit|phb} and the {@skill Intimidation} skill if you don't already have it. You also have a signature piece of your ensemble, such as a mask or a specific appearance to a weapon, which you can craft in 1 hour using a {@item disguise kit|phb}, and can be completed over the course of a short rest.
While wearing this signature piece, you have advantage on Charisma ({@skill Intimidation}) checks.

entries.name: Malicious Stare
entries: Also at 3rd level, you can radiate pure malice and intent to kill against a specific creature. As a bonus action, you can cause one creature you can see within 120 feet of you that can also see you to make a Wisdom saving throw (DC = 8 + your proficiency bonus + your Charisma modifier). On a failed save, the target is {@condition frightened} of you for 1 minute, and you can use your Sneak Attack against the target even if you don't have advantage on the attack roll for the same duration, but not if you have disadvantage on it.
A creature can repeat this saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on itself on a success. A creature that succeeds its saving throw is immune to Malicious Stare for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Prey Sense
entries: At 9th level, you can sense the lambs that you would bring to slaughter. When you roll initiative, you learn the general direction of every hostile creature that can see you within 120 feet of you, even if a creature is hidden or {@condition invisible}.

Additionally, as an action, you can see creatures that have flowing blood within 60 feet of you until the end of your next turn, even if they are {@condition invisible}, on the other side of a wall, or otherwise in total cover. You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of once), and you regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: False Hope
entries: At 13th level, your mastery over terror allows you to come back from the brink of death, bringing despair to your foes where once there was hope. When you would make a death saving throw, you can regain 1 hit point, instead of rolling. If you do so, the next time you use your Malicious Stare within the next minute, the target has disadvantage on the saving throw, and on a failed save you have advantage on all attacks against them until the end of your next turn.
Once you use this feature, you must finish a long rest before you can use it again.

subclassFeature.name: Ultimate Despair
entries: At 17th level, your prey rues the moment it loses sight of you. While you're hidden from a creature you can see within 120 feet of you, you can use your action to move up to 120 feet towards the target and make a melee weapon attack. On a hit, if the attack benefits from your Sneak Attack, you deal an additional {@dice 2d6} damage.

If the target is under the effects of your Malicious Stare, the target must also succeed on a Wisdom saving throw with a DC equal to your Malicious Stare DC or become {@condition paralyzed} until the end of your next turn.

Additionally, when you bring a creature to the brink of death, it is plagued by nightmares of you. If you reduce a creature affected by your Malicious Stare to 0 hit points, it can't benefit from short or long rests for 7 days.

subclassFeature.name: The Shadow
entries: Your patron is a being that is likely viewed in a light of villainy; a Wraith, a Shadow Demon, a particularly potent Shadow, a Nightwalker, or even an embodiment or deity of Darkness itself. Regardless of their point of existence, your pact with that being is one that leaves you with a feeling of fulfilment. You might have ade it as a small child who sought nothing more than a guide through the scary darkness. Or perhaps you made it to have a reliable assistant through your endeavors. You might have even made the pact to have a stalwart companion who would never betray you, or maybe you just wanted to not be alone any more. But the same gnawing question eventually arises within your mind: Why did your patron show you what seems to be kindness? Are they truly benevolent? Have they nefarious goals, as most do? The question of why a kindness was done unto you is one all who make a pact with a patron of shadow ask themselves, but even through the darkest nights and the most insatiable of curiosities, the bestowed shadow stands at your side.

entries.name: Expanded Spell List
entries: The Shadow lets you choose from an expanded list of spells when you learn a warlock spell. The following spells are added to the warlock spell list for you.

subclassFeature.name: Tenebrous Companion
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can manifest a blessing from your patron at the end of a short or long rest. This shadowy creature is Small or Medium - your choice when you summon it - and has a shape and appearance of your choice, but if it resembles an existing creature, they are clearly distinguishable from each other. It is friendly to you and your companions, and regards you like a trusted friend to be heeded and protected.

The bestowed shadow is sentient, but its personality and alignment closely match your own, unless your DM decides otherwise. For example, many shadows copy the personality of their warlock when the pact is created, but develop individually of the warlock after.

In combat, the bestowed shadow shares your initiative count, but takes its turn immediately after yours. You can command it on its turn (no action required). If you don't, it will follow its judgement on what would be in your best interests, or what you would desire. Additionally, you can cast spells as though you are in the shadow's space. See this creature's statistics in the {@creature bestowed shadow|TheDarkAscension} stat block.

The bestowed shadow cannot benefit from a short or long rest if you do not also benefit from it, and when it does, it regains all lost hit points. If the bestowed shadow dies, it becomes a part of your shadow until you manifest it again at the end of a short or long rest. When it is manifested, it reappears with the memories it had when it was last with you.

subclassFeature.name: Enter the Shadow
entries: At 6th level, your shadows protect you, and you have grown to seek comfort in them. As a reaction when an attack hits or misses you, you can teleport to a location within dim light or darkness that you can see within 120 feet of you. Once you do so, you cannot do so again until you finish a short or long rest.
Additionally, your bestowed shadow gains a swim speed equal to ten times your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Light Creates Shadow
entries: At 10th level, you embrace light, for without it your companion cannot exist. You have resistance to radiant damage, and when you take radiant damage, you and your bestowed shadow gain temporary hit points equal to the hit points you lost. When your shadow hits with an attack, it can lose any number of these temporary hit points to deal the same amount as additional necrotic or radiant damage to the target.

subclassFeature.name: Spies from the Shadows
entries: At 14th level, you create many clones from shadows. As an action, you can summon a number of additional shadows equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of one). The additional shadows act on your bestowed shadow's initiative and share its game statistics, except each shadow only has 1 hit point, and don't benefit from your eldritch invocations. These shadows remain for 10 minutes, or until destroyed, and for the duration, you and each of your shadows share a telepathic link.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
Additionally, your bestowed shadow gains a fly speed equal to ten times your Charisma modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Divinity Domain
entries: While a true cleric is a rare enough sight already, a cleric of the Divinity domain might be seen once in a lifetime at most. A cleric of this domain isn't a cleric in the strictest sense, but a godly being able to manifest miracles with their own power, rather than the power of another god. Whether they have the blood of a deity within them or are the incarnation of a deity, they'll become a deity themselves eventually. Until that time has come, they have the freedom to decide their own destiny. That freedom is a blessing granted only to a mortal, not a god.

entries.caption: Divinity Domain Spells
rows: {@spell aura of purity}, {@spell guardian of faith}

subclassFeature.name: Light of Judgement
entries: At 1st level, you judge as a god would. You can use your action to launch a beam of light at a creature that you can see within 60 feet of you. The target takes radiant damage equal to 1 + your Wisdom modifier, and until the end of your next turn, you can use this feature against the target even if you can't see it. The beam of light ignores cover, but fails if the target isn't within range. You can use this feature only once per turn.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: White Plume
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to unleash your divine power. As an action, you can cause two angelic wings to sprout from your back for 1 minute or until you lose your concentration (as if you were concentrating on a spell). Any clothing or armor you're wearing are magically altered for the duration to accommodate the angelic wings.
While you have these wings, you gain a flying speed of 30 feet, and you can use Light of Judgement as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Paradise Lost
entries: Starting at 6th level, you rise toward godhood. When you use White Plume, you can spend additional uses of your Channel Divinity. You gain two more wings for each additional use you spend. You also gain two more wings if you use White Plume while you have wings from it. If you have more than two wings from White Plume, it doesn't require concentration.
While you have four wings from White Plume, your flying speed increases to 60 feet, and the damage of Light of Judgement increases by half your cleric level.
While you have six wings from White Plume, your flying speed increases to 120 feet, and the damage of Light of Judgement increases by your cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Endless Light
entries: At 17th level, you're able to wield the power of a god. You can target up to six creatures with Light of Judgement, and the damage increases to 2 + twice your Wisdom modifier.
In addition, when you successfully use Divine Intervention, you can cast any spell instantaneously without expending a spell slot and without components instead of an intervention.

subclassFeature.name: Chance Domain
entries: The Chance domain focuses on the balance between risk and reward. Unlike the Luck domain, the Chance domain doesn't alter their fate, willing to wager their life for a single gamble. The gods of luck, such as Tymora and Vergadain, are also the gods of chance. They will intervene for a cleric of the Chance domain only if they must, watching them is more interesting.

subclassFeature.name: Game of Chance
entries: At 1st level, you become proficient in two {@filter gaming sets|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set} of your choice. Your proficiency bonus is doubled for any ability check you make that uses either of those {@filter gaming sets|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set}.
If you're proficient with a {@filter gaming set|items|source=phb|miscellaneous=mundane|type=gaming set}, you can use it as a spellcasting focus for your cleric spells.

subclassFeature.name: Risk and Reward
entries: At 1st level, you gamble in combat. When you make an attack, you can make it with advantage, but the next attack roll made against you before the start of your next turn has advantage.

Alternatively, when a willing creature you can see within 10 feet of you makes an attack, you can cause it to be made with advantage. The next attack made against that creature before the start of its next turn has advantage. You can't use this feature if the creature already has advantage on the attack.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the start of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Gamble Life
entries: At 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to wager your life for another, the loser having their life stolen from them.

As a bonus action, choose a creature you can see within 10 feet of you, and choose a number up to your cleric level. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes necrotic damage equal to three times the chosen number. On a successful save, you take necrotic damage equal to the chosen number.

subclassFeature.name: Perfect Play
entries: Starting at 6th level, your skill isn't affected by circumstance. When you make an ability check with a skill or tool you're proficient with, you can ignore disadvantage. If you do so, you can't gain advantage on the check.

subclassFeature.name: Potent Spellcasting
entries: Starting at 8th level, you add your Wisdom modifier to the damage you deal with any cleric cantrip.

subclassFeature.name: Life or Death
entries: Starting at 17th level, you've lost any fear for your own life. When you use your Gamble Life, you can choose a number up to your cleric level + 10.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Branch Reader
entries: Within the world, there are endless possibilities. Among the durumir, famed for their intellect and cosmic connection, there are a gifted few that are able to perceive and act on these possibilities. These are called branches by the durumir, and those who percieve them are known as branch readers. Rather than be overcome by emotion, they enter a state of ocus and choose one path to take from countless choices.

entries.name: Restriction: Durumir Only
entries: Only durumir can follow the Path of the Branch Reader. To be a branch reader requires a particular sensitivity, and is an honored role rarely bestowed on those of another race. Your DM can lift this restriction to better suit the campaign. It might not apply to your DM's setting.

entries.name: Branching Path
entries: Starting at 3rd level, you can read the branches of the great tree. You can forgo resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage when you enter a rage. If you do so, you gain the following benefits for the duration of the rage.

items: Attack rolls made against you have disadvantage.
You have advantage on Intelligence checks and Intelligence saving throws.

When you or a creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you are hit by an attack, you can use your reaction to reduce the damage by half your barbarian level + your Intelligence modifier (minimum 1).
Your rage doesn't end early as a result of not attacking a hostile creature or taking damage since your last turn.

entries.name: Chosen Path
entries: At 3rd level, you decide on your path without any hesitation. You can give yourself a bonus to your initiative rolls equal to your Intelligence modifier.

subclassFeature.name: Break the Branch
entries: Starting at 6th level, you can forcefully break a branch that would lead to catastrophe. When you or a creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you is hit by an attack while you're under the effect of Branching Path, you can cause your rage to end early and reduce the damage to 0. You can't use this feature again for 1 minute.

subclassFeature.name: Prepare the Path
entries: Starting at 10th level, you prepare the path for everyone else. Immediately before the start of your turn, you can exchange the initiative count of you and a willing creature that can hear you within 60 feet of you. The target acts immediately, and you act on your new initative count.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until the end of your next turn.

subclassFeature.name: Knowledge of the Great Tree
entries: Starting at 14th level, you can reach a state of enlightenment, able to read the branches while you're overcome by emotion. When you enter a rage, you can benefit from Branching Path without forgoing resistance.
Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Circle of the Legion
entries: There is a layer of the Abyss within which nature is constantly absorbing itself, in order to grow and perpetuate. The everconsuming force is alluring to some, and encapsulating to others. They become so in tune with the feeling and need for perpetual consumption that they extend it to the nature of the Material Plane, and even to the monsters they summon, eventually able to harness the power of their souls and expel them with great force.

entries.name: Circle Spells
entries: Your understanding of the energies of the Abyss grants you the eternal knowledge to cast spells sometimes held beyond your grasp. At 2nd level, you learn the {@spell primal savagery|XGE} cantrip. At 3rd, 5th, 7th, and 9th level you gain access to the circle spells listed below.

Once you gain access to a circle spell, you always have it prepared, and it doesn't count against the number of spells you can prepare each day. If you gain access to a spell that doesn't appear on the druid spell list, the spell is nonetheless a druid spell for you.
Lastly, demons summoned from a circle spell aren't hostile to you and your allies and obey your orders, and when you cast {@spell summon lesser demons|XGE}, you can choose what is summoned.

entries.name: Abyssal Wilds
entries: Starting at 2nd level, the chaotic energies of the abyss flow through your veins, and manifest at your will when you transform. When you expend a use of wild shape to take a new form, you gain temporary hit points equal to your druid level + your Wisdom modifier, and can change the damage type of the new form's attacks to cold or fire for the duration, chosen when you transform. While transformed in this way, you can appear as a demonic mockery of your chosen form bristling with the element you chose, changing your type to fiend for the duration of your wild shape.
Additionally, when you expend a use of wild shape to take a new form, you can cast your {@spell primal savagery|XGE} cantrip even if you normally couldn't cast it in that form.

subclassFeature.name: Consume
entries: At 6th level, your knowledge expands to fiendish depths, and you can consume demons you summon to overhaul your capabilities. When you summon one or more demons with a spell, you can expend a use of wild shape to merge with one as part of the action used to cast the spell. If you do, you must sacrifice that creature as you cast the spell, reducing it to 0 hit points. For the duration of the spell, you gain the ability scores, armor class, saving throw proficiencies, languages, resistances, immunities, movement speeds, traits, actions, and reactions that the creature you summon would have, unless your own would be better. Additionally, you gain temporary hit points equal to the creature's hit point maximum, and you can choose to change your size to match the creature's own. While morphed in this way, you follow the wild shape restrictions for spellcasting, and your damage is considered magical for the purpose of overcoming immunity and resistance to nonmagical attacks and damage.
If you are under the effects of Abyssal Wilds, you can cast a spell to activate Consume even if you normally couldn't cast it in that form.

subclassFeature.name: Fiendish Regeneration
entries: At 10th level, you can destroy a monster you've conjured to fuel yourself. You can slay a creature you have summoned or created as an action to regain a number of hit points equal to the creature's current hit points. You cannot exceed half your hit point maximum from healing in this way.

subclassFeature.name: Heart of Chaos
entries: At 14th level, the Abyss has buried deep into your heart, enabling even greater capabilities. When you cast a spell to activate Consume, it doesn't require concentration, and you can use one of the creature's attacks as a bonus action on the same turn you cast the spell.

Additionally, you can ignore the wild shape restrictions for spellcasting while under the effects of Consume to cast spells from a merged demon's Innate Spellcasting trait, if it has one. You cast them using your spell slots, and its spells are considered druid spells for you.
You can also cast one spell from its list without expending a spell slot while merged. Once you cast a spell in this way, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Ysgardian Bloodline
entries: The forefathers were known for their brutality and inspiring strength on the battlefield, and when they fell, their spirits ascended to the halls of Ysgard. However, their spirits did not go without a fight, and each of them left behind a portion of their essence for their descendants to one day tap into, to protect them, and to perhaps one day fight with them. Perhaps your ancestor was a powerful viking, or perhaps this essence was passed onto you from an ended bloodline by the Allfather, or another being; maybe even the spirit of a dead viking themselves bestowed this unto you. But any sorcerer, be it by birthright or by shouldering the burden of another lineage that met its demise, can tap into the powers of Ysgard.

subclassFeature.name: Bonus Proficiencies
entries: You gain proficiency with simple melee weapons, martial melee weapons, and light armor.

subclassFeature.name: Spirits of the Halls
entries: Starting at 1st level, you can call forth the spirits of your ancestors to repel your foes, or to aid you in dire straits. As a bonus action, you can summon two Ysgardian spirits, which hover in your space, to fight alongside you. These spirits last for 1 minute, or until you use this feature again. Your carrying capacity is multiplied by an amount equal to 1 + the number of spirits you currently have. You can command the spirits to act in the following ways:

items: When you deal damage to a creature, you can command one or more spirits to push that creature 15 feet horizontally in a direction of your choice, plus an extra 15 feet for every spirit you command past the first.
Whenever you make a saving throw, you can use your reaction to command one or more spirits to aid you. You can roll {@dice 1d4} per spirit you command and add it to the result of the saving throw.

entries: After a spirit acts, it disappears. You can use this feature three times, and regain all expended uses on a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Fury of Ysgard
entries: At 6th level, the Ysgardian spirits you summon brandish weapons with which to strike your foes, their legacies fighting through you. When you use your Spirits of the Halls feature, you gain an additional command option:

items: As a bonus action, you can command one spirit to strike a creature you can see within 30 feet of you. The spirit uses your spell attack modifier for attack rolls and deals force damage equal to {@dice 1d12} + your Charisma modifier on a hit. Alternatively, you can use an action to command any number of spirits remaining to attack, rolling separately for each spirit.

entries: Additionally, you can now use your Spirits of the Halls feature by spending up to 3 sorcery points to summon the same number of spirits.

subclassFeature.name: Ysgardian Ancestry
entries: At 14th level, when you would be reduced to 0 hit points, you can call upon the immortality of your ancestors. You are reduced to 1 hit point instead, and your hit points can't fall below 1 until after the start of your next turn. Once you use this feature, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.

Additionally, you know the {@spell plane shift} spell, it doesn't count against your spells known, and you don't need a tuning fork to travel to Ysgard. Traveling to Ysgard makes you arrive at the gates of Valhalla.

subclassFeature.name: Immortal Legacy
entries: At 18th level, your glory becomes eternal, and the legacy of your ancestors has become immortalized. Memories of you can never be erased from a creature's mind, and you are always a valid target for the {@spell legend lore} spell.
Ysgardian spirits no longer disappear when you summon more. Instead, you can have up to five Ysgardian spirits active at once, and they last until you finish a long rest.
Additionally, you gain five Ysgardian spirits whenever you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Royalty Domain
entries: You serve a deity of royalty, often rulers of pantheons such as Odin, Zeus, or Ba'al, who supports sovereignty by divine right to rule. You are an emissary of this belief; often dealing with the issues and strife caused between nobility and the common folk, ensuring the lessers know their place, and also preventing corruption and abuse by those above them.

subclassFeature.name: Monarch's Presence
entries: At 1st level, you gain proficiency with heavy armor and the {@skill Performance} skill.
Additionally, you can use your bonus action to summon a spiritual symbol of your station, such as a crown, emblem, or ring, which remains until you dismiss it or summon it again.

subclassFeature.name: Royal Decree
entries: Also at 1st level, you can emit a royal presence causing others to falter. As an action, you cause other creatures within 10 feet of you, except those you choose, to have disadvantage on attack rolls until the end of your next turn.
As a reaction when a creature under this effect misses you with an attack, you can make an attack against it.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to your Wisdom modifier (minimum of once). You regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

subclassFeature.name: Channel Divinity: Crown's Guard
entries: Starting at 2nd level, you can use your Channel Divinity to call upon your deity for protection befitting the crown.
As a reaction to taking damage, you can present your holy symbol and use your Channel Divinity to reduce the damage you take by {@dice 1d10} + your Wisdom modifier + your cleric level.

subclassFeature.name: Imposing Exile
entries: At 6th level, you can expel transgressors, causing them to stagger backwards from your brilliance. When you damage a creature with a weapon attack or cleric cantrip, you can push the creature up to 10 feet directly away from you.

subclassFeature.name: Divine Strike
entries: Starting at 8th level, you gain the ability to infuse your weapon strikes with your force of will. Once on each of your turns when you hit a creature with a weapon attack, you can cause the attack to deal an extra {@dice 1d8} force damage to the target. When you reach 14th level, the extra damage increases to {@dice 2d8}.

subclassFeature.name: Sovereign Immunity
entries: At 17th level, none can harm you, for all are undeserving to strike you. You are always under the effect of the {@spell sanctuary} spell, using your spell save DC. If a creature succeeds a saving throw against it, it's immune to this effect for 24 hours.

subclassFeature.name: Path of the Avenger
entries: While many barbarians channel their rage from a well of anger sculpted by focus, experience, or pushing themselves beyond their limits, those of the Path of the Avenger channel their rage from a deep sense of regret, and a desire for atonement for a mistake they cannot undo. Extremely vengeful and known to traverse entire planes of existence to slay those who have wronged their allies, they are ruthless, protective, and to those foolish enough to cross them, a nightmare made manifest.

entries.name: Avenger's Fury
entries: Starting at 3rd level, your fury brings forth memories of the fallen, and the weapons they wielded. While you're raging, an ethereal retributive weapon floats above your head, which you are considered proficient with, and it takes an appearance of your choosing. When a friendly creature is hit by an attack from a creature you can see within 30 feet of you, you can use your reaction to have the weapon appear adjacent to the attacker, striking it. Make a melee weapon attack using your Strength or Dexterity modifier against the target. On a hit, the target takes force damage equal to {@dice 1d10} + your rage damage bonus, and the retributive weapon returns to you.
Additionally, while raging you are aware of the location of any friendly creature within 120 feet of you that is dying or at 0 hit points, even if it is {@condition invisible}.

entries.name: A Lesson Learned
entries: Also at 3rd level, you have learned from your mistakes. You automatically succeed Wisdom ({@skill Medicine}) checks made to stabilize a dying creature, and you can stabilize a creature as a bonus action.

subclassFeature.name: Inhuman Rampage
entries: Starting at 6th level, the anger that flows through your veins surpasses mortal understanding. While raging, if an ally within 30 feet of you takes damage from a hostile creature, you can suppress all conditions affecting you until the end of your next turn. You can use this ability only once per rage.

subclassFeature.name: Avenger's Eye
entries: Starting at 10th level, those soaked in the blood of your allies will never escape your wrath. When a hostile creature damages any creature friendly to you, you are aware of its location if it is within 30 feet of you, even if it is hidden or {@condition invisible}, until the end of your next turn.

You can also track those that have fled from their crime. You can spend 1 minute concentrating on a wound a creature friendly to you has been dealt. Until your next long rest, you know how far the creature that dealt the wound is and in what direction it lies. Whenever you are presented with a choice of paths along the way, you automatically determine which path is the shortest and most direct (but not necessarily the safest) route to the creature.

subclassFeature.name: Unrelenting Vengeance
entries: Starting at 14th level, your retribution is unmatched. The damage dice of your retributive weapon increases to {@dice 2d10} + your rage damage bonus.

Additionally, when an ally is reduced to 0 hit points by a hostile creature while you're raging, your next attack against that creature before the end of your next turn automatically hits and is a critical hit. You can gain this benefit only once per rage.

entries.name: Will of the Dragonbane
entries: You have advantage on saving throws against being {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Wrath of the Serpent
entries: When you hit an attack against a creature, you can reduce the target's flying speed to 0 until the end of its next turn. You can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Blessing of the Tree
entries: You can add your Constitution modifier to death saving throws.

entries.name: Mighty Legs
entries: When you fall and aren't {@condition incapacitated}, you can subtract up to 50 feet from the fall when calculating falling damage.

entries.name: Delay the Inveitable
entries: When hit by a weapon attack that isn't a critical hit, you can use your reaction to have the attack miss instead. The creature that attacked you can't miss with its next attack against you within the next minute. You can't do this again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Thine Prophecy Foretold
entries: If you or an ally within 60 feet of you is surprised, you can choose one ally. They can't be surprised this round. You can't do this again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Breath of the Wild
entries: You can spend 10 minutes with your eyes closed in meditation, at the end of which you gain blindsight out to 60 feet, are {@condition incapacitated}, and cannot move. If you are in a forest, you gain blindsight out to 120 feet instead and also become {@condition invisible}. You can end these effects at will.

entries.name: Fleet of Hoof
entries: Your walking speed increases to 35 feet.

entries.name: Close to Nature
entries: Pick one {@filter cantrip of your choice from the druid spell list|spells|level=0|class=Druid}. Intelligence is your spellcasting ability for it.

entries.name: Epitome of Grace
entries: Your broad wingspan means that you are always under the effects of a {@spell feather fall} spell.

entries.name: Soothing Appearance
entries: You have advantage on Wisdom ({@skill Animal Handling}) checks to calm hostile or agitated animals.

entries.name: A Hunter's Upbringing
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Perception} and {@skill Survival} skills.

entries.name: Hardened Carapace
entries: When you aren't wearing armor, your AC is 14 + your Dexterity modifier (to a maximum of 2). You can use your natural armor to determine your AC if the armor you wear would leave you with a lower AC. A shield's benefits apply as normal while you use your natural armor.

entries.name: Extra Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write two extra languages of your choice.

entries.name: High Class
entries: You can double your proficiency bonus for Charisma checks when speaking with noble or high-class creatures.

entries.name: Well-Mannered
entries: Whenever you make an ability check with a Charisma-based skill you are proficient in, you can treat a {@dice d20} roll of 4 or lower as a 5.

entries.name: River Hunter
entries: You have a swimming speed equal to your walking speed. If you swim at least 10 feet and reach the surface of water you're in, you can breach 5 feet above the surface of the water and begin flying.

entries.name: Voice Like Raindrops
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill. Additionally, your singing is perfectly audible to creatures you choose even when you or a listening creature is in light or heavy rain.

entries.name: Melody: Respite's Embrace
entries: As part of a short or long rest, you can hum a melody, which continues even as you sleep. Choose your targets within range, which can include yourself. Only the targets you chose can hear this magical humming, and such creatures are immune to any effects that would prevent them from benefiting from a short or long rest while they can hear it.

Additionally, you can sing a melody of reassurance. Choose your targets amongst friendly creatures within range, which can include yourself. For 1 minute or until the song ends, whichever occurs first, the targets gain the effects of the {@spell heroism} spell, using your Charisma as the spellcasting ability modifier.
Once you use this melody, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Luring Song: Lullaby
entries: Starting at 3rd level, while a creature is {@condition charmed} by your Luring Song, you can use an action to try and focus the song on it, which counts as continuing to sing. The creature repeats the saving throw, and has disadvantage if it is within 5 feet of you. On a failure, the song ends for all other creatures, but the target you chose falls {@condition unconscious} until the song ends, or until it takes damage. On a success, the song continues as normal, but the creature can't be affected by Lullaby again for the next 24 hours. The creature automatically succeeds the saving throw to overcome your Luring Song if it is woken up by damage.

entries.name: Thunder Acclimated
entries: You have resistance to thunder damage, as well as advantage on saving throws made to avoid being deafened. Additionally, your singing is perfectly audible to creatures you choose even through thunder.

entries.name: Melody: Battle Cry
entries: You can echo a thunderous battle cry that inspires targets you choose within range, which can include yourself. For 1 minute or until the song ends, the targets deal an additional {@dice 1d4} thunder damage on weapon attacks. Additionally, each target gains a {@dice 1d4} bonus to attack rolls until it hits. It regains this bonus when you continue singing on your turn.
Once you use this melody, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Luring Song: Blood Lust
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you continue your Luring Song, you can inspire blood lust in one creature {@condition charmed} by it. The creature repeats the saving throw, and has disadvantage if it is within 5 feet of you. On a failure, the creature immediately makes one melee attack against a random creature within reach other than you. If no other creatures are within reach, it makes a ranged attack against a random creature other than you within range, throwing its weapon if necessary. On a success, the song continues as normal, but the creature can't be affected by Blood Lust again for the next 24 hours.

entries.name: One With Wind
entries: Your base walking speed increases to 35 feet. Additionally, your singing is perfectly audible to creatures you choose even when you or a listening creature is in an area of light or strong wind..

entries.name: Peak Dweller
entries: You're acclimated to high altitude, including elevations above 20,000 feet. You're also naturally adapted to cold climates, as described in chapter 5 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.

entries.name: Melody: Whistling Winds
entries: You can begin whistling a light tune that makes targets you choose within range fleet of foot, which can include yourself. For 1 minute or until the song ends, the targets' walking speed increases by 10 feet, ignoring the effects of difficult and hazardous terrain as they step across little platforms of air that form beneath their feet, and opportunity attacks against them are made with disadvantage.
Once you use this melody, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Luring Song: Wind Tunnel
entries: Starting at 3rd level, when you continue your Luring Song, you can reshape the winds to your will around one creature affected by the song. Until the start of your next turn, you can move 2 feet for every 1 foot of movement you expend as long as you move in a straight line toward that creature, and you have advantage on your next melee attack against it. Additionally, if you move at least 20 feet toward the creature and hit it during this turn, it must make a Strength saving throw or be pushed up to 30 feet away from you.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals acid damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to acid damage.

entries.name: Brutal and Cruel
entries: You add your Constitution modifier to the damage of your breath weapon.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals lightning damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Desert Predator
entries: As an action, you can burrow yourself into sand or other loose soil. Creatures that attempt to detect you take a -5 penalty to their Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks as long as you remain there without moving or taking actions.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Boldly Talkative
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals lightning damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to lightning damage.

entries.name: Dragon of the Coast
entries: You have a swim speed of 30 feet.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals acid damage in a line 30 feet long and 5 feet wide. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to acid damage.

entries.name: Good Host
entries: You have proficiency in {@skill Performance}, and can speak, read, and write one extra language of your choice.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Reserved Companion
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Insight} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals poison damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to poison damage.

entries.name: Manipulative Schemer
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Deception} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals fire damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Dexterity saving throw. You have resistance to fire damage.

entries.name: Arrogant Tyrant
entries: You have advantage on saving throws to avoid being {@condition charmed} or {@condition frightened}.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals cold damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Hoarding History
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill History} skill.

entries.name: Draconic Ancestry
entries: Your breath weapon deals cold damage in a 15-foot cone. Your breath weapon imposes a Constitution saving throw. You have resistance to cold damage.

entries.name: Primal and Vengeful
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Survival} skill.

entries.name: Survival Instincts
entries: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Perception}, {@skill Stealth}, or {@skill Survival}. You can add half your proficiency bonus to checks made with the skills you didn't choose, unless you would already add your proficiency bonus to them.

entries.name: Corruption of the Abyss
entries: When you deal damage to a creature with an attack or spell, you can infuse the wound with abyssal corruption. The creature must succeed on a Charisma saving throw (DC = 8 + your Charisma modifier + your proficiency bonus) or become corrupted, gaining disadvantage on attack rolls for 1 minute. The target can repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns, ending the effect on a success. If a corrupted creature rolls an attack roll, ability check, or saving throw, and the roll on the {@dice d20} is equal to your proficiency bonus or lower, it takes psychic damage equal to {@dice 1d8} + your Charisma modifier, as it hears voices mocking them.
You can use this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Abyssal Arcana
entries: Spellcasting is inherent within you, and as you grow in strength, you unlock more latent talent. At 1st level, 3rd level, and 5th level, you can choose a spell from the respective column of the Abyssal Arcana Spells table. Each row is tied to a demon prince, but you don't have to pick a spell from the same row as when last you gained a spell from this trait.

You can cast a spell gained from this trait only once until you complete your next long rest, unless it is a cantrip, which you can cast at will, as normal. For 1st-level spells whose effect changes if cast using a spell slot of 2nd-level or higher, you cast the spell as if using a 2nd-level slot. Spells of 2nd-level are cast as if using a 2nd-level slot.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells. You can change the spells you gained from this feature whenever you gain the Ability Score Improvement feature from your class.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Abyssal.

entries.name: Mercenary Necessities
entries: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: {@skill Acrobatics}, or {@skill Athletics}. You also have proficiency with light armor and one martial weapon of your choice.

entries.name: Brutal Efficiency
entries: After you make a melee attack, you can teleport up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space that you can see.

You can teleport using this trait a number of times equal to your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest. If you reduced a creature to 0 hit points with the melee attack, you can teleport without expending a use, even if there are no uses remaining.

entries.name: Gehennal Heritage
entries: Spellcasting is inherent within you, and as you grow in strength, you unlock more latent talent. At 1st level, 3rd level, and 5th level, you can choose a spell from the respective column of the Gehennal Heritage Spells table. Each row is tied to a deity that formerly had a domain on Gehenna, but you don't have to pick a spell from the same row as when last you gained a spell from this trait.

You can cast a spell gained from this trait only once until you complete your next long rest, unless it is a cantrip, which you can cast at will, as normal. For 1st-level spells whose effect changes if cast using a spell slot of 2nd-level or higher, you cast the spell as if using a 2nd-level slot. Spells of 2nd-level are cast as if using a 2nd-level slot.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells. You can change the spells you gained from this feature whenever you gain the Ability Score Improvement feature from your class.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common, and your choice of Abyssal or Infernal.

entries.name: Silver Tongue
entries: You have proficiency in one of the following skills of your choice: Deception, Intimidation, or Persuasion. You can add half your proficiency bonus to checks made with the skills you didn't choose, unless you would already add your proficiency bonus to them.

entries.name: Luck of the Devil
entries: When you roll a {@dice d20} without disadvantage and the roll is equal to or less than your proficiency bonus, you can choose to reroll it and take any of the rolls.
You can use this feature a number of times equal to 1 + your proficiency bonus, and regain all expended uses when you finish a long rest.

entries.name: Infernal Legacy
entries: Spellcasting is inherent within you, and as you grow in strength, you unlock more latent talent. At 1st level, 3rd level, and 5th level, you can choose a spell from the respective column of the Infernal Legacy Spells table. Each row is tied to an archdevil, but you don't have to pick a spell from the same row as when last you gained a spell from this trait.

You can cast a spell gained from this trait only once until you complete your next long rest, unless it is a cantrip, which you can cast at will, as normal. For 1st-level spells whose effect changes if cast using a spell slot of 2nd-level or higher, you cast the spell as if using a 2nd-level slot. Spells of 2nd-level are cast as if using a 2nd-level slot.
Charisma is your spellcasting ability for these spells. You can change the spells you gained from this feature whenever you gain the Ability Score Improvement feature from your class.

entries.name: Languages
entries: You can speak, read, and write Common and Infernal.

entries.name: Inherited Athleticism
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Athletics} skill.

entries.name: Drop-Kick
entries: When a creature is in the path of your jump, you can attempt to shove them {@condition prone} as part of the movement, dropkicking them in the process. Regardless of the outcome, you end your jump in the closest unoccupied space within 5 feet of the target. Once you use this ability, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Inherited Eloquence
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Persuasion} skill.

entries.name: Consultation
entries: When you would make an ability check that requires Intelligence or Wisdom, you can gain a bonus to the check equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum 1). Once you do so, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Lunar Guidance
entries: You cannot have disadvantage on Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks based on sight.

entries.name: Moonlight Walker
entries: You have proficiency in the {@skill Stealth} skill. Additionally, you can take the Hide action as a bonus action at night, and while the moon is full and the sky is visible, you don't need to be obscured to hide.

entries.name: Ethereality
entries: As a bonus action, you can vanish from your current plane of existence and appear in the Ethereal Plane until the start of your next turn, as per the {@spell blink} spell but with a duration of 1 round. Once you use this trait, you can't do so again until you finish a short or long rest.

entries.name: Star's Sight
entries: You have advantage on saving throws made to resist being {@condition blinded}.

entries.name: Unending Breath
entries: You can hold your breath indefinitely while you're not {@condition incapacitated}.

entries.name: Healing Energies
entries: As a bonus action, you can touch a creature and cause it to regain a number of hit points equal to your Charisma modifier. Once you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
At 3rd level, you can instead cast the {@spell lesser restoration} spell using this feature.

entries.name: Solar Channelling
entries: You know the {@spell light} cantrip, and can choose to make the duration until dispelled. If you do so, the spell ends when you cast it again. Charisma is your spellcasting ability for it. Additionally, when you create light through a spell you cast, you can choose to make the light as sunlight.
